source.subject: Expansion stitch checkpoint: usage growth & add-on timeline
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Michael Lee <michael.lee@helico-tech.com>, Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@helico-tech.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Expansion stitch checkpoint: usage growth & add-on timeline

Hi Michael / Sarah —

Quick weekly check-in on the MAP stitch we discussed last month. High-level: usage is trending above the committed baseline, the new analytics team is slated to onboard in March, and procurement asked for a single add-on SKU to be provisioned for two test teams.

Summary (current):
- Daily tokens: 1.8M avg (up 34% vs baseline)
- Active seats: 48 (analytics pilot + product)
- Add-on requested: Realtime Inference (+priority burst) for 2 teams

Next milestones & timeline:
- Pricing addendum sent to procurement (action: Mateo / Mar 1) — attached stub here: MAP_Stitch_20270220.xlsx
- Provisioning window (post-signature): 48 business hours for Realtime add-on; Diego to confirm exact schedule
- Onboarding session for analytics team: target week of Mar 8 (owner: Monica)

Blockers / decisions needed:
- Procurement needs a single-line pricing addendum with a 6-month pilot price and the volume banding.
- Customer asks whether we can shadow existing latency SLOs for the pilot; Diego, can you confirm compatibility with their current model sizes?

Action items (owners):
1) Mateo — deliver finalized addendum to Procurement by Mon, Mar 1 (MAP_Stitch_20270220.xlsx attached)
2) Diego — confirm provisioning lead time & SLO compatibility by Mon, Feb 22
3) Monica — schedule analytics onboarding (options: Mar 8 or Mar 10) and share invite by Tue, Feb 23
4) Marissa — prepare short runbook for the two test teams (deployment checklist) by Wed, Feb 24

Proposed next checkpoint: Wed, Mar 3, 10:00 PT (30m). Let me know if that works for your calendar.

Thanks —
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0191

Attachments: MAP_Stitch_20270220.xlsx (spreadsheet - MAP milestones and pricing stub)

From: Michael Lee <michael.lee@helico-tech.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@helico-tech.com>, Procurement <procurement@helico-tech.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 16:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Expansion stitch checkpoint: usage growth & add-on timeline

Thanks Mateo — got this and the spreadsheet. A few quick clarifications before we pull Procurement in:

1) The 48-hour provisioning sounds fine, but can you confirm whether the "Realtime Inference + priority burst" SKU requires a different region or instance class than our current Dedicated pool? We want to avoid cross-region latency.

2) For pricing: the finance team will want to see the pilot term explicitly (6 months) and a renewal rate if we hit the >2x usage band. Can you put both numbers on the addendum?

3) If we escalate provisioning to go-live earlier than Mar 8, is there a one-time provisioning fee?

Looping in Procurement to triage the addendum. Procurement — please confirm the legal contact for DocuSign and any PO requirements.

-Michael
VP, Data Platform
Helico Tech
michael.lee@helico-tech.com

On Sat, Feb 20, 2027 at 16:12 Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Michael Lee <michael.lee@helico-tech.com>, Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@helico-tech.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 17:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Expansion stitch checkpoint: usage growth & add-on timeline

Hi Michael — thanks for the quick read. Answering inline below and adding details for Procurement: 

> 1) Does the SKU require a different region/instance class?
It does not require a different region. The Realtime + priority burst SKU runs on our same Dedicated pools in us-west-2. The only caveat is that priority burst reserves a small GPU headroom; Diego - confirm we can honor Helico's latency SLOs for model sizes up to 7B (expectation: sub-120ms p50).

> 2) Pricing & pilot term
We've drafted a 6-month pilot row in the attached pricing_addendum_heli_co.pdf stub (attached). Pilot rate and a three-tier renewal schedule are included. If you'd like, we can add explicit language for the >2x usage band renewal cap.

> 3) One-time provisioning fee?
No one-time provisioning fee for the first two pilot teams — we'll absorb initial env work. Any cross-account custom integration would be scoped separately.

DocuSign & PO notes for Procurement:
- DocuSign signatory: Michael Lee (VP, Data Platform) — primary
- Redwood legal contact: Rishi Malhotra (rishi.malhotra@redwood.com)
- PO accepted after signature; Procurement can route to billing@redwood.com with PO number.

Next steps we've assigned internally: Diego to confirm SLO compatibility (by Mon); Mateo to finalize addendum and send DocuSign link; Monica to post onboarding slots.

Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

---
Attachments: pricing_addendum_heli_co.pdf (PDF - pricing stub)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 20 Feb 2027 18:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Expansion stitch checkpoint: usage growth & add-on timeline

Quick internal: approved the 5% pilot credit for Helico as discussed (applies to first 6 months). Monica, please include the credit line in the DocuSign addendum and note it in the billing memo.

Diego — prioritize the SLO check for 7B models; Marissa and Mateo will need ETA to share with Procurement.

Rishi Malhotra
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.com

source.subject: Proposal: staged top-up and synchronized renewal timeline
messages:
From: Nina Park <nina.park@brightwyre.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@brightwyre.com
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: staged capacity top-up + align renewal?

Hi Mateo / Ben,

Quick note from Brightwyre — Q2 usage forecasts are trending higher than we built into our current commit. We expect a ~40% spike during a limited pilot in Apr–Jun for a new vector search feature. Procurement asked whether we can top up capacity now and align the renewal to the same date later this year to simplify invoicing.

Can Redwood support:
- A staged top-up (50k token-equivalent units initially, then an auto-top to 150k if usage threshold hit)
- Co-term the new top-up to our Feb 2028 renewal date rather than creating a separate annual contract
- A short-term uplift discount for the first 3 months while we ramp (we're hoping for something in the 8–12% range)

I've attached Q4 usage and a short SOW draft procurement asked us to share. Our PO team is looped (procurement@brightwyre.com) — they'll need an approximate invoice schedule and any amendment language so they can prepare the PO.

Thanks — happy to hop on a call if that's easier.

— Nina Park
Head of Platform Ops, Brightwyre
Nina.Park@brightwyre.com

Attachments: Brightwyre_usage_Q4_2026.pdf, requested_sow_draft.docx

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Park <nina.park@brightwyre.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@brightwyre.com
Date: Tue, 19 Jan 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: staged capacity top-up + align renewal?

Nina — thanks for the details and the usage files. Attaching an initial pricing model (redwood_pricing_staged-topup.xlsx) and a short amendment outline. High-level options from our side:

A) Staged top-up + co-term (recommended)
  - Initial 50k token-equivalent top-up (billing immediately)
  - Auto-extend to 150k if a daily avg threshold is breached for 7 consecutive days
  - Co-term the added capacity to your Feb 1, 2028 renewal; pro-rate the remaining months on a daily basis
  - Ramp discount: 10% for months 1–3 on the new top-up capacity only

B) Temporary burst capacity (short-lived, meter-based)
  - No co-term, higher per-token rate but no contract change

If you prefer A, we can draft an amendment + DocuSign and propose invoice timing: initial invoice at execution for the 50k, monthly true-up thereafter. Monica — can you confirm a realistic onboarding window for the staged add? Ben — if this looks directionally OK I'll pull legal in for the amendment language.

Thanks,
Mateo
Principal Account Exec — Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: redwood_pricing_staged-topup.xlsx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Park <nina.park@brightwyre.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@brightwyre.com
Date: Wed, 20 Jan 2027 08:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: staged capacity top-up + align renewal?

Nina, Mateo — appreciate the quick turnaround. A couple of negotiation notes from the commercial side:

- We can confirm the 10% ramp discount for months 1–3 as Mateo suggested.
- For co-terming: to keep accounting clean we typically amortize the remaining months of the top-up into the Feb renewal and show the pro-rate on the invoice. That gives Brightwyre a single renewal event but keeps monthly visibility.
- We can also offer a small incentive for a 12-month commitment on the top-up (additional 3% discount on the top-up portion) if Brightwyre is willing to commit past the co-term date.

Would procurement be ok with a PO that references an amendment (we'll provide amendment document IDs) and two invoices (initial execution + Feb renewal balance)? If PO needs a fixed amount, we can provide a capped estimate.

Ben Carter
Director, Commercial — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

Attachment: pricing-amendment-v1.pdf (proposal excerpt)

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Park <nina.park@brightwyre.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@brightwyre.com
Date: Fri, 22 Jan 2027 11:16:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: staged capacity top-up + align renewal?

Hi all — operational notes from CS:

- We can onboard the 50k top-up in a two-week window after amendment signing. For the auto-extend to 150k we recommend a canary rollout (route 10% of traffic initially) to validate latency and KV cache behavior.
- Risk items: spikes may temporarily increase latency; we'll provide guardrails (rate limits and early cache priming).
- Onboarding tasks we need from Brightwyre: expected peak query patterns, contact for escalation, and whether the pilot requires dedicated instances.

If you're comfortable with the ameneded billing cadence Ben suggested, I'll slot Brightwyre for the week of Feb 8 for onboarding (assuming signing by end of Jan).

Thanks — Monica
Customer Success Lead — Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com

Quoted: 
> On Wed, Jan 20, 2027 at 08:47 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > We can also offer a small incentive for a 12-month commitment on the top-up (additional 3% discount) if Brightwyre is willing to commit past the co-term date.

Attachment: onboarding-checklist.pdf

From: Nina Park <nina.park@brightwyre.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@brightwyre.com
Date: Mon, 25 Jan 2027 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: staged capacity top-up + align renewal?

Thanks everyone — internal approval move fast once we have the amendment language and an estimated invoice schedule. Brightwyre is comfortable proceeding with option A (staged top-up + co-term) with the 10% ramp discount. Procurement asked for two clarifications before sign:

1) Can Redwood cap the initial invoice value (we can accept a capped estimate for PO)?
2) We need the DocuSign link for the amendment and the SOW so legal can sign remotely.

If you can add the optional 3% long-term incentive to the amendment as an opt-in clause, I'll present that to our CFO (she's likely to greenlight if it's in writing). PO number will be BWR-33450 pending the capped invoice amount.

If you send the DocuSign and a capped estimate today I can get procurement to sign the PO next week.

Best,
Nina
Head of Platform Ops — Brightwyre
Nina.Park@brightwyre.com

--- Forwarded attachment stub ---
requested_sow_draft.docx (SOW summary)

DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.com/sign/BW-9827

  - Ramp discount: 10% for months 1–3 on the new top-up capacity only

B) Temporary burst capacity (short-lived, meter-based)
  - No co-term, higher per-token rate but no contract change

If you prefer A, we can draft an amendment + DocuSign and propose invoice timing: initial invoice at execution for the 50k, monthly true-up thereafter. Monica — can you confirm a realistic onboarding window for the staged add? Ben — if this looks directionally OK I'll pull legal in for the amendment language.

Ben Carter

source.subject: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys (VP/CFO signoff needed)
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 Jan 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys (VP/CFO signoff needed)

Marissa —

Following up from the demo last week and the procurement back-and-forth: CardioSys (Helen Park) is asking for a one-time onboarding/on‑ramp credit to cover integration and data ingress costs. They are a mid-stage digital cardiology vendor; expected first-year usage is concentrated in Q1 and Q2 while they integrate our SDK.

Key ask: they want a 25% credit on the first three months of committed spend (approx. $120k list across quarters). Sales is proposing a conditional on‑ramp credit capped at $90k if we can secure a 12‑month commitment and milestone signoffs.

Revenue/commitment snapshot:
- Proposed committed ARR: $360,000 (12 months)
- Expected first‑quarter billings (list): ~$120,000
- Requested/on‑ramp ask: 25% of Q1 (~$30k/mo x 3 = $90k)

Why this matters: CardioSys is vertically strategic for our healthcare playbook and intends to be a reference customer in clinical workflows. They also plan to pilot across three hospital systems in Q2, which could meaningfully accelerate adoption if we secure the reference.

Risks:
- Sets a precedent for future onboarding credits for other mid-market health customers.
- Short-term gross margin hit if they underuse after onboarding.

Mitigations:
- Credit contingent on 12‑month commitment and three milestone check-ins (integration complete, first production run, reference approval).
- Clawback clause: pro rata repayment if they terminate before 6 months without cause.
- Require minimum monthly usage floor to avoid churn risk.

Attached: CardioSys_PROPOSAL_v3.pdf (proposal with line items) and usage-projection-q1.csv (sales forecast).

Can you review and advise whether we can sign off up to $90k or if we should cap lower? If approved, we need a VP/CFO signoff to include in amendment.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: CardioSys_PROPOSAL_v3.pdf (proposal)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 20 Jan 2027 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys (VP/CFO signoff needed)

Thanks Mateo — good summary. Quick context from my side:
- Product/solution fit is strong; Helen was explicit that onboarding costs (data normalization, PHI-safe ingestion tooling) are the blocker to signing quickly.
- Procurement pushed hard for a dollar‑based onboarding offset rather than an ongoing discount.

Proposed structure (sales recommendation):
1) One‑time onboarding credit of $75,000 paid as deduction across first three invoices (not recurring).
2) Must obtain a 12‑month commitment at the proposed ARR and a signed amendment capturing clawback if they cancel before month 6.
3) Require monthly usage reporting and a CS checkpoint at months 1, 3, and 6 before the credit is released in full.

Why $75k vs $90k: gives us flexibility and keeps a negotiating buffer. If procurement pushes, we can escalate up to $85k with finance approval.

Please see my redline on the proposed amendment (attached inline in the PDF). If Markus is comfortable with an authorization up to $85k with the conditions above, we can finalize paperwork and trigger DocuSign.

- Marissa
VP, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Jan 19, 2027 at 09:12 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message quoted)
>

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 21 Jan 2027 08:21:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys — Exec summary for approval

Markus / Lauren —

For executive review: I’m forwarding the thread and adding a concise memo below so you can sign off quickly.

Executive summary (two pages condensed to bullets):
- Customer: CardioSys (digital cardiology), procurement lead Helen Park.
- Ask: One‑time onboarding/on‑ramp credit to offset integration costs. Sales recommends $75k credit; sales escalation cap $85k. Mateo proposed a maximum of $90k as a negotiating anchor.
- Commitment: Customer to sign a 12‑month commitment at $360k ARR (monthly billings).
- Payment mechanics: Credit applied as invoice reduction across first three invoices; full credit only released after milestone verifications.
- Financial impact (estimate):
  • If we approve $75k credit: ~22% reduction to Q1 list revenue (~$120k) but with a secured 12‑month ARR of $360k.
  • Incremental gross margin impact for Q1 estimated at -$45k (after COGS), with payback expected by month 7 under forecasted usage.
- Strategic rationale: access to three hospital system pilots and probable Reference 1 within 6 months; health vertical acceleration priority.
- Risks and mitigations: precedent risk (mitigated with strict milestone release and clawback); usage shortfall (mitigated via minimum usage floor and monthly reporting).

Recommendation: Approve up to $85,000 in one‑time onboarding credit subject to:
1) 12‑month commitment executed and signed amendment reviewed by Legal.
2) CS-owned milestone verification (integration, first production, reference approval).
3) Finance to record the credit as deferred revenue adjustment with monthly reporting.

If you authorize up to $85k we will push a final amendment to the customer and route DocuSign. If you want a firmer cap at $75k we will close faster but will likely need an additional negotiation round.

Please reply with approval level and any conditions.

Thanks,
Mateo
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

> On Wed, Jan 20, 2027 at 10:03 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > (Marissa message quoted)
>

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 22 Jan 2027 15:44:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys (VP/CFO signoff needed)

Thanks for the clear package and the mitigation plan. I approve a one‑time onboarding credit of $80,000 under the following conditions:

1) Amendment capturing:
   - 12‑month minimum commitment at the proposed ARR.
   - Credit to be applied across first three invoices and released only after the three milestones are validated in writing by CS.
   - Clawback pro rata if termination occurs before month 6 (exceptions for material breach by Redwood).

2) Finance note: record the credit as a contra‑revenue adjustment and monitor monthly; provide a short monthly variance report to me for 6 months.

3) Legal to review and sign the amendment language before we send to the customer; legal to include a short usage-floor clause (minimum monthly tokens/requests threshold) to protect against underuse.

4) Sales: do not publicize this as a standard offer — it is an exception tied to CardioSys and the specific milestone structure.

Marissa / Mateo: proceed with the $80k amendment draft and loop Legal. Lauren, finance please confirm the accounting flow and set up the deferred tracking.

-Markus
CFO, Redwood Inference

On Thu, Jan 21, 2027 at 08:21 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (executive summary forwarded)
> [Attachment: usage-projection-q1.csv (sales forecast)]

Attached: CardioSys_PROPOSAL_v3.pdf (proposal with line items) and usage-projection-q1.csv (sales forecast).

Can you review and advise whether we can sign off up to $90k or if we should cap lower? If approved, we need a VP/CFO signoff to include in amendment.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: CardioSys_PROPOSAL_v3.pdf (proposal)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 20 Jan 2027 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys (VP/CFO signoff needed)

Thanks Mateo — good summary. Quick context from my side:
- Product/solution fit is strong; Helen was explicit that onboarding costs (data normalization, PHI-safe ingestion tooling) are the blocker to signing quickly.
- Procurement pushed hard for a dollar‑based onboarding offset rather than an ongoing discount.

Proposed structure (sales recommendation):
1) One‑time onboarding credit of $75,000 paid as deduction across first three invoices (not recurring).
2) Must obtain a 12‑month commitment at the proposed ARR and a signed amendment capturing clawback if they cancel before month 6.
3) Require monthly usage reporting and a CS checkpoint at months 1, 3, and 6 before the credit is released in full.

Why $75k vs $90k: gives us flexibility and keeps a negotiating buffer. If procurement pushes, we can escalate up to $85k with finance approval.

Please see my redline on the proposed amendment (attached inline in the PDF). If Markus is comfortable with an authorization up to $85k with the conditions above, we can finalize paperwork and trigger DocuSign.

- Marissa
VP, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Jan 19, 2027 at 09:12 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message quoted)
>

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 21 Jan 2027 08:21:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys — Exec summary for approval

Markus / Lauren —

For executive review: I’m forwarding the thread and adding a concise memo below so you can sign off quickly.

Executive summary (two pages condensed to bullets):
- Customer: CardioSys (digital cardiology), procurement lead Helen Park.
- Ask: One‑time onboarding/on‑ramp credit to offset integration costs. Sales recommends $75k credit; sales escalation cap $85k. Mateo proposed a maximum of $90k as a negotiating anchor.
- Commitment: Customer to sign a 12‑month commitment at $360k ARR (monthly billings).
- Payment mechanics: Credit applied as invoice reduction across first three invoices; full credit only released after milestone verifications.
- Financial impact (estimate):
  • If we approve $75k credit: ~22% reduction to Q1 list revenue (~$120k) but with a secured 12‑month ARR of $360k.
  • Incremental gross margin impact for Q1 estimated at -$45k (after COGS), with payback expected by month 7 under forecasted usage.
- Strategic rationale: access to three hospital system pilots and probable Reference 1 within 6 months; health vertical acceleration priority.
- Risks and mitigations: precedent risk (mitigated with strict milestone release and clawback); usage shortfall (mitigated via minimum usage floor and monthly reporting).

Recommendation: Approve up to $85,000 in one‑time onboarding credit subject to:
1) 12‑month commitment executed and signed amendment reviewed by Legal.
2) CS-owned milestone verification (integration, first production, reference approval).
3) Finance to record the credit as deferred revenue adjustment with monthly reporting.

If you authorize up to $85k we will push a final amendment to the customer and route DocuSign. If you want a firmer cap at $75k we will close faster but will likely need an additional negotiation round.

Please reply with approval level and any conditions.

Thanks,
Mateo
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

> On Wed, Jan 20, 2027 at 10:03 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > (Marissa message quoted)
>

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 22 Jan 2027 15:44:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Tiered On‑Ramp Credit — CardioSys (VP/CFO signoff needed)

Thanks for the clear package and the mitigation plan. I approve a one‑time onboarding credit of $80,000 under the following conditions:

1) Amendment capturing:
   - 12‑month minimum commitment at the proposed ARR.
   - Credit to be applied across first three invoices and released only after the three milestones are validated in writing by CS.
   - Clawback pro rata if termination occurs before month 6 (exceptions for material breach by Redwood).

2) Finance note: record the credit as a contra‑revenue adjustment and monitor monthly; provide a short monthly variance report to me for 6 months.

3) Legal to review and sign the amendment language before we send to the customer; legal to include a short usage-floor clause (minimum monthly tokens/requests threshold) to protect against underuse.

4) Sales: do not publicize this as a standard offer — it is an exception tied to CardioSys and the specific milestone structure.

Marissa / Mateo: proceed with the $80k amendment draft and loop Legal. Lauren, finance please confirm the accounting flow and set up the deferred tracking.

-Markus
CFO, Redwood Inference

On Thu, Jan 21, 2027 at 08:21 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (executive summary forwarded)
> [Attachment: usage-projection-q1.csv (sales forecast)]

source.subject: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com>, security_ops@acme-industries.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 11 Jan 2027 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff

Hi Samantha / Acme security team,

Thanks again for partnering with Redwood for the onboarding. To keep things predictable for your engineering and security teams, we put together a short sprint plan covering SSO (SAML), SCIM provisioning, IP allowlist windows, and service-account setup. Goal: end-to-end validation in your staging environment and a production cutover window the week after.

High-level requests from our side:
- We need your SAML IdP metadata file and an SP metadata from us (attached).
- A list of CIDR ranges to allow to our staging endpoints for the verification window.
- A service-account owner and desired permission scope (we'll provide a recommended matrix).

I attached an initial copy of our SP metadata and a draft org policy template for review. Monica (CS lead) will run the technical validations; Ben (infra contact) will create the service accounts and confirm scopes with your team.

Proposed milestone dates (all PST):
1) Exchange metadata + baseline policy review — Thu Jan 14
2) SCIM test provisioning (staging) — Mon Jan 18
3) SAML sign-on smoke tests — Tue Jan 19
4) Cutover readiness call / signoff — Wed Jan 20

Can you confirm the IP ranges you can open for the staging window and who on your side will own service-account approvals? If the dates don't work we can shift by a week.

Thanks,

--
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
[Attachment: acme_saml_metadata.xml (xml)]
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: security_ops@acme-industries.com, procurement@acme-industries.com
Date: Mon, 11 Jan 2027 12:02:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff

Hi Mateo / team,

Thanks — that schedule looks reasonable. Quick answers:
- I'll attach our IdP metadata in a follow-up (trying to confirm a minor cert rollover first).
- Staging IPs: we can open 54.212.0.0/18 and 18.197.32.0/19 for the test window.
- Service-account approver will be me and our security lead, Priya Natarajan (cc'ed from security_ops).

Notes/questions:
- For SCIM, we need to ensure group sync is optional — only user attributes initially.
- Can Redwood provide a minimal permission matrix for the service account (read vs write scopes) — that will help our procurement and least-privilege review.

I'll send the IdP metadata by tomorrow morning.

Samantha
Director, IT Security
Acme Industries
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Jan 2027 08:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff

Thanks Samantha — appreciate the quick responses. A couple of clarifications from the CS side:

- SCIM: we can scope to user-only attributes for initial provisioning; group sync is a separate sprint due to group mappings and role translation. We'll add an explicit opt-in checkbox in the SCIM job.

- Service-account matrix: attached a draft (service_account_matrix.xlsx). Ben and Jordan will propose the minimal RBAC for the account used to call our SCIM API and to manage templates.

- For the IP allowlist: please confirm whether the staging CIDRs will be open for the full Jan 18–20 block; we'll need at least a 4-hour window for repeated end-to-end tests.

I'll schedule a 30-minute technical alignment call for Jan 14 10:00 AM PST unless you'd prefer a different time.

Best,

--
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: service_account_matrix.xlsx (xlsx)]
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Jan 2027 09:10:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff

Jordan / team — quick operations note:

- I'll provision two service accounts in our staging tenant: one for SCIM provisioning (scim-sync@redwood-stg) and one for template management (template-api@redwood-stg). Jordan, can you confirm minimum scopes? My recommendation (subject to Acme approval):
  * scim-sync: users.read, users.write (no group write)
  * template-api: templates.read, templates.create (no delete)

- Once we have approval from Samantha/Priya, I'll rotate keys and post fingerprints in the secure Drive folder.

- For auditability, I'll also enable token retention for 30 days on these staging accounts and forward logs to the joint Drive link.

Ben
Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: org_policy_template.md (md)]
From: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@acme-industries.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 11:07:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff

On Mon, Jan 11, 2027 at 12:02 PM Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com> wrote:
> Hi Mateo / team,
> 
> Thanks — that schedule looks reasonable. Quick answers:
> - I'll attach our IdP metadata in a follow-up (trying to confirm a minor cert rollover first).
> - Staging IPs: we can open 54.212.0.0/18 and 18.197.32.0/19 for the test window.
> - Service-account approver will be me and our security lead, Priya Natarajan (cc'ed from security_ops).

Folks — update: Priya confirmed group sync should be disabled for now, and the minimal permission set for scim-sync is acceptable. We approved the two staging CIDRs for Jan 18 09:00–18:00 EST.

We've uploaded IdP metadata to the Drive folder and added a comment on the service account matrix (see Drive link). We're good to proceed with the SCIM user sync on Jan 18 at 09:15 EST.

Thanks everyone — Priya and I will be on the call for signoff.

Samantha
Director, IT Security
Acme Industries
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@acme-industries.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@acme-industries.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Jan 2027 15:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Orchestrating Enterprise Entry Controls — Sprint plan & ops handoff

Thanks Samantha / Priya — great to have that confirmation. Quick checklist before the Jan 18 tests: 
1) Acme IdP metadata present in Drive (confirmed)
2) Staging CIDRs opened for Jan 18 09:00–18:00 EST (confirmed)
3) Service-account scopes approved and keys rotated for staging accounts — Ben doing this today
4) Monica to run SCIM dry-run at 09:15 EST and validate user attributes only

If all goes well during the dry-run we'll execute SAML smoke tests at 11:00 EST and aim for a light production cutover on Wed Jan 20 pending final signoff. I'll keep notes in the Jira ONB-821 that Monica is updating.

Appreciate the collaboration — looping back with test notes after the call.

--
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Acme Industries

source.subject: Customer update: Root cause, mitigations, and closure plan for Jan 9 token latency surge
messages:
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@helixhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Cc: Olivia Tan <olivia@helixhealth.com>, ops@helixhealth.com
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2027 02:15:23 -0800
Subject: Immediate: Token latency spikes causing timeouts on inference routes

Hi Mateo,

We started seeing a latency spike on the Redwood endpoints serving our model around 01:50 PST. Several downstream jobs timed out and our nightly batch inference failed. Error rates climbed from ~0.3% to ~8% over a 20-minute window.

Can you confirm if anything changed on your side? This is impacting production dashboards and a subset of our customer-facing predictions. We opened an internal alert (ops@helixhealth.com) but wanted to loop you in directly.

- Sanjay

Attachments: none
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Jan 2027 02:37:12 -08:00
Subject: Re: Immediate: Token latency spikes causing timeouts on inference routes

Hi Sanjay — thanks for the flag and sorry for the disruption. We're investigating now.

Initial notes from our on-call: we observed increased tail latency on the shared hosted pool serving your traffic; a small portion of requests hit cold starts and some requests retried to fall back to a slower quantized variant. We've opened INF-1248 to track.

Immediate mitigations we've applied (already in progress):
- Temporarily routing Helix Health traffic to a warmed dedicated slice to reduce cross-tenant interference.
- Rolling back a non-critical autoscaler tweak that changed cache retention thresholds.
- Increasing request timeouts on the client-facing gateway to reduce observed timeouts while we stabilize.

Can we schedule a 30-minute debrief this morning to walk through timeline and next steps? I've looped Marissa for account context and Stephanie/Connor from CS/SRE.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0134

Attachments: none
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>, Sanjay Rao <sanjay@helixhealth.com>, Olivia Tan <olivia@helixhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 10 Jan 2027 09:48:05 -08:00
Subject: Draft RCA and timeline: Jan 9 latency surge (INF-1248)

On Fri, Jan 9, 2027 at 02:37, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Sanjay — thanks for the flag and sorry for the disruption. We're investigating now.

Team — preliminary RCA below for Helix Health customers impacted 01:50–02:12 PST:

Summary (short):
A combination of a caching retention regression introduced by an autoscaler parameter change and an unexpected workload pattern caused increased KV-cache evictions for the quantized model variant. This triggered higher cold-start frequency and forced many requests onto a slower runtime path, increasing tail latency and request timeouts.

Timeline (UTC/PST converted where relevant):
- 2027-01-09 01:46 PST (09:46 UTC): Autoscaler parameter change deployed (internal config change to cache TTL behavior).
- 2027-01-09 01:50 PST: Helix Health jobs start to see rising latency; retries escalate.
- 2027-01-09 01:52 PST: Monitoring alerts trigger for elevated 95/99 p50->p99 gap.
- 2027-01-09 01:55 PST: Our mitigations: routing Helix traffic to dedicated warmed slice; rollback autoscaler tweak initiated.
- 2027-01-09 02:10 PST: Latency returns to baseline after KV cache warmup and rollback completed.

Root cause:
- The autoscaler change reduced the retention of model KV caches under burst scenarios. For quantized variants, a higher miss rate requires more frequent recomputation of attention caches and some codepaths fall back to slower kernels. The combined effect increased CPU/GPU scheduling pressure and tail latency.

Immediate corrective actions taken:
1) Rolled back the autoscaler cache-retention change (completed 01:56 PST).
2) Re-routed Helix traffic to a warmed dedicated slice to avoid multi-tenant cold starts (started 01:55 PST).
3) Increased operational timeouts at the gateway while we stabilize (01:58 PST).

Planned next steps (owner / ETA):
- Connor: Deliver a patch to the autoscaler to reintroduce adaptive retention logic + additional unit tests (INF-1248) — target deploy 2027-01-17.
- SRE team: Add telemetry for quantized variant cache hit/miss rates and alerting on deviations — target 2027-01-20.
- Product/Build: Add a deployment gate for cache-related changes to avoid global rollouts without canarying — target 2027-01-28.
- Mateo/Marissa: Customer follow-up & credit discussion (we propose a one-month service credit applied to Jan bill) — coordinate by 2027-01-15.

Attachments: draft RCA PDF (will attach final summary once reviewed).

Connor O'Brien
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.ai
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@helixhealth.com>, Olivia Tan <olivia@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 14 Jan 2027 16:22:41 -08:00
Subject: Final customer brief + action items — Jan 9 latency surge (attached)

Hi Sanjay and Olivia,

Thank you again for your patience while we completed our internal analysis. Attached is the final one-page RCA and the patch diff we will deploy next week. Below are the concise action items and delivery commitments we discussed.

What happened (one sentence):
A recent autoscaler tweak reduced KV-cache retention under burst loads for the quantized model, which increased cold-start frequency and caused a short-lived tail latency spike on Jan 9.

What we fixed (done):
- Rolled back the autoscaler change during the incident (01:56 PST).
- Temporarily routed Helix traffic to a warmed dedicated slice to avoid cross-tenant cold-starts.
- Increased gateway timeouts to prevent client-observed timeouts while we worked the fix.

Committed deliverables and timelines:
1) INF-1248 patch (autoscaler adaptive retention + tests) — Connor — deploy window 2027-01-17, post-deploy verification 2027-01-18.
2) Telemetry & alerts for quantized cache-miss signals — SRE — 2027-01-20.
3) Deployment gate for cache-related runtime changes (product + infra) — 2027-01-28.
4) Customer operational follow-up & one-month service credit proposal — Mateo & Marissa — delivery by 2027-01-15.

Next steps for Helix Health:
- Please review the attached RCA PDF and the planned patch diff. If you approve the proposed deploy window (Jan 17), we'll schedule the maintenance slot and share the verification playbook.
- If you prefer we can run the patch in a mirrored staging environment first; we can coordinate that with your ops team.

We value Helix Health's production usage and understand the impact. We're committing engineering and SRE time to ensure this class of problem is guarded against in the future.

If you want to discuss live, I'm available for a call tomorrow (Jan 15) between 09:00–12:00 PST.

Thanks,

Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0134

Attachments:
- 20270114-helix-rca-summary.pdf (one-page RCA, 87KB)
- INF-1248-patch.diff (autoscaler patch, 4KB)

source.subject: Immutable admin ledger export & provenance handoff
messages:
From: Elena Mendez <elena.mendez@healthsys.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: admin activity ledger export for SOC 2 attestation

Hi Mateo,

HealthSys is preparing for a SOC 2 Type II evidence collection. The auditor has asked for an "admin activity ledger" covering the last 12 months for all tenant-level admin activity and operator actions tied to HealthSys resources. They want details about what events are logged, retention windows, and the export options including tamper-evidence (checksums/HMACs, signed manifests).

Can you provide:
- A concise list of the event types captured (sign-ins, role changes, API key creates/revokes, console actions, operator session traces, etc.)
- Retention policy for those records and any short-term caches that may not be included
- Export formats you can produce and options for proving integrity (HMAC, signed manifest, time-stamped attestation)
- Delivery options (SFTP, presigned S3, encrypted drive) and estimated lead time

Auditor note: they prefer immutable file artifacts (compressed NDJSON/CSV) with a per-file SHA256 and an overall HMAC signed with a customer-visible key or Redwood KMS attestation.

Would appreciate a reply with an export plan and quick ETA. We may need this in the auditor portal by Jan 8.

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Mendez
Sr. Compliance Manager, HealthSys

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Mendez <elena.mendez@healthsys.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity ledger export for SOC 2 attestation

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear list. Quick confirmation up front: we can produce a forensically-signed export that meets auditor requirements. Below is a short plan and the formats we recommend. I’ve looped in Kim and Jordan for retention & ops details.

Summary of logged event types (high level) — we will include full event schema mapping in the manifest:
- Authentication events: successful & failed admin console sign-ins, SSO assertions, MFA challenges
- Privileged role changes: grant/revoke of org roles, permission group edits, service admin promotions
- API credential lifecycle: API key/secret creation, rotation, revocation, enrollments
- Operator console actions: runbook executions, instance access, console button clicks that trigger privileged operations
- Job/Batch operations: scheduled model/deployment changes, rollout events, autoscale triggers
- KV/KV-cache admin ops: manual cache invalidations, prefix cache clears tied to customer tenants
- Admin API calls: higher-fidelity logs for actions executed via internal APIs (includes request_id)
- Operator session traces: ephemeral session start/stop, source_ip, agent_fingerprint (where available)

Retention and indexing:
- Canonical admin ledger: 12 months (rolling) by default for hosted customers; can be extended under Enterprise terms.
- Short-term operational logs (high-cardinality telemetry) are retained for 90 days before summarization/aggregation (these are not part of the canonical per-event ledger unless requested).
- All timestamps in the ledger are normalized to UTC and include a millisecond precision ISO8601 field and a monotonic sequence id to preserve ordering.

Export formats & tamper-evidence options:
- Primary export: gzipped NDJSON (one event per line) + per-file SHA256 checksums.
- Alternate: split CSV shards (admin_ledger_shard-0000.csv.gz ...) with consistent column mapping.
- Manifest: admin_ledger_manifest.csv (metadata rows that map shard -> SHA256 -> event_count -> first_ts -> last_ts -> monotonic_range).
- Integrity: per-file SHA256 + an overall manifest HMAC computed with a dedicated Redwood HSM/KMS signing key. We can either:
  1) Sign the manifest with Redwood's HSM and provide an attestation certificate (PKCS7) that includes key id and timestamp, or
  2) Compute an HMAC with a customer-supplied key via a secure key exchange (customer-provided HSM/KMS integration required).
- Time-stamp: we can include a timestamped attestation from our KMS and optionally timestamp it against an external TSA (time-stamp authority) for the manifest.

Delivery & packaging options:
- Option A (one-off snapshot): produce a signed bundle (tar.gz) with shards, manifest, export_manifest.hmac.sig and deliver to your SFTP endpoint. Typical ETA: 5–7 business days.
- Option B (on-demand presigned S3): we stage shards in a locked S3 bucket and provide presigned URLs; we also upload the manifest & signature to the same path. ETA: 3–5 business days (faster if we already have an S3 handshake).
- Option C (scheduled monthly snapshot): for audits that require periodic attestations.

Attachments: I’m attaching a short manifest template (admin_ledger_manifest.csv) and an example export_manifest.hmac.sig stub to show structure.

Next steps: Kim/Jordan — can you confirm retention extensions for HealthSys and whether we can do customer-supplied key signing for this account?

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Technical Lead — Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Mendez <elena.mendez@healthsys.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Dec 2026 08:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity ledger export for SOC 2 attestation

Mateo — thanks, this is a good outline. A few operational clarifications from retention & evidence POV: 

1) Timestamp fidelity & ordering:
- Confirmed: canonical ledger uses UTC ISO8601 (milliseconds) + monotonic sequence id per-tenant. Important for the auditor: supply both fields in manifest and each event.

2) Identity & provenance fields to ensure traceability:
- Include operator_id (internal user id), display_name, source_ip, request_id, user_agent, and, when applicable, service_principal_id.
- For API key events include the key_id and partial_fingerprint (first/last 8 chars) — never full secret.

3) Chain-of-custody and HSM attestation:
- Our preferred path is Redwood HSM-signed manifest + a PKCS7 attestation that includes the signing certificate and timestamp. That meets most auditor checklists.
- If HealthSys requires customer-managed signing, we can accept a wrapped request to compute HMAC with the supplied key via a secure KMS ingress flow; this requires an SSO/KMS key exchange and adds ~3 business days to ETA.

4) Redaction & sampling options:
- For PII-sensitive fields (rare for admin ledger) we can provide a redacted view and a separate PII-index map to show what was redacted, but auditors mostly want full fidelity.

5) Delivery window
- We can stage the snapshot in a locked S3 path and mirror to your SFTP. To meet the Jan 8 portal deadline, we need confirmation by Dec 28 to request an on-demand snapshot with the 5–7 day SLA.

Please confirm: (A) preferred delivery method (SFTP vs presigned S3), (B) whether HealthSys wants Redwood HSM attestation or will provide a signing key, and (C) any additional fields the auditor specifically expects.

—Kim
Kimberly Park
Head of Evidence Delivery, Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Elena Mendez <elena.mendez@healthsys.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: admin activity ledger export for SOC 2 attestation

Hi Mateo,

HealthSys is preparing for a SOC 2 Type II evidence collection. The auditor has asked for an "admin activity ledger" covering the last 12 months for all tenant-level admin activity and operator actions tied to HealthSys resources. They want details about what events are logged, retention windows, and the export options including tamper-evidence (checksums/HMACs, signed manifests).

Can you provide:
- A concise list of the event types captured (sign-ins, role changes, API key creates/revokes, console actions, operator session traces, etc.)
- Retention policy for those records and any short-term caches that may not be included
- Export formats you can produce and options for proving integrity (HMAC, signed manifest, time-stamped attestation)
- Delivery options (SFTP, presigned S3, encrypted drive) and estimated lead time

Auditor note: they prefer immutable file artifacts (compressed NDJSON/CSV) with a per-file SHA256 and an overall HMAC signed with a customer-visible key or Redwood KMS attestation.

Would appreciate a reply with an export plan and quick ETA. We may need this in the auditor portal by Jan 8.

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Mendez
Sr. Compliance Manager, HealthSys



From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Mendez <elena.mendez@healthsys.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 21 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: admin activity ledger export for SOC 2 attestation

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear list. Quick confirmation up front: we can produce a forensically-signed export that meets auditor requirements. Below is a short plan and the formats we recommend. I’ve looped in Kim and Jordan for retention & ops details.

Summary of logged event types (high level) — we will include full event schema mapping in the manifest:
- Authentication events: successful & failed admin console sign-ins, SSO assertions, MFA challenges
- Privileged role changes: grant/revoke of org roles, permission group edits, service admin promotions
- API credential lifecycle: API key/secret creation, rotation, revocation, enrollments
- Operator console actions: runbook executions, instance access, console button clicks that trigger privileged operations
- Job/Batch operations: scheduled model/deployment changes, rollout events, autoscale triggers
- KV/KV-cache admin ops: manual cache invalidations, prefix cache clears tied to customer tenants
- Admin API calls: higher-fidelity logs for actions executed via internal APIs (includes request_id)
- Operator session traces: ephemeral session start/stop, source_ip, agent_fingerprint (where available)

Retention and indexing:
- Canonical admin ledger: 12 months (rolling) by default for hosted customers; can be extended under Enterprise terms.
- Short-term operational logs (high-cardinality telemetry) are retained for 90 days before summarization/aggregation (these are not part of the canonical per-event ledger unless requested).
- All timestamps in the ledger are normalized to UTC and include a millisecond precision ISO8601 field and a monotonic sequence id to preserve ordering.

Export formats & tamper-evidence options:
- Primary export: gzipped NDJSON (one event per line) + per-file SHA256 checksums.
- Alternate: split CSV shards (admin_ledger_shard-0000.csv.gz ...) with consistent column mapping.
- Manifest: admin_ledger_manifest.csv (metadata rows that map shard -> SHA256 -> event_count -> first_ts -> last_ts -> monotonic_range).
- Integrity: per-file SHA256 + an overall manifest HMAC computed with a dedicated Redwood HSM/KMS signing key. We can either:
  1) Sign the manifest with Redwood's HSM and provide an attestation certificate (PKCS7) that includes key id and timestamp, or
  2) Compute an HMAC with a customer-supplied key via a secure key exchange (customer-provided HSM/KMS integration required).
- Time-stamp: we can include a timestamped attestation from our KMS and optionally timestamp it against an external TSA (time-stamp authority) for the manifest.

Delivery & packaging options:
- Option A (one-off snapshot): produce a signed bundle (tar.gz) with shards, manifest, export_manifest.hmac.sig and deliver to your SFTP endpoint. Typical ETA: 5–7 business days.
- Option B (on-demand presigned S3): we stage shards in a locked S3 bucket and provide presigned URLs; we also upload the manifest & signature to the same path. ETA: 3–5 business days (faster if we already have an S3 handshake).
- Option C (scheduled monthly snapshot): for audits that require periodic attestations.

Attachments: I’m attaching a short manifest template (admin_ledger_manifest.csv) and an example export_manifest.hmac.sig stub to show structure.

Next steps: Kim/Jordan — can you confirm retention extensions for HealthSys and whether we can do customer-supplied key signing for this account?

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Technical Lead — Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Privacy elevator pitch + short DPA snapshot for demo
messages:
From: Rebecca Shaw <rebecca@globocorp.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Cc: Acme Demo Ops <demo-ops@acme.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Hosted API privacy snippet for Wednesday demo

Hi Mateo / Laura,

We have a product demo with our execs on Wednesday and they asked for a one-paragraph privacy blurb and a short DPA summary we can read aloud. They specifically want: a) a single-sentence elevator pitch about what data we process when using the hosted API, b) a line about subprocessors (where to find the list), and c) a plain-English summary of cross-border transfers (one or two lines).

Can you send something trade-ready by EOD today? We will include it in the slide deck. If there's a legal-approved snippet or a link to the subprocessor map that we can point to in the demo notes, that would be perfect.

Thanks,
Rebecca
Rebecca Shaw
Director, Product Partnerships
Globocorp

(Attachment: example-slide.png (image))

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Rebecca Shaw <rebecca@globocorp.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API privacy snippet for Wednesday demo

Thanks Rebecca — looping legal and product marketing.

Draft below (first pass) — trying to keep it short for slides:

"Redwood's Hosted API processes text and metadata you send for inference to generate model outputs; we do not use customer prompt or response content to train our models for hosted customers unless you opt in. We work with a small set of subprocessors (cloud infra, logging, and analytics providers) — the current list is at redwood.ai/subprocessors. Data may be transferred across regions to serve requests; we use contractual safeguards and standard transfer mechanisms to protect data in transit and at rest."

Questions for legal: is the "opt-in" phrasing accurate for Hosted (vs. Dedicated/Private)? Also, need a one-sentence public-facing DPA summary I can paste into the slide notes. Attached: dpa_snippet_v1.docx (candidate language).

Mateo
Product Marketing, Redwood Inference

-----Original message-----
On Sat, Dec 19, 2026 at 09:12 AM Rebecca Shaw <rebecca@globocorp.com> wrote:
> Hi Mateo / Laura,
> 
> We have a product demo with our execs on Wednesday and they asked for a one-paragraph privacy blurb..."

From: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>, Rebecca Shaw <rebecca@globocorp.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 12:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API privacy snippet for Wednesday demo

Mateo — thanks for the draft. A few edits from legal to keep it tight and accurate for a public audience:

1) Change "we do not use customer prompt or response content to train our models for hosted customers unless you opt in" to "we do not use customer prompts or outputs to train our models for customers using the Hosted API; customers can opt in to data usage for model improvement."
2) Replace the public link placeholder with the canonical subprocessor map link and encourage a link to the living map rather than embedding a list in slides.
3) For cross-border transfers, use this short phrase: "Data may be processed or stored in multiple countries; we rely on standard contractual measures and appropriate safeguards to protect transfers."

Suggested final slide copy (one paragraph):

"Redwood's Hosted API processes text and related metadata you provide to generate model outputs. We do not use prompts or outputs to train models for Hosted customers by default; customers can opt in to allow model improvement. A list of subprocessors is maintained at the public subprocessor map (link in slide notes). Data may be processed or stored in multiple countries; we use contractual safeguards and standard transfer mechanisms to protect data."

I've attached the DPA excerpt we typically share internally (dpa_snippet_v1.docx) and the current subprocessor export (subprocessors_map_v2.csv). For the slide notes, Rishi — can you confirm the wording around "canonical subprocessor map" and whether we should point to the console privacy card instead of the public page?

— Mina
Legal Counsel, Redwood Inference

On Sat, Dec 19, 2026 at 10:03 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Rebecca — looping legal and product marketing.
> 
> Draft below (first pass) — trying to keep it short for slides:...

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Rebecca Shaw <rebecca@globocorp.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 20 Dec 2026 15:44:00 -08:00
Subject: Final: Privacy & DPA lines for Wednesday demo

Hi Rebecca — attaching a cleaned-up 1-pager and the approved slide copy. Short version below that you can paste into the deck.

Slide (one-paragraph, public-facing):
"Redwood's Hosted API processes text and related metadata you send to generate helpful outputs. By default, Hosted customers' prompts and outputs are not used to train our models; customers can opt in to allow model improvement. We publish a live subprocessor map you can link to for details. Data may be processed or stored in multiple countries; contractual safeguards and standard transfer mechanisms are used to protect transfers."

Slide notes / backup bullets (for the presenter):
- DPA summary: Our standard DPA includes processor obligations, data return/deletion terms, and transfer safeguards (SCCs where applicable). See internal excerpt (attached) for quick reference.
- Subprocessors: primary categories are cloud compute, telemetry/metrics, and monitoring; link to the living map in the console for live names.

source.subject: Testimonial & Press Quote — confidentiality gating + logo use
messages:
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@heliosanalytics.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Hi Mateo —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our marketing team would like to run a short case-study blurb and include the Redwood logo on a landing page. Two quick asks from our side: (1) can we use a 1–2 sentence quote attributed to Helios Analytics, and (2) permission to display Redwood's logo alongside Helios on the microsite?

We will follow any embargo window you require and can accept limited wording. I attached a draft testimonial the team put together.

Best,
Jordan
Senior Product Ops
Helios Analytics

Attachment: Helios_testimonial_draft.docx
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.example>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Jordan —

Appreciate the draft. Two internal items we need to confirm before signoff:

1) Publicity clause in our MSA/order form — our standard language limits reference rights to a short customer quote and logo use only if the customer: (a) confirms the final quote in writing, (b) agrees to an approved embargo date or immediate publish, and (c) grants perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free rights for Redwood to use the quote/logo in marketing materials.
2) We have a privacy gating requirement: testimonials may not include Helios customer data or screenshots containing PII without a separate asset approval.

Kimberly/Hannah — can legal confirm whether the attached MSA_redlines_v2.pdf (I pulled the latest redlines from procurement) are sufficient to cover the proposed quote + logo use? Preferred outcomes: explicit line in the order form that: (i) authorizes the exact quote text, (ii) requires final approval via reply-all, and (iii) limits logo files to an approved set. If we need more restrictive language (time-limited license or embargo windows), call that out.

Jordan — I cc'd our legal and contracts leads so we can close quickly. If you can confirm that the quote in the docx is the final verbiage, that speeds this up.

Thanks,
Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: MSA_redlines_v2.pdf, Helios_testimonial_draft.docx
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>, Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.example>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 10:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Hi all —

Quick legal read: the MSA_redlines_v2.pdf covers the basics but I recommend three specific edits before we publish anything:

- Publicity/Reference Rights: change from blanket "perpetual, worldwide" to "perpetual for marketing use as defined in Section 2.3, subject to Customer's final written approval of the quote and a logoset approval process." This clarifies we won't reuse disallowed logo variants.
- Embargo: add an optional embargo field on the order form (Order Form: Section 1.5) so customers can specify a release date; default to immediate if blank.
- Asset Approval: require that any screenshots, dashboards, or excerpts that contain derived metrics be redacted or submitted for separate asset approval.

If Helios confirms the quote text and signs the short addendum in the order form, I can approve the testimonial and logo usage. I attached a one-page snippet you can paste into the order form to capture these items (I included bracketed language and a short signature line).

Jordan — if you want us to keep the rights narrower (time-limited logo license or limited channels), let us know now. Otherwise the language below is what we typically push to marketing.

Suggested order-form snippet (paste-ready):
"[Publicity & Reference Rights] Customer grants Redwood a non-exclusive, royalty-free right to use the Final Approved Quote and Customer's official logo in Redwood marketing materials worldwide. Customer must provide final written approval of the Quote and an official logo file prior to publication. Customer may specify an embargo date in the Order Form; absent an embargo, publication may occur upon written approval. Any assets containing customer data or non-public metrics require separate asset approval."

-Kim
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: orderform_publicity_snippet.txt
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.example>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Thanks everyone — this looks good. I confirmed with Priya (marketing) and we accept the suggested snippet, embargo set to 2027-01-15 for the product launch, and we will provide the official logo files in SVG/PNG. We also confirm no screenshots with live customer data will be used.

Priya and I have signed the order form addendum via DocuSign, and I uploaded the signed stub here: https://redwood.app/docusign/DW-4931

Mateo — please proceed to publish the approved quote after the embargo date and notify me of the permalink.

Jordan
Senior Product Ops
Helios Analytics

Attachment: OrderForm_Helios_signed_stub.pdf

On Thu, Dec 14, 2026 at 10:25 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example> wrote:
> Hi all —
> 
> Quick legal read: the MSA_redlines_v2.pdf covers the basics but I recommend three specific edits before we publish anything: ...
>

messages:
From: Emily Chen <emily@cardiopulse.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-10T09:22:00-08:00
Subject: Packaging flexibility for enterprise pilot (split-hosted/dedicated)

Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — product team loved the latency profile. We're heading into procurement and there's one ask from our CFO: can Redwood split the onboarding commitment between Hosted API (for quick ramp) and Dedicated (for predictable throughput) while waiving the standard one-time onboarding surcharge? They want a 9-month ramp: 40% Hosted, 60% Dedicated after month 3. Also asking for a one-time go-live credit to offset migration costs (roughly $45k).

We're ready to sign an LOI if we can get a commercial note that preserves a blended rate and a one-off credit. I'll need internal approvals quickly — is there an exec memo you can circulate for VP/CFO signoff?

Best,
Emily
VP Product, CardioPulse

[Attachment: draft_cardio_requirements.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-11T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Packaging flexibility for enterprise pilot (split-hosted/dedicated) — proposed exception memo attached

Team,

Forwarding CardioPulse's ask. Key commercial asks:
- Split-bucket commitment (40% Hosted first 3 months, transition to 60% Dedicated)
- Waive one-time onboarding surcharge (~$12k) or roll into credits
- One-time go-live credit ~$45k
- Blended committed rate across both modes; customer requests LOI contingent on a short exec note

Attached is a first-pass exception memo and an initial P&L (pricing_model_splitbucket.csv). Can finance and commercial please weigh in on:
1) Net present value hit over 12 months for the proposed credits
2) Alternative structures (stair-step credit, deferred surcharge)
3) Suggested approval language for VP/CFO sign-off

I've looped in Markus for margin impact and Marissa for precedent. Aim to finalize a memo by Monday so I can share with the customer.

Thanks,
Mateo
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: exception_memo_v2.pdf]

We're heading into procurement and there's one ask from our CFO: can Redwood split the onboarding commitment between Hosted API (for quick ramp) and Dedicated (for predictable throughput) while waiving the standard one-time onboarding surcharge? They want a 9-month ramp: 40% Hosted, 60% Dedicated after month 3. Also asking for a one-time go-live credit to offset migration costs (roughly $45k).
> 
> We're ready to sign an LOI if we can get a commercial note that preserves a blended rate and a one-off credit. I'll need internal approvals quickly — is there an exec memo you can circulate for VP/CFO signoff?
> 
> Best,
> Emily

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T09:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Packaging flexibility for enterprise pilot — high-level recommendation

Mateo,

High level:
- Approve with guardrails. CardioPulse is strategic in healthcare vertical and credible for high-volume usage; win would help us land other regional health systems.
- Replace a single $45k credit with a two-part credit: $25k go-live + $20k staging credit paid out against verified onboarding milestones (safe for revenue recognition and reduces risk).
- Do not fully waive the onboarding surcharge; convert surcharge to a deferred credit amortized over 6 months. That preserves margin while giving the customer cashflow relief.

Impact:
- Estimated 12-month margin hit assuming current model mix: ~6.8% (see pricing_model_splitbucket.csv). If we amortize surcharge, impact drops to ~4.1%.

Approval language suggestion for VP/CFO memo:
"Authorize a one-time commercial concession for CardioPulse: $25k up-front go-live credit, $20k milestone-backed staging credit, and conversion of the $12k onboarding surcharge into a deferred credit amortized over 6 months. Approval contingent on LOI and finance verification of usage forecast."

Happy to draft the final memo for routing. Marissa
Director, Commercial Operations
Redwood Inference

On Wed, Dec 11, 2026 at 11:03 AM Mateo Alvarez wrote:
> Team,
> 
> Forwarding CardioPulse's ask...

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-13T16:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Packaging flexibility for enterprise pilot — finance clearance

Mateo/Marissa,

Finance sign-off required from CFO, but we can support Marissa's structure with minor edits. Notes:
- Recommend cap on cumulative credits: maximum $35k payable unless actual usage exceeds forecast by 25% — then escalate to VP Commercial for additional concessions.
- Require a 6-month reporting cadence with access to token-level telemetry; include a termination clawback if customer reduces committed usage by >30% within the first 9 months.
- If CFO signs off, I will prepare the formal memo with the exact accounting treatment and routing to VP/CFO for signature.

If you want, I'll draft the memo language and include an approval checkbox matrix for the CFO.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Finance Manager
Redwood Inference

> 
> High level:

source.subject: Incremental commit & bridge pricing — Neurolytics uplift request
messages:
From: Elena Ramos <elena.r@neurolytics.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: mid-contract capacity increase + co-terming?

Hi Mateo,

Quick note from our side — we had a much larger model training run last week and usage jumped ~3.8x. We expect a sustained uplift for the next 3 months while we finish the pilot. Can we talk about adding incremental committed capacity for the remainder of our contract and co-term the added capacity to our existing renewal date (2027-06-30)?

Key asks:
- A top-line delta price for an incremental 50% commit for 6 months (starting 12/15),
- Whether you can honor current seat/discount band for the uplift or if there is a separate uplift discount,
- Any impact to our billing cadence if we co-term mid-contract.

I attached our monthly usage export for the team to review.

Thanks —
Elena
Head of ML Ops
Neurolytics

Attachment: Neurolytics_MonthlyUsage.csv (CSV, 1.2MB)
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ramos <elena.r@neurolytics.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: mid-contract capacity increase + co-terming?

Elena — thanks for the note and the usage file, that helps. Short answer: yes, we can support a 6-month incremental commit and co-term to your renewal date; a few details to align on so I can pull official numbers from finance.

Two straightforward paths we usually offer for this scenario:

1) Short-term uplift (6 months) as a true-up top-up: you pay list for the extra capacity but keep your existing discount bands for the base contract. This avoids changing the annual commitment but has less favorable unit economics on the incremental tokens.

2) Temporary committed add-on that is billed as a bridged amendment and annualized pro-rata to your renewal date. This lets us offer an uplift discount (typically 10–18% depending on size) in exchange for committing to the top-up amount for the 6-month window and co-terming it to your June renewal. We can also structure vouchers if you prefer consumption-first billing with guaranteed reserved capacity.

What I need from you to get a firm quote from finance:
- Confirm target uplift (you mentioned ~50% — is that an average monthly uplift or peak?)
- Whether you want consumption-first (vouchers) or reserved capacity add-on

If that works, I’ll spin up two concrete numbers (path 1 and path 2) and loop in Marissa (re: legal amendment) once we land on the structure.

Proposed next step: 30-minute call Thursday 12/3 at 11am PT to agree on structure and opt-in.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mateo: (415) 555-0192

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Dec 2026 09:07:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Neurolytics uplift — pricing ask (need quick finance/legal sign-off)

Team — forwarding Neurolytics ask. They have a sustained ~50% uplift projected for Dec–May and want added committed capacity co-termed to 2027-06-30.

My proposal to present to the customer (draft):
- Option A: True-up top-up (no change to base contract). Incremental units billed at list (no uplift discount).
- Option B: 6-month committed add-on, co-termed to renewal, with a temporary uplift discount of 15% on the incremental units; Redwood to provide pro-rata invoice split into monthly billings. Addendum to include a sunset clause that returns unused vouchers to Neurolytics as credits if utilization <60% over the bridge period.

Questions for finance/legal:
- Can we go to 15% discount on the uplift band for a 6-month add-on (deal DL-NTX-4553)?
- Any issues with the proposed voucher credit mechanism (refund as credit vs cash) from an accounting standpoint?
- Is there any SLA/priority change we need to note for short-term reserved capacity?

Numbers: if they add 50% (approx. 5.5M tokens/mo additional) we estimate additional monthly ARR of $48k at list; a 15% uplift discount would move that to $40.8k/mo. Full numbers below if helpful — happy to run modeling.

—Mateo

On Thu, Dec 2, 2026 at 10:03 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — thanks for the note and the usage file, that helps. Short answer: yes, we can support a 6-month incremental commit and co-term to your renewal date; a few details to align on so I can pull official numbers from finance.
>
> Two straightforward paths we usually offer for this scenario:
>
> 1) Short-term uplift (6 months) as a true-up top-up: you pay list for the extra capacity but keep your existing discount bands for the base contract. This avoids changing the annual commitment but has less favorable unit economics on the incremental tokens.
>
> 2) Temporary committed add-on that is billed as a bridged amendment and annualized pro-rata to your renewal date. This lets us offer an uplift discount (typically 10–18% depending on size) in exchange for committing to the top-up amount for the 6-month window and co-terming it to your June renewal. We can also structure vouchers if you prefer consumption-first billing with guaranteed reserved capacity.
>
> What I need from you to get a firm quote from finance:
> - Confirm target uplift (you mentioned ~50% — is that an average monthly uplift or peak?)
> - Whether you want consumption-first (vouchers) or reserved capacity add-on
>
> If that works, I’ll spin up two concrete numbers (path 1 and path 2) and loop in Marissa (re: legal amendment) once we land on the structure.
>
> Proposed next step: 30-minute call Thursday 12/3 at 11am PT to agree on structure and opt-in.
>
> Best,
> Mateo
From: Elena Ramos <elena.r@neurolytics.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Dec 2026 16:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Incremental commit & bridge pricing — Neurolytics uplift request

On Wed, Dec 3, 2026 at 9:07 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — forwarding Neurolytics ask. They have a sustained ~50% uplift projected for Dec–May and want added committed capacity co-termed to 2027-06-30.

Thanks everyone for the quick follow-up. We reviewed options internally and prefer Option B (committed add-on co-termed). The reasons: predictability for our infra budgeting and the slightly better unit economics. A few clarifying points before we sign: 

- We prefer vouchers (consumption-first) but need clarity on the crediting rule for unused vouchers — Neurolytics requests a refund as applied credit to the renewal invoice rather than carrying forward unused vouchers indefinitely.
- Can Redwood cap the uplift discount to 15% for the contracted 6-month window? We can agree to a 6-month minimum commitment to take effect 12/15.
- On co-terming: we want the add-on to align to 2027-06-30 and have the pro-rated charges reflected on monthlies; final settlement at renewal.

If Redwood can confirm these points, I’ll instruct procurement to proceed quickly; please attach the amendment and we’ll route internally.

Best,
Elena Ramos
Head of ML Ops | Neurolytics

Attachment: proposed-amendment-v2.pdf

---
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
Phone: (415) 555-0192
