text:
From: Elena Ramos <elena.r@neurolytics.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: mid-contract capacity increase + co-terming?

Hi Mateo,

Quick note from our side — we had a much larger model training run last week and usage jumped ~3.8x. We expect a sustained uplift for the next 3 months while we finish the pilot. Can we talk about adding incremental committed capacity for the remainder of our contract and co-term the added capacity to our existing renewal date (2027-06-30)?

Key asks:
- A top-line delta price for an incremental 50% commit for 6 months (starting 12/15),
- Whether you can honor current seat/discount band for the uplift or if there is a separate uplift discount,
- Any impact to our billing cadence if we co-term mid-contract.

I attached our monthly usage export for the team to review.

Thanks —
Elena
Head of ML Ops
Neurolytics

Attachment: Neurolytics_MonthlyUsage.csv (CSV, 1.2MB)

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ramos <elena.r@neurolytics.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 2 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: mid-contract capacity increase + co-terming?

Elena — thanks for the note and the usage file, that helps. Short answer: yes, we can support a 6-month incremental commit and co-term to your renewal date; a few details to align on so I can pull official numbers from finance.

Two straightforward paths we usually offer for this scenario:

1) Short-term uplift (6 months) as a true-up top-up: you pay list for the extra capacity but keep your existing discount bands for the base contract. This avoids changing the annual commitment but has less favorable unit economics on the incremental tokens.

2) Temporary committed add-on that is billed as a bridged amendment and annualized pro-rata to your renewal date. This lets us offer an uplift discount (typically 10–18% depending on size) in exchange for committing to the top-up amount for the 6-month window and co-terming it to your June renewal. We can also structure vouchers if you prefer consumption-first billing with guaranteed reserved capacity.

What I need from you to get a firm quote from finance:
- Confirm target uplift (you mentioned ~50% — is that an average monthly uplift or peak?)
- Whether you want consumption-first (vouchers) or reserved capacity add-on

If that works, I’ll spin up two concrete numbers (path 1 and path 2) and loop in Marissa (re: legal amendment) once we land on the structure.

Proposed next step: 30-minute call Thursday 12/3 at 11am PT to agree on structure and opt-in.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mateo: (415) 555-0192

messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Martinez <sofia@acme.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T09:17:00-07:00
Subject: Observability retention + dashboards — pilot offer and pricing options

Hi Sofia —

Thanks again for the product walkthrough last week. I spoke with Priyom and the AE team about the retention/tracing ask you flagged — we put together a couple of concrete options so you can evaluate cost vs. observability coverage without committing to an annual top-line change. High level: 

- Option A (Pilot/Trial): 3-month pilot, limited to 10 TB ingest/month, 30-day retention, full tracing + advanced dashboards. Includes one cross-team onboarding session and up to 4 custom queries. We can provide a $10k pilot credit to offset ingestion costs.
- Option B (Commit): 12-month commitment with tiered retention buckets (30/90/365 days) and 20% discount on dashboard seat add-ons; recommended for teams that want guaranteed cost predictability.

Questions for you: which option makes sense operationally? If Pilot is best, we’ll provision a dedicated trial project and assign a single owner on our side. If you want to move to Commit, we can share a sample SOW and a pro-forma invoice.

I attached a short one-pager (observability_onepager_retention.pdf) that summarizes feature differences. Priyom, looping you in to outline pricing details if Sofia wants numbers.

Next steps: can we lock a quick 30-minute call tomorrow to walk the pilot scope?

Thanks,
Mateo
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147

Attachment: observability_onepager_retention.pdf (one-pager)
From: Sofia Martinez <sofia@acme.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Procurement <procurement@acme.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability retention + dashboards — pilot offer and pricing options

Mateo —

Appreciate the quick follow-up. The pilot option sounds closest to what our infra team needs so we can validate trace fidelity and dashboard UX before locking retention windows. A few clarifying points from procurement/infra: 

1) Can the $10k pilot credit be applied to egress or is it ingestion-only? We have spikes from customers that would blow through ingestion quickly.
2) For the trial environment, will data be isolated per-project (we require separation between staging and production telemetry)?
3) Are there limits on API call rates during pilot? Our pipelines do frequent small writes.

If you can confirm those items, procurement is happy to sign a simple pilot MSA. Also — any chance of an extra short-term concession on dashboard seats? We’d like 5 seats for the 3-month pilot.

Best,
Sofia Martinez
Head of Infra, Acme Analytics
sofia@acme.com

On Thu, Dec 3, 2026 at 09:17 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sofia —
> 
> Thanks again for the product walkthrough last week. I spoke with Priyom and the AE team about the retention/tracing ask you flagged — we put together a couple of concrete options so you can evaluate cost vs. observability coverage without committing to an annual top-line change. High level: 
> 
> - Option A (Pilot/Trial): 3-month pilot, limited to 10 TB ingest/month, 30-day retention, full tracing + advanced dashboards. Includes one cross-team onboarding session and up to 4 custom queries. We can provide a $10k pilot credit to offset ingestion costs.
> - Option B (Commit): 12-month commitment with tiered retention buckets (30/90/365 days) and 20% discount on dashboard seat add-ons; recommended for teams that want guaranteed cost predictability.
> 
> Questions for you: which option makes sense operationally? If Pilot is best, we’ll provision a dedicated trial project and assign a single owner on our side. If you want to move to Commit, we can share a sample SOW and a pro-forma invoice.
> 
> I attached a short one-pager (observability_onepager_retention.pdf) that summarizes feature differences. Priyom, looping you in to outline pricing details if Sofia wants numbers.
> 
> Next steps: can we lock a quick 30-minute call tomorrow to walk the pilot scope?
> 
> Thanks,
> Mateo
> Account Executive | Redwood Inference
> mateo@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147
From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Martinez <sofia@acme.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T12:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability retention + dashboards — pilot offer and pricing options

Hi Sofia —

Thanks — quick answers inline and a concrete counter we can move on today: 

1) Pilot credit scope: the $10k credit can be applied to ingestion and storage but not egress. That said, for this pilot we can grant an additional $2k ingress buffer if you expect short-term ingestion spikes (one-time).

2) Isolation: yes — we’ll provision a dedicated project and apply strict RBAC rules so staging/prod telemetry remain separated. We’ll also enable a short-lived export key for your SREs.

3) API rate limits: no hard throttling for the pilot under 10 TB/month. We will provide a rate guidance doc and monitor for hotspots.

Dashboard seats: we can include 5 seats for the 3-month pilot at no charge.

Attached is our pricing calc (ACME_pricing_calc_v2.xlsx) that walks through a run-rate view and shows the commit discount waterfall for Option B. Our proposed pilot agreement items: 

- 3 months, dedicated project, 10 TB ingest/month cap (with one-time +$2k buffer)
- 30-day retention for traces and spans; standard indexing for advanced dashboards
- 5 free dashboard seats for the pilot
- Pilot credit: $10k applied to storage/ingest + $2k one-time buffer

If that looks ok, I’ll follow up with a simple pilot MSA and a link to the onboarding checklist.

Thanks,
Priyom Das
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
priyom@redwood.com

Attachments: ACME_pricing_calc_v2.xlsx (pricing workbook)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Sofia Martinez <sofia@acme.com>, Acme Procurement <procurement@acme.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability retention + dashboards — pilot offer and pricing options

Looping in as the manager — this concession path is approved on our side. A few notes for the legal/procurement team at Acme: 

- The pilot MSA will be a short, two-page agreement covering scope, data handling, and limited liability for trial usage only.
- For billing: no invoicing during the pilot; we will track usage against the $12k aggregate credit (10k + 2k buffer) and provide an itemized summary at pilot close.
- If Acme elects to move to Option B commit within the pilot window, we will honor the 20% dashboard-seat discount retroactively for seats purchased within 30 days of conversion.

Sofia — please let us know a good time for the 30-minute kickoff. Priyom/Mateo — please prepare the MSA draft and onboarding checklist; target to send both by COB tomorrow.

Regards,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales — US | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference & observability for LLM apps

Appreciate the quick follow-up. The pilot option sounds closest to what our infra team needs so we can validate trace fidelity and dashboard UX before locking retention windows. A few clarifying points from procurement/infra: 

1) Can the $10k pilot credit be applied to egress or is it ingestion-only? We have spikes from customers that would blow through ingestion quickly.
2) For the trial environment, will data be isolated per-project (we require separation between staging and production telemetry)?
3) Are there limits on API call rates during pilot? Our pipelines do frequent small writes.

If you can confirm those items, procurement is happy to sign a simple pilot MSA. Also — any chance of an extra short-term concession on dashboard seats? We’d like 5 seats for the 3-month pilot.

Best,
Sofia Martinez
Head of Infra, Acme Analytics
sofia@acme.com

On Thu, Dec 3, 2026 at 09:17 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sofia —
> 
> Thanks again for the product walkthrough last week. I spoke with Priyom and the AE team about the retention/tracing ask you flagged — we put together a couple of concrete options so you can evaluate cost vs. observability coverage without committing to an annual top-line change. High level: 
> 
> - Option A (Pilot/Trial): 3-month pilot, limited to 10 TB ingest/month, 30-day retention, full tracing + advanced dashboards. Includes one cross-team onboarding session and up to 4 custom queries. We can provide a $10k pilot credit to offset ingestion costs.
> - Option B (Commit): 12-month commitment with tiered retention buckets (30/90/365 days) and 20% discount on dashboard seat add-ons; recommended for teams that want guaranteed cost predictability.
> 
> Questions for you: which option makes sense operationally? If Pilot is best, we’ll provision a dedicated trial project and assign a single owner on our side. If you want to move to Commit, we can share a sample SOW and a pro-forma invoice.
> 
> I attached a short one-pager (observability_onepager_retention.pdf) that summarizes feature differences. Priyom, looping you in to outline pricing details if Sofia wants numbers.
> 
> Next steps: can we lock a quick 30-minute call tomorrow to walk the pilot scope?
> 
> Thanks,
> Mateo
> Account Executive | Redwood Inference
> mateo@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147


From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Martinez <sofia@acme.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T12:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability retention + dashboards — pilot offer and pricing options

Hi Sofia —

Thanks — quick answers inline and a concrete counter we can move on today: 

1) Pilot credit scope: the $10k credit can be applied to ingestion and storage but not egress. That said, for this pilot we can grant an additional $2k ingress buffer if you expect short-term ingestion spikes (one-time).

2) Isolation: yes — we’ll provision a dedicated project and apply strict RBAC rules so staging/prod telemetry remain separated. We’ll also enable a short-lived export key for your SREs.

3) API rate limits: no hard throttling for the pilot under 10 TB/month. We will provide a rate guidance doc and monitor for hotspots.

Dashboard seats: we can include 5 seats for the 3-month pilot at no charge.

Attached is our pricing calc (ACME_pricing_calc_v2.xlsx) that walks through a run-rate view and shows the commit discount waterfall for Option B. Our proposed pilot agreement items: 

- 3 months, dedicated project, 10 TB ingest/month cap (with one-time +$2k buffer)
- 30-day retention for traces and spans; standard indexing for advanced dashboards
- 5 free dashboard seats for the pilot
- Pilot credit: $10k applied to storage/ingest + $2k one-time buffer

If that looks ok, I’ll follow up with a simple pilot MSA and a link to the onboarding checklist.

Thanks,
Priyom Das
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
priyom@redwood.com

Attachments: ACME_pricing_calc_v2.xlsx (pricing workbook)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Sofia Martinez <sofia@acme.com>, Acme Procurement <procurement@acme.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability retention + dashboards — pilot offer and pricing options

Looping in as the manager — this concession path is approved on our side. A few notes for the legal/procurement team at Acme: 

- The pilot MSA will be a short, two-page agreement covering scope, data handling, and limited liability for trial usage only.
- For billing: no invoicing during the pilot; we will track usage against the $12k aggregate credit (10k + 2k buffer) and provide an itemized summary at pilot close.
- If Acme elects to move to Option B commit within the pilot window, we will honor the 20% dashboard-seat discount retroactively for seats purchased within 30 days of conversion.

Sofia — please let us know a good time for the 30-minute kickoff. Priyom/Mateo — please prepare the MSA draft and onboarding checklist; target to send both by COB tomorrow.

Regards,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales — US | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference & observability for LLM apps

source.subject: Cross‑Cloud Closing Cadence: evaluation checkpoints → invoice handoff
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood-inference.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@medixcore.com>, MedixCore Procurement <procurement@medixcore.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood-inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood-inference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Cross‑Cloud Closing Cadence: evaluation checkpoints → invoice handoff

Hi Elena / procurement team —

Thanks again for the call today. Per our discussion, attached is a concise MAP (v1) that lays out the acceptance checkpoints across evaluation, technical signoff, commercial approval, and PO/invoice handoff for a GCP private offer + AWS/Azure fallbacks. Key asks and timelines are in the first page so procurement can map milestones to internal approval gates.

Quick highlights:
- Evaluation window: 2 weeks (sandbox + curated acceptance tests)
- Tech acceptance: runbook for perf/latency targets; test harness to be run by MedixCore infra on Day 10
- Billing path: if PO arrives before 2026‑12‑10 we push invoice on enclosed billing route (AWS private offer via marketplace invoice flow; GCP handled via private offer + commitment queue)
- Legal/Docs: SOW draft attached; we expect redlines by end of week 1 after evaluation completes

Attachments: 20261119-MedixCore-MAP-v1.pdf, redwood-pricing-breakdown-3cloud.xlsx, SOW-draft-Redwood-MedixCore.docx

Next steps:
1) Can procurement confirm the PO routing owner (name + email) and preferred invoice cadence?
2) Can Elena confirm the two people who will run acceptance tests (names + Slack)?

I can host a 30‑minute handoff with Finance and Legal on Monday if that helps accelerate.

Thanks —
Mateo Alvarez
Enterprise Sales (Marketplace & Private Offers)
Redwood Inference
From: Elena Rossi <elena@medixcore.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: MedixCore Procurement <procurement@medixcore.com>, Samir Patel <samir.p@cloudconsult.io>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 12:02:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Cross‑Cloud Closing Cadence: evaluation checkpoints → invoice handoff

Thanks Mateo — we reviewed the MAP and it looks clear. A couple quick clarifications before we confirm the PO owner internally: 

1) Technical acceptance: our infra team will own the sandbox runs. I will assign two engineers — Nora Kim and Akash Mehta — to run the tests and share logs. They will need the test harness commands and the KV/cache warmup checklist.

2) Billing: Procurement prefers GCP private offer as the primary route. If the private offer timeline slips, we may accept an AWS private offer as fallback but would like Redwood to confirm any billing/cost delta after quantization suggestions are applied.

3) SOW: Our legal team flagged one redline around data retention (45 days vs 30). They also requested a short clause on audit logs accessibility. I can send the redline PDF by Friday EOD.

Procurement owner for PO: procurement@medixcore.com (we'll route through our ERP). I will escalate the invoice cadence decision to Finance and confirm whether we need Net‑30 or Net‑45 by Monday.

Elena
VP Product Operations | MedixCore
From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood-inference.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@medixcore.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood-inference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 10:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Cross‑Cloud Closing Cadence: evaluation checkpoints → invoice handoff

Appreciate the quick read, Elena — and good flags. A few operational clarifications from our side to keep the MAP execution tight: 

Acceptance checklist (short):
- Latency SLO: p95 <= 220 ms for 512 tokens on the GCP n2a test fleet (we include kernel profile).
- Throughput: sustained 10 rps with KV cache enabled.
- Output QA: 10 curated prompts with pass/fail rules (we'll share the prompt set).
- Security gate: SOC2 evidence + audit log access (we can provide read‑only access to audit buckets for 45 days).

Billing path options (confirming):
A) GCP private offer (preferred): Redwood issues invoice after private offer acceptance; MedixCore submits PO to procurement@medixcore.com → Finance processes Net‑30/Net‑45 per your decision.
B) AWS private offer fallback: Redwood will map the same committed units; however, invoicing moves through AWS Marketplace (we'll coordinate unit mapping and a one‑time reconciliation).

Action items we will complete this week: upload the test harness + KV warmup script to a shared Drive, and send the prompt set for evaluation. If MedixCore legal sends the SOW redline, Jordan will review and return an annotated SOW within 48 hours.

If helpful, Jordan and I can join your Finance/Procurement sync on Monday 11/29 at 9am ET.

—Grace
Director, Customer Success (Marketplace)
Redwood Inference
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood-inference.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@medixcore.com>, MedixCore Procurement <procurement@medixcore.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood-inference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood-inference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Fri, 26 Nov 2026 14:32:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Cross‑Cloud Closing Cadence: evaluation checkpoints → invoice handoff

Elena — thanks for the SOW redlines (received Fri morning). I reviewed the data retention and audit clause: we can extend retention to 45 days for logging metadata; for raw user data the standard is 30 days unless a formal DPA amendment is signed. I flagged language to preserve your audit access via read‑only buckets; attached is an annotated SOW and a concise change list.

On the billing delta question: if the customer accepts an AWS fallback, our pricing sheet shows a potential 6–9% uplift after instance type mapping and marketplace fees. We'll absorb any immediate delta on the first invoice reconciliation if MedixCore wants to proceed without contract changes, but long term we'll align the committed unit equivalence on the contract.

I've attached the annotated SOW and a redlined pricing snapshot. If procurement confirms PO routing and the Net‑term (Net‑30 likely), Finance can expect our invoice within 5 business days after PO receipt.

Attachments: SOW-draft-Redwood-MedixCore.docx (annotated), redwood-pricing-breakdown-3cloud.xlsx (snapshot)

Jordan Blake
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference
From: MedixCore Procurement <procurement@medixcore.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood-inference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood-inference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Elena Rossi <elena@medixcore.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2026 16:48:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Cross‑Cloud Closing Cadence: evaluation checkpoints → invoice handoff

Team — procurement confirms the following and we are ready to accept the MAP as the working plan: 

- PO routing owner: procurement@medixcore.com (ERP receives and routes to Finance).
- Net terms: Net‑30.
- Acceptance engineers: Nora Kim (nora.kim@medixcore.com), Akash Mehta (akash.mehta@medixcore.com).
- Expected PO date: target 2026‑12‑08 pending Finance signoff.

We request Redwood to issue the final SOW with your annotated changes and an invoice template we can use for ERP mapping. Once we receive the final SOW and invoice template, we will issue the PO.

Thanks — looking forward to the sandbox kickoff.
Procurement Team
MedixCore

source.subject: Operational Marshall Plan & Milepost Roster - Kickoff and Owner Slate
messages:
From: Elaine Park <elaine@aurora-cloud.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.dev>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Operational Marshall Plan & Milepost Roster - Kickoff

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our infra team reviewed Redwood’s latency profiles and we want to move forward with a staged pilot. Before we lock dates, we need a concise mutual action plan (MAP) that calls out milestones, owners, blackout windows, and acceptance criteria.

Top-level asks from Aurora: 
- a 6-week pilot timeline with explicit milestone dates
- on-call and rollback owners for each milestone
- a single procurement/contract milestone (DocuSign) we can gate on

source.subject: CodeCity student hackathon — partner stipend, vendor invoice, and PO routing
messages:
From: Aisha Gupta <partnerships@codecity.org>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal — CodeCity Student Hackathon (requesting partner stipend & vendor payment)

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the chat last week — CodeCity is excited to run a mid-December student hackathon and we'd love Redwood to sponsor with a $5,000 partner stipend to cover student prizes and hospitality. We will be using SparkEvents LLC (local events vendor) to handle onsite logistics and need either:

- a vendor PO issued to SparkEvents LLC (for their services, estimated $2,800)
- plus a partner stipend payment to CodeCity (check or ACH), or
- alternatively, Redwood can cover the vendor directly and issue a stipend to CodeCity.

Attached: budget_proposal.xlsx (estimated line items and headcount)

Can you confirm the right path for a PO and what info Procurement/Finance will need? Event date: 2026-12-18. Expected final bill not to exceed $6,000 including a small buffer.

Best,
Aisha Gupta
Partnerships Lead
CodeCity Foundation

---
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Gupta <partnerships@codecity.org>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal — CodeCity Student Hackathon (requesting partner stipend & vendor payment)

Hi Aisha — thanks for the summary and the proposal.

Marissa / Dylan — looping you in to route the PO/stipend. Key asks:
- Confirm we can issue a PO to SparkEvents LLC for up to $2,800 (services/production)
- Confirm partner stipend routing for CodeCity (up to $2,200) — will this be a vendor payment to CodeCity or a sponsorship budget line?
- Cost center(s): please advise GL coding. My current suggestion: 4021 (Field Marketing — EDU) for the vendor and 7020 (Partnerships & Sponsorships) for the stipend, but confirm.

Aisha: I'll follow up once Finance confirms required docs — W9 for SparkEvents, final invoice, and copy of CodeCity's payment instructions if they want ACH.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Field Marketing Manager
Redwood Inference

---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>, Aisha Gupta <partnerships@codecity.org>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 10:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal — CodeCity Student Hackathon (requesting partner stipend & vendor payment)

Thanks for the context, Mateo.

Procurement/Finance checklist to issue PO and process stipend:
1) Vendor paperwork: W9 for SparkEvents LLC (required). If SparkEvents is a sole proprietor/contractor, note it here.
2) Final vendor invoice (PDF) that lists services & amounts (no PO number yet is fine).
3) Clarify how the stipend should be paid to CodeCity (W9 for CodeCity if they require payment to an entity; or if it's a grant, we need the organization's payment instructions and tax status).
4) Confirm final spend cap. You mentioned $6,000 — we'll use that as the not-to-exceed for approvals.
5) Desired cost center split and GL codes for bookkeeping.

If we get the W9 and invoice from SparkEvents and confirmation from CodeCity on payment routing, I can generate a PO within 1 business day (assuming manager approval). For PO template and routing, see attached: finance_po_template.pdf

Note: For stipend payments to non-vendor partners, we typically route through Partner Grants (GL 7020) and require a business justification and internal approval from Partnerships leadership. If you prefer to have Redwood pay SparkEvents directly for all items and then treat CodeCity's stipend as waived, that simplifies the vendor flow.

Marissa Cole
Sr. Finance Manager — Procure to Pay
Redwood Inference

---
From: SparkEvents LLC <invoices@sparkevents.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Aisha Gupta <partnerships@codecity.org>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 14:18:00 -0800
Subject: SparkEvents invoice + W9 for CodeCity Hackathon

Hi Mateo / Aisha,

Attached are the invoice and W9 for SparkEvents LLC related to the CodeCity Student Hackathon on 2026-12-18.

Attachments:
- SparkEvents_invoice_992.pdf (services: setup, AV, booths; total $2,750)
- vendor_w9_sparkevents.pdf

Please let us know if you need PO number on the invoice; we can reissue with PO appended. Thanks and excited to work with Redwood on site logistics.

Best,
Liam Chen
Operations Manager
SparkEvents LLC

---
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Dec 2026 15:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Proposal — CodeCity Student Hackathon (requesting partner stipend & vendor payment)

Thanks for the docs, team.

I will open a PO for SparkEvents (estimated $2,750) and route for manager approval today. For the partner stipend to CodeCity ($2,200), we need Partnerships leadership sign-off (per policy >$1,000). Mateo — can you confirm that Partnerships (Aisha's request) has leadership approval? If not, tag the Partnerships VP (cc'ed?) or reply with the internal approval note and I'll attach it to the PO request.

Cost center split: I will allocate 4021 for SparkEvents $2,750 and 7020 for CodeCity stipend $2,200. If Finance prefers different GLs, Marissa please advise and I'll update.

Dylan Brooks
Events Operations
Redwood Inference

On Thu, Dec 2, 2026 at 10:10 AM, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Confirmed — once PO is created we'll need the approvals attached. I'll prepare the PO template for signature.
>
> -Marissa

---

Subject: Re: Proposal — CodeCity Student Hackathon (requesting partner stipend & vendor payment)

Thanks for the context, Mateo.

Procurement/Finance checklist to issue PO and process stipend:
1) Vendor paperwork: W9 for SparkEvents LLC (required). If SparkEvents is a sole proprietor/contractor, note it here.
2) Final vendor invoice (PDF) that lists services & amounts (no PO number yet is fine).
3) Clarify how the stipend should be paid to CodeCity (W9 for CodeCity if they require payment to an entity; or if it's a grant, we need the organization's payment instructions and tax status).
4) Confirm final spend cap. You mentioned $6,000 — we'll use that as the not-to-exceed for approvals.
5) Desired cost center split and GL codes for bookkeeping.

If we get the W9 and invoice from SparkEvents and confirmation from CodeCity on payment routing, I can generate a PO within 1 business day (assuming manager approval). For PO template and routing, see attached: finance_po_template.pdf

Note: For stipend payments to non-vendor partners, we typically route through Partner Grants (GL 7020) and require a business justification and internal approval from Partnerships leadership. If you prefer to have Redwood pay SparkEvents directly for all items and then treat CodeCity's stipend as waived, that simplifies the vendor flow.

Marissa Cole

Aisha Gupta
Partnerships Lead
CodeCity Foundation

source.subject: Kickoff: HealthGrid enablement — streaming, tool-calls & schema handoff
messages:
From: Samantha Cole <samantha@healthgrid.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Kickoff: HealthGrid Redwood feature enablement & schema handoff

Hi Mateo / Camila / Monica,

Thanks for agreeing to partner on this — excited to get Redwood capabilities live for HealthGrid. High level goals from our side:
- Real-time transcription & structured event outputs into HealthGrid ingestion pipeline (streaming + structured JSON).
- One function/tool-calling integration (patient lookup) triggered from model responses.
- Clear JSON schema contract + validation so our downstream ETL is stable.

Key asks for the kickoff:
1) A 30–45 minute technical kickoff next week to align on schema fields, sample payloads, and test plan.
2) A mutual action plan (milestones: schema freeze, pilot tests, go/no-go) and owners.
3) A short SOW or milestone doc we can reference for procurement.

We can be flexible on timing — Jared (cc'd) is our lead eng and will bring sample payloads. If you can propose 2 options for next Tue/Wed that works for you, we'll pick one.

Best,
Samantha Cole
Director of Integrations, HealthGrid
S: (415) 555-0192

Attachments: healthgrid-requirements-brief.pdf (see section 3 for payload examples)
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Dec 2026 11:03:45 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: HealthGrid Redwood feature enablement & schema handoff

Samantha — thanks for the clear summary. Happy to run the kickoff and take initial ownership of the mutual action plan. Proposed next steps from Redwood:

1) Quick kickoff (30 min) to align roles, acceptance criteria, and schedule. Proposed windows (PST):
   - Tue 12/08 10:00–10:30
   - Tue 12/08 15:00–15:30
   - Wed 12/09 09:00–09:45

2) Initial deliverables (we'll circulate these after kickoff):
   - HG-SOW-v1.pdf (outline milestones & acceptance criteria) [attached]
   - proposed-milestones.xlsx (timeline with owners) [attached]
   - schema-contract-template.json (internal; we'll share editable copy in Drive)

3) Engineering checklist for the pilot (short):
   - Schema mapping review & sample payloads validated against schema validators.
   - Streaming readiness test: synthetic stream with 500 messages/min to validate batching and prefix caching behavior.
   - Tool/function contract: definition for "patient_lookup" endpoint (inputs, outputs, retries, auth).
   - Monitoring hooks: token-level latency + example structured-output metrics.

Camila (CS lead) and Monica (engineer) will run the schema and tool-call mapping with your team. I can own the mutual action plan and be the primary point for scheduling.

If those slots work, I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
---
Attachments: HG-SOW-v1.pdf, proposed-milestones.xlsx
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha@healthgrid.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 10:15:10 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: HealthGrid Redwood feature enablement & schema handoff

Quick notes from CS side before we meet:

- Schema ownership: please confirm which fields are canonical on HealthGrid side vs. derived by Redwood. Example: patient_id (canonical), primary_dx (canonical), ai_confidence (derived).
- Acceptance tests we want to define in kickoff:
  * Field-level validation (required/optional, types),
  * Round-trip toolcall simulation (mock patient_lookup responses),
  * Streaming stability under realistic burst patterns (3x baseline).
- Data retention & PII handling: include a short appendix in the SOW that references the retention window and redaction expectations so procurement can review.

Also, can HealthGrid share a small set (3–5) of real-ish sample payloads (PII redacted) that exercise edge cases? We'll use those for schema validation and for the streaming pilot.

Thanks — I can lead the technical validation checklist in the pilot once we have sample payloads.

Camila Reyes
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Samantha Cole <samantha@healthgrid.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Jared Lin <jared.lin@healthgrid.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Dec 2026 13:05:22 -0800
Subject: Re: Kickoff: HealthGrid Redwood feature enablement & schema handoff

Thanks Mateo/Camila — those windows work. We’ll take Tue 12/08 10:00 PST.

Attaching a small set of sample payloads (PII redacted) and a short note on canonical fields: healthgrid-sample-payload.json (attached).

Per your ask on retention: procurement wants a one-paragraph appendix in the SOW describing logs retention, deletion flows, and access controls — I expect to circulate redlines after kickoff.

See you on 12/08 at 10:00 PST. Agenda request: 1) Introductions & roles (5m), 2) Schema review & sample payload walkthrough (10m), 3) Toolcall contract sketch (10m), 4) Milestones & next actions (5m).

Best,
Samantha Cole
Director of Integrations, HealthGrid

Attachments: healthgrid-sample-payload.json

On Tue, Dec 1, 2026 at 11:03 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Samantha — thanks for the clear summary. Happy to run the kickoff and take initial ownership of the mutual action plan. Proposed next steps from Redwood:
> 
> 1) Quick kickoff (30 min) to align roles, acceptance criteria, and schedule. Proposed windows (PST):
>    - Tue 12/08 10:00–10:30
>    - Tue 12/08 15:00–15:30
>    - Wed 12/09 09:00–09:45
> 
> 2) Initial deliverables (we'll circulate these after kickoff):
>    - HG-SOW-v1.pdf (outline milestones & acceptance criteria) [attached]
>    - proposed-milestones.xlsx (timeline with owners) [attached]
>    - schema-contract-template.json (internal; we'll share editable copy in Drive)
> 
> 3) Engineering checklist for the pilot (short):
>    - Schema mapping review & sample payloads validated against schema validators.
>    - Streaming readiness test: synthetic stream with 500 messages/min to validate batching and prefix caching behavior.
>    - Tool/function contract: definition for "patient_lookup" endpoint (inputs, outputs, retries, auth).
>    - Monitoring hooks: token-level latency + example structured-output metrics.
> 
> Camila (CS lead) and Monica (engineer) will run the schema and tool-call mapping with your team. I can own the mutual action plan and be the primary point for scheduling.
> 
> If those slots work, I'll send a calendar invite and a short agenda.
>

> Mateo Alvarez

See you on 12/08 at 10:00 PST. Agenda request: 1) Introductions & roles (5m), 2) Schema review & sample payload walkthrough (10m), 3) Toolcall contract sketch (10m), 4) Milestones & next actions (5m).

Best,
Samantha Cole
Director of Integrations, HealthGrid

Attachments: healthgrid-sample-payload.json

source.subject: Spend snapshot & pilot outline — next steps
source.thread:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia@nimbuspay.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 17:12:00 -0800
Subject: Spend snapshot & pilot outline — next steps

Alicia — thanks again for the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum. I copied Monica and Ben so we can move the sizing and scope work in parallel.

Key pain points you confirmed:
- Monthly spend is volatile and concentrated in chat (peak spikes from customer support flows).
- Many short-turn messages (~20–60 tokens) lead to low batching; current infra routes each message individually.
- No persistent prefix/KV caching for multi-turn sessions, so repeated context is sent every request.
- Budget constraint: finance has set a soft cap of ~$60k/month for inference (we discussed this on the call).

Goals you stated:
- Reduce inference unit cost by ~30–40% while keeping p50 latency under ~300ms for chat paths.
- Run a quick proof (representative subset of traffic) to validate savings and GA-ready latency.
- Keep data in US region and preserve current retention/audit requirements.

Redwood levers we recommend testing in the pilot:
- Continuous batching (pack short messages into micro-batches) — expected 20–35% token cost improvement on chat-heavy workloads.
- Prefix/KV caching for repeated context (support and onboarding flows) — can avoid re-sending the same system prompts and reduce effective tokens by another 10–25% on cached paths.
- Model mix/routing: route non-sensitive or low-precision queries to a quantized 7B variant and reserve the 13B/70B for high-fidelity paths (cost delta we estimate in the attached).
- 8-bit quantization and kernel selection for your chosen GPU footprint to lower per-token GPU cost without materially changing latency.

Proposed pilot (outcomes-driven, 30 days) — minimal ops lift:
- Duration: 30 days (2 weeks instrument/rollout + 2 weeks steady-state).
- Scope: mirror ~20% of live chat traffic (representative endpoints we identify together).
- Metrics: baseline & post-change cost per 1k messages, p50/p95 latency, error rate, percentage of requests served from cache.
- Acceptance: >=30% cost savings vs baseline OR per-message cost <= $0.XX (we will refine once we see sample logs).

Immediate asks from your team to size the pilot accurately:
1) Anonymized sample logs for 7 consecutive days (timestamps, prompt length, reply length, endpoint tags). CSV is fine.
2) Confirmation of monthly spend cap ($60k) and any billing constraints (PO / procurement cadence).
3) Any guarded API/node URLs or feature flags we should respect (SSO/NDA notes).

Attachments: I attached a short pilot scope + a sample-format spreadsheet for logs.

Next steps:
- If you agree, we’ll send a 1-page scope + HubSpot deal record and a small SOW for procurement (Ben will help on pricing).
- Timeline: if we get logs + PO window within 10 business days, we can start instrumenting within 2 weeks.

Thanks — happy to jump on another 20–30 minute call to align with finance/infra if helpful.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference

---
From: Alicia Gomez <alicia@nimbuspay.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 09:08:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Spend snapshot & pilot outline — next steps

Thanks Mateo — that recap is helpful. A few quick clarifications from the finance side:
- Confirming the $60k/month cap for inference ops (we may have a short runway to go to $75k if we show clear ROI).
- They want a conservative savings estimate for the first 30 days (not optimistic). Can you provide a conservative and upside view?

Operational Qs:
- We can share anonymized logs; is the sample format you attached sufficient? We’ll scrub PII and send CSV next week.
- Procurement asks: we need a short SOW and an estimate on what a 30-day pilot will cost us (line item: Redwood services + infra delta). Is there a standard template?

I’m available Wed afternoon for a follow-up with our head of finance (if Mateo/Ben can join).

—
Alicia Gomez
Head of Product Operations | NimbusPay
alicia@nimbuspay.com | +1 415-555-0198

---
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Dec 2026 10:30:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Spend snapshot & pilot outline — next steps

Agree with the outline. I’ll draft a conservative vs upside savings table based on three scenarios: no batching/no cache (baseline), batching only, batching + cache + model mix.
Attaching a one-pager cost-op summary we can send to procurement (PDF). I’ll also populate a rough pilot budget: Redwood services + infra delta + instrumentation (line items).

Ben
Sr. Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachment: cost-op-onepager.pdf

---
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <alicia@nimbuspay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 2 Dec 2026 09:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Spend snapshot & pilot outline — next steps

Alicia — thanks for the fast reply and for lining up finance. Short answers:
- Conservative estimate (30-day pilot): 18–25% cost reduction vs baseline when we enable batching + modest prefix caching (assumes 20% of chat traffic is cacheable).
- Upside: 35–45% with full batching + broader caching + model mix routing. Ben’s one-pager (attached) includes scenario math and a simple per-1k-message cost table.
- Pilot cost template: we will send a short SOW + line-item budget (Redwood services: engineering + monitoring + support; infra delta: quantization + GPU hours). Ben will attach the budget in HubSpot and I’ll send the DocuSign link for the pilot SOW.

We’ve attached:
- redwood-30d-pilot-scope.pdf — scope, acceptance criteria, timeline.
- nimbuspay-sample-format.xlsx — example CSV columns for logs.
- cost-op-onepager.pdf — our conservative/upside scenario math.

Related links: HubSpot deal and shared Drive folder above (drive link). I’ll put a short SOW in DocuSign pending your procurement review.

Proposed next meeting: Wednesday 2026-12-08 10:00am PT with your head of finance — does that work?

If you send the sample logs by EOD next Tue (2026-12-07) we’ll pre-populate the model and give you an updated conservative projection before the meeting.

Best,
Mateo
Senior Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-30d-pilot-scope.pdf, nimbuspay-sample-format.xlsx, cost-op-onepager.pdf

source.subject: Post-discovery: schedule engineering handoff & demo (60-90m)
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Rachael Torres <rachael.torres@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0500
Subject: Post-discovery: schedule engineering handoff & demo (60-90m)

Hi Rachael — great to meet earlier today. Quick recap from our discovery and a proposed next step so we can align infra/ops teams for a hands-on engineering run through.

Highlights from today's conversation:
- Goal: validate latency / cost profile on your typical inference workloads and confirm integration pattern for inference-in-VPC.
- Success criteria: reproduce a representative endpoint run, confirm any network egress constraints, and capture an initial ops checklist (SLOs, logging, key rotation).
- Participants we recommend for the next session: your infra/devops engineer + the product owner for the feature; on our side: Marissa (solutions eng) + Sergio (sales engineer) + me.

Proposed next step: 60–90 minute engineering handoff / demo where we run a short proof-of-behavior with a sample workload and walk through the deployment options. Please pick one of these slots (all ET):
- Tue, Dec 1 — 11:00–12:15 ET
- Wed, Dec 2 — 10:00–11:30 ET
- Mon, Dec 6 — 10:00–11:15 ET

I've attached a proposed agenda (high-level) and included a link to a short pre-read that covers network requirements and the basic deployment patterns. If those times don't work, tell me a couple alternatives and we'll accommodate. Also please confirm the right person at Helix to include for the hands-on items (stack access, sample dataset availability).

Attachment: redwood-proposed-agenda.pdf (1 page – high-level topics + checklist)

Thanks — Mateo

source.subject: Evidence request: quarterly vulnerability cadence + CVE SLA confirmation
messages:
From: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@blueridge.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: security_ops@blueridge.com
Date: 2026-11-29T09:23:00-08:00
Subject: SOC2 evidence request: VM cadence and CVE SLA details

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. As part of our SOC 2 scoping and SIG/CAIQ review, our compliance team has a couple of targeted asks around vulnerability management to close our control evidence:

- Confirm your authenticated scanning cadence (tool + frequency) and whether you perform both infrastructure and dependency (SCA) scans.
- Provide your SLA/commitment for CVE remediation by severity (we typically ask for P1/P2/P3 timelines).
- A short attestation that you have a documented patching/exception process and a sample recent scan index (csv or similar) covering the last 90 days.
- Any exceptions or compensating controls you rely on (e.g., WAF, network isolation).

We'd appreciate a concise packet for our auditor — a single PDF and a CSV index is ideal. Also if you can call out the most recent critical/urgent CVE you tracked and the closure date (we are referencing CVE-2026-4821 in our questionnaire).

Can you get this over by EOD PT tomorrow? Thanks — we have a tight window for the SOC2 portal ingestion.

Best,
Eleanor
Eleanor Shaw
Senior InfoSec Program Manager
BlueRidge Financial
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@blueridge.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T10:04:00-08:00
Subject: RE: SOC2 evidence request: VM cadence and CVE SLA details

All — looping you in. BlueRidge needs a compact VM evidence packet for SOC2/SIG. They asked for: authenticated scan cadence, SCA cadence, CVE timelines by severity, a 90-day scan index csv, and an attestation mentioning our exception handling.

Proposed split of work:
- Monica: assemble scan-index.csv (include scanner name, scan_id, date, scope, top findings, remediation owner and closure date) and the sample authenticated scan metadata.
- Stephanie: draft the formal SLA attestation paragraph and list of compensating controls we rely on (network isolation, private clusters, runtime policies).
- Kimberly: export the recent CVE tracker for the last 90 days and create the short closure timeline for CVE-2026-4821 (owner, detection date, fix PR, prod deployment date).

We use AstraScan v3.2 for authenticated infra scans (weekly) and Snyk Enterprise for SCA (nightly SBOM checks + weekly full SCA). Our operating SLA for CVEs is the usual: P1 (7 days), P2 (30 days), P3 (90 days) unless security review escalates to an exception — but let's formalize that in Stephanie's draft.

Target: send BlueRidge a zipped packet (PDF + CSV) before EOD tomorrow. If anyone needs more time, call it out now.

—Mateo
Senior Security Partner
Redwood Inference
From: Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-29T14:18:00-08:00
Subject: RE: SOC2 evidence request: VM cadence and CVE SLA details

Quick progress update and attached stubs (see attachment placeholders):

Attachment stubs included: vm-scan-index.csv, cve-closure-plan.pdf, auth-scan-credentials-guide.txt

Summary (to be copied into the final PDF):
- Scanner profile: AstraScan v3.2 (authenticated agent scans on jump-hosts with role-scoped creds) — weekly infra scans covering prod, staging, and admin VPCs.
- SCA: Snyk Enterprise, nightly SBOM scan triggered on CI + weekly full dependency inventory for all repo tags.
- Detection/alerting: Findings are triaged in JIRA (SEC-xxx) with automatic ticket creation for critical vulns. Remediation owners are auto-assigned based on service ownership map.
- Recent notable CVE: CVE-2026-4821 (kernel privilege escalation). Detected by AstraScan on 2026-10-14, owner assigned 2026-10-15, patch PR merged 2026-10-20, rollout completed to prod 2026-10-22. Closure evidence: PR link + deployment tag included in CSV.

Proposed SLA table (draft):
- P1 (Critical): remediation within 7 calendar days; emergency rollback plan if patch not available.
- P2 (High): remediation within 30 calendar days.
- P3 (Medium): remediation within 90 calendar days.
- Exceptions: documented exceptions require Security approval and compensating controls (WAF rules, host isolation, temporary egress deny).

I also included a short index mapping for the CSV columns. If Stephanie can draft the attestation paragraph, I will assemble into the PDF and attach to the response to BlueRidge.

—Monica
Security Operations Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@blueridge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-30T14:12:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: RE: SOC2 evidence request: VM cadence and CVE SLA details

Eleanor — attaching our VM evidence packet for the SOC2 upload (attached: cve-closure-plan.pdf, vm-scan-index.csv). Summary below; full packet is in the attached PDF and the CSV index.

Summary attestation (signed by Redwood Security):
Redwood Inference attests that we operate a documented vulnerability management program incorporating weekly authenticated infrastructure scans (AstraScan v3.2), nightly SCA/SBOM checks (Snyk Enterprise), and a triage and remediation workflow that maps findings to service owners. Our remediation SLAs are P1: 7 days, P2: 30 days, P3: 90 days; exceptions are documented and require Security approval. The packet includes the 90-day scan index and an evidence timeline for CVE-2026-4821.

Attachments (referenced):
- cve-closure-plan.pdf (signed attestation + compensating control list)
- vm-scan-index.csv (scan_id, scanner, date, scope, top_findings, owner, closure_date, evidence_link)
- auth-scan-credentials-guide.txt (notes for auditor on authenticated scan setup; redacted credentials omitted)

Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2B3C-scan-evidence

If you need a short letter on company letterhead for the auditor or an in-call to walk through the packet, happy to schedule.

Best regards,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Security Partner, Redwood Inference
On Mon, 2026-11-29 at 09:23, Eleanor Shaw <eleanor@blueridge.com> wrote:
> Hi Mateo,
> 
> Thanks for the quick intro last week. As part of our SOC 2 scoping and SIG/CAIQ review, our compliance team has a couple of targeted asks around vulnerability management to close our control evidence:
> 
> - Confirm your authenticated scanning cadence (tool + frequency) and whether you perform both infrastructure and dependency (SCA) scans.
> - Provide your SLA/commitment for CVE remediation by severity (we typically ask for P1/P2/P3 timelines).
> - A short attestation that you have a documented patching/exception process and a sample recent scan index (csv or similar) covering the last 90 days.
> - Any exceptions or compensating controls you rely on (e.g., WAF, network isolation).
> 
> We'd appreciate a concise packet for our auditor — a single PDF and a CSV index is ideal. Also if you can call out the most recent critical/urgent CVE you tracked and the closure date (we are referencing CVE-2026-4821 in our questionnaire).
> 
> Can you get this over by EOD PT tomorrow? Thanks — we have a tight window for the SOC2 portal ingestion.
> 
> Best,
> Eleanor
> Senior InfoSec Program Manager
> BlueRidge Financial

- Detection/alerting: Findings are triaged in JIRA (SEC-xxx) with automatic ticket creation for critical vulns. Remediation owners are auto-assigned based on service ownership map.
- Recent notable CVE: CVE-2026-4821 (kernel privilege escalation). Detected by AstraScan on 2026-10-14, owner assigned 2026-10-15, patch PR merged 2026-10-20, rollout completed to prod 2026-10-22. Closure evidence: PR link + deployment tag included in CSV.

Proposed SLA table (draft):
- P1 (Critical): remediation within 7 calendar days; emergency rollback plan if patch not available.
- P2 (High): remediation within 30 calendar days.
- P3 (Medium): remediation within 90 calendar days.
- Exceptions: documented exceptions require Security approval and compensating controls (WAF rules, host isolation, temporary egress deny).

I also included a short index mapping for the CSV columns. If Stephanie can draft the attestation paragraph, I will assemble into the PDF and attach to the response to BlueRidge.

—Monica

Redwood Inference

Date: 2026-11-29T09:23:00-08:00
Subject: SOC2 evidence request: VM cadence and CVE SLA details

Hi Mateo,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. As part of our SOC 2 scoping and SIG/CAIQ review, our compliance team has a couple of targeted asks around vulnerability management to close our control evidence:

- Confirm your authenticated scanning cadence (tool + frequency) and whether you perform both infrastructure and dependency (SCA) scans.
- Provide your SLA/commitment for CVE remediation by severity (we typically ask for P1/P2/P3 timelines).
- A short attestation that you have a documented patching/exception process and a sample recent scan index (csv or similar) covering the last 90 days.
- Any exceptions or compensating controls you rely on (e.g., WAF, network isolation).

We'd appreciate a concise packet for our auditor — a single PDF and a CSV index is ideal. Also if you can call out the most recent critical/urgent CVE you tracked and the closure date (we are referencing CVE-2026-4821 in our questionnaire).

Can you get this over by EOD PT tomorrow? Thanks — we have a tight window for the SOC2 portal ingestion.

Best,
Eleanor
Eleanor Shaw
Senior InfoSec Program Manager
BlueRidge Financial

Target: send BlueRidge a zipped packet (PDF + CSV) before EOD tomorrow. If anyone needs more time, call it out now.

source.subject: VAT reclassification + credit note request — BrightBridge November invoice
messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@brightbridge.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-24T09:18:00-08:00
Subject: VAT on Nov invoice — request for credit note

Hi Mateo,

Hope you're well. Our AP team reviewed the invoice you sent for November (attached) and noticed VAT was charged at 20% as a sales tax rather than being zero-rated under our EU VAT registration. We are registered in Ireland and provided our VAT ID during procurement, so VAT should not have been collected.

Can Redwood issue a credit note for the VAT portion on INV-20261101 so our AP can reprocess the payment? AP has asked for a formal credit note with the original invoice reference, credit note number, and clear wording that VAT is reversed due to EU intra-community supply.

Also: our team flagged a small duplicate metering event that caused ~€1,120 in extra usage on that invoice. Would Redwood consider a goodwill adjustment for the overcharge while you reclassify VAT? If so, please confirm whether the credit note can include both the VAT reversal and the goodwill amount, or whether you prefer separate documents.

I've attached the invoice for reference. AP contact: BrightBridge AP <ap@brightbridge.com>.

Thanks — Elena

Attachments: INV-20261101-BB.pdf (application/pdf, 78KB)
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@brightbridge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-24T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: VAT on Nov invoice — request for credit note

Hi Elena — thanks for the heads-up and for attaching the invoice. Apologies for the mix-up on VAT and for the duplicate metering hit — I can see how that would be disruptive for your month-end.

Looping in Monica from Finance and Marissa from Sales Ops to confirm process:
- Monica: can Finance issue a single credit note that covers the VAT reversal (explicit reference to INV-20261101) and a goodwill line for the duplicate usage? Is there any compliance/legal reason to keep them separate?
- Marissa: if we can include the goodwill credit, do we need commercial approval or is this covered under our standard goodwill policy for meter errors?

Elena — happy to coordinate timing so AP can receive a PDF-ready credit note and instructions for re-submitting or receiving a refund.

Best,
Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T08:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: VAT on Nov invoice — request for credit note

Thanks Mateo — internal note for the record: BrightBridge (BB) provided a valid EU VAT ID on 2026-09-03 and should have been zero-rated. Finance will need: original invoice number (INV-20261101), the VAT amount to reverse, and the customer's VAT ID (Elena included earlier).

Proposal: issue one credit note that contains two line items — (1) VAT reversal (amount: EUR 14,400 * 20% = EUR 2,880) with legal wording referencing intra-community supply and the customer's VAT ID, and (2) goodwill adjustment for duplicate metering: EUR 1,120. Total credit note: EUR 4,000.

Reasons to combine: single document is easier for AP to apply and reduces reconciliation friction. Risks: tax teams sometimes prefer separate fiscal documents for VAT vs commercial credits depending on local filing. I'll mark the credit note as a VAT-correcting document and include the supporting audit note.

Next steps for me: draft the credit note PDF and share a DRAFT for your and Elena's review; confirm whether BrightBridge wants the credit applied as an account credit or refunded to the original payment method (they told us they were close to payment run).

Attachments: DRAFT-CREDITNOTE-BB-202611.pdf (created by Finance, draft).

Monica
Senior Finance Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: VAT on Nov invoice — request for credit note

Agree with Monica — combine into one credit note that calls out the VAT reversal and the goodwill adjustment. Commercial approval: I approve up to EUR 5k in goodwill for production-impact meter errors; this is within threshold. Please include the following in the final credit note: original invoice ID, credit note ID, a short explanation of the duplicate metering bug and the date it occurred (2026-10-29), and our contact for any audit questions (monica@redwood.com).

Recommend we offer two options to BB in the final email: (A) refund of the EUR 4,000 to their original payment method (preferred for quick closure) or (B) post as a credit to their Redwood account to offset the next invoice. Ask Elena which they prefer; we should not auto-select.

Marissa
Head of Sales Ops, Redwood Inference

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@brightbridge.com>, BrightBridge AP <ap@brightbridge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T16:32:00-08:00
Subject: Credit note issued for INV-20261101 — action items for AP

Hi Elena / BrightBridge AP,

Thanks for your patience. Finance has issued a formal credit note that addresses both the VAT reclassification and the goodwill adjustment for the duplicate metering event. Details below and PDF attached (CREDITNOTE-BB-202611.pdf).

Credit note summary:
- Credit note ID: RN-2026-11-0042
- References: Original invoice INV-20261101
- VAT reversal line: EUR 2,880 (20% of taxable base); wording: "VAT reversed due to intra-community supply — customer VAT ID IE12345678"
- Goodwill adjustment: EUR 1,120 (duplicate metering correction)
- Total credit: EUR 4,000

Action for AP:
1) If you would like a refund to the original payment method, please confirm the bank/merchant details to which you'd like the funds returned (BrightBridge AP <ap@brightbridge.com>).
2) If you prefer the credit to sit on the BrightBridge Redwood account, let us know and we will apply it to the next invoice.

Attachments included: CREDITNOTE-BB-202611.pdf (application/pdf, 32KB), AP_Instructions.pdf (application/pdf, 8KB) — AP_Instructions contains the refund flow, bank confirmation template, and the HubSpot record ID for the transaction review.

If AP needs a separate VAT-only fiscal document for local filings, we can generate an additional VAT-statement, but from a reconciliation standpoint one combined credit note with clear VAT wording should be suitable. Monica is copied for any tax follow-ups.

Would you prefer refund or account credit? If refund, please confirm beneficiary details and target currency.

Thanks — Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Please include the following in the final credit note: original invoice ID, credit note ID, a short explanation of the duplicate metering bug and the date it occurred (2026-10-29), and our contact for any audit questions (monica@redwood.com).

Recommend we offer two options to BB in the final email: (A) refund of the EUR 4,000 to their original payment method (preferred for quick closure) or (B) post as a credit to their Redwood account to offset the next invoice. Ask Elena which they prefer; we should not auto-select.

Marissa
Head of Sales Ops, Redwood Inference



From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@brightbridge.com>, BrightBridge AP <ap@brightbridge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-28T16:32:00-08:00
Subject: Credit note issued for INV-20261101 — action items for AP

Hi Elena / BrightBridge AP,

Thanks for your patience. Finance has issued a formal credit note that addresses both the VAT reclassification and the goodwill adjustment for the duplicate metering event. Details below and PDF attached (CREDITNOTE-BB-202611.pdf).

Credit note summary:
- Credit note ID: RN-2026-11-0042
- References: Original invoice INV-20261101
- VAT reversal line: EUR 2,880 (20% of taxable base); wording: "VAT reversed due to intra-community supply — customer VAT ID IE12345678"
- Goodwill adjustment: EUR 1,120 (duplicate metering correction)
- Total credit: EUR 4,000

Action for AP:
1) If you would like a refund to the original payment method, please confirm the bank/merchant details to which you'd like the funds returned (BrightBridge AP <ap@brightbridge.com>).
2) If you prefer the credit to sit on the BrightBridge Redwood account, let us know and we will apply it to the next invoice.

Attachments included: CREDITNOTE-BB-202611.pdf (application/pdf, 32KB), AP_Instructions.pdf (application/pdf, 8KB) — AP_Instructions contains the refund flow, bank confirmation template, and the HubSpot record ID for the transaction review.

If AP needs a separate VAT-only fiscal document for local filings, we can generate an additional VAT-statement, but from a reconciliation standpoint one combined credit note with clear VAT wording should be suitable. Monica is copied for any tax follow-ups.

Would you prefer refund or account credit? If refund, please confirm beneficiary details and target currency.

Thanks — Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Scope bump: security + procurement joining — need 2-hour demo block
messages:
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@clearline.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Natalie Cruz <natalie.cruz@clearline.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo — proposed Nov 30th at 10am PT

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the quick turnaround on the deck. 10am PT on Nov 30th works for product and a couple of our engineers.  

One quick heads-up — procurement asked to join the call to review our contract timeline, and security wants to do a short architecture checklist the same day. They said they "might need a longer slot" if we plan to walk through infra diagrams. Can we make the session 90–120 minutes instead of 45? If so we can confirm with travel plans.

Also looping Natalie (procurement) who will coordinate internal approvals.

Thanks,
Alex
---
Alex Turner
Director of Platform Partnerships
Clearline Systems
A: 312-555-0142

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-20T10:04:00-08:00
Subject: FW: Redwood demo — proposed Nov 30th at 10am PT

Team — forwarding Clearline. They added procurement + security and are asking for a 90–120 minute slot. Travel still in play for a few folks. Quick sync: can we accommodate a two-hour session and split into 1) 45-minute product walkthrough + 2) 60–75 minute deep-dive for infra/security/procurement?

Wishlist for the deep-dive:
- Vivek: high-level routing and VPC/private deployment architecture (20m)
- Priyom: SLA/commit model and dedicated capacity options (15m)
- Marissa: procurement/MAP items and contract cadence (15m)
- Security Q&A (we should prepare the SOC2 evidence path + data retention summary)

Attachments: mutual_action_plan_v3.xlsx (draft we can share if they want), security_contact_list.pdf (for contact routing).

If two hours is tight, propose we split into a day-of overview and a follow-up security workshop next week. Thoughts?

-Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference

From: Natalie Cruz <natalie.cruz@clearline.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@clearline.com>
Cc: "Clearline Systems - procurement" <procurement@clearline.com>, "Clearline Systems - security" <security@clearline.com>
Date: 2026-11-22T08:33:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo — proposed Nov 30th at 10am PT

Thanks Mateo — appreciate the suggested agenda. Procurement needs to see the MAP and proposed procurement timeline during the call (we're trying to align with our quarter close). Security would like the infra diagrams in advance so they can triage questions; otherwise the workshop can run long.

Would you be able to move to Nov 30th, 1:00pm PT — that gives our team time after travel — and reserve 2 hours? If not, the week of Dec 6th also works but we'd prefer to keep it in the week of the 30th if possible.

Also please add the following security contacts to the invite: Julian Park (julian.park@clearline.com) and Mina Saleh (mina.saleh@clearline.com).

-Natalie
Natalie Cruz
Head of Procurement
Clearline Systems

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@clearline.com>, Natalie Cruz <natalie.cruz@clearline.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-27T15:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo — proposed Nov 30th at 10am PT

Alex/Natalie — thanks for the clarity and the heads-up on travel. We can absolutely reserve two hours on Nov 30th at 1:00pm PT and will adjust the agenda to prioritize procurement + security in the second hour.

Plan for Nov 30th (90–120m):
- 1:00–1:30pm PT: Product overview + narrow use-case demo (Marissa)
- 1:30–2:10pm PT: Infra & private deployment architecture (Vivek)
- 2:10–2:30pm PT: Procurement/MAP & pricing pathways (Priyom + Marissa)
- 2:30–2:45pm PT: Security Q&A + next steps (Evelyn)

Action items for us before the call:
- Share updated MAP and a short infra diagram PDF by EOD Nov 29th (Marissa, Vivek) — will attach to the calendar invite.
- Pre-send security contact list to Clearline (Evelyn) — security asked to triage in advance.
- Draft follow-up workshop plan in case security needs a deeper review (Priyom).

Natalie — I'll send a calendar invite for Nov 30th, 1–3pm PT and include the attachments. If your security folks prefer a separate workshop, we can book that for Dec 3rd or 7th; flag preferred dates and we'll hold a slot.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

-Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
T: +1 415-555-0198

source.subject: W-8BEN-E signatory confirmation and PO routing for Bluepoint Europe
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Bluepoint Finance <finance@bluepoint.example.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-24T14:12:00-05:00
Subject: Remote signatory confirmation for tax form and PO kickoff

Hi Lena / Bluepoint team —

Thanks for moving quickly on onboarding. We’re starting the PO setup on our side but have a couple of items to clarify before Procurement can finalize:

1) Form type: you submitted a W-8BEN-E indicating Bluepoint Europe Ltd is a foreign entity. That matches our classification, but the signature block is executed by an AP manager ("A. Peters, AP Manager") rather than a company officer.
2) TIN mapping: the TIN on the form appears to be the UK UTR. For our tax team we need a clear mapping between the corporate legal name on the W-8BEN-E and the tax identifier used for payments.

Can you confirm:
- Who is authorized to sign on behalf of Bluepoint Europe Ltd (title and corporate role)?
- If the signer is delegated, can you provide a signatory authorization letter or a bank letter confirming the account owner and the authorized signatory?

If live notarization or an e-notary/video-notary is easiest for your team, we can accept that as evidence of authority (finance and legal will accept e-notary + bank letter). If you prefer, we can also accept a notarized authorization PDF signed by an officer.

Attachments (for your reference): none on this message.

Next steps: once we have signatory evidence + the completed W-8BEN-E, Procurement can create a provisional PO (we typically issue a PO in ‘provisional’ status with a funds hold until legal/tax clearance completes).

Thanks — copying Mateo (eng lead for this contract) and Kimberly (procurement) to coordinate the PO window.

—Jordan
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
From: Lena Sorensen <lena.s@bluepoint.example.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Bluepoint Finance <finance@bluepoint.example.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-24T17:03:00+00:00
Subject: Re: Remote signatory confirmation for tax form and PO kickoff

Hi Jordan / team —

Thanks for the note. Apologies for the confusion — Bluepoint Europe Ltd is the UK-incorporated entity; the W-8BEN-E we uploaded was completed by our AP team during onboarding (they handled the data entry). The authorized signatory per our corporate records is the CFO, Marcus Hale.

What we can provide now:
- Attached: W-8BEN-E_Bluepoint_20261124.pdf (filled and signed by AP Manager A. Peters) — submitted to accelerate onboarding.
- Attached: Bluepoint_Bank_Letter_20261124.pdf — bank letter confirming account ownership (signed by bank relationship manager).
- We can produce either: an officer-signed W-8BEN-E (CFO signature) OR a separate signatory authorization letter signed by the CFO delegating authority to A. Peters for onboarding activities.

We prefer to do an e-notary/video-notary session for the authorization letter to avoid shipping originals. If Redwood can provide the e-notary instructions we will schedule a session this week.

Also: please confirm whether Redwood requires the W-8BEN-E to be resigned by the CFO, or if the authorization letter + bank letter will suffice for Procurement to provisionally create the PO.

Thanks — I’ll await the instructions.

Best,
Lena
Operations Manager
Bluepoint Europe Ltd

Attachments: W-8BEN-E_Bluepoint_20261124.pdf (application/pdf, 245KB), Bluepoint_Bank_Letter_20261124.pdf (application/pdf, 180KB)
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Lena Sorensen <lena.s@bluepoint.example.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-25T09:07:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Remote signatory confirmation for tax form and PO kickoff

Hi Lena —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and for attaching the bank letter. To keep this moving with minimal friction, Procurement and Tax are comfortable with either:

A) A re-signed W-8BEN-E by the CFO (Marcus Hale) — straightforward, we then map the TIN and release the PO; or
B) An officer-signed delegation (signed by Marcus Hale) that explicitly authorizes A. Peters to sign onboarding tax forms + a recorded e-notary/video-notary session for the delegation document, plus the bank letter you provided.

If you pick option B we will accept the following evidence package:
- Delegation letter (PDF) signed by CFO Marcus Hale.
- Video-notary recording or e-notary audit trail (link or short certificate).
- The bank letter confirming account ownership (already attached).

Operationally: Procurement can create a provisional PO and set payment terms (Net-30) but will keep the PO in provisional status (payment hold) until Tax confirms the TIN mapping. That lets your team start work/ordering while we finish compliance checks. If that works, please:
- Upload the delegation PDF to the DocuSign/eNotary flow (I’m including eNotary instructions in an attachment).
- Reply with the preferred date/time for a 15-minute session (we can do a midday slot UTC+0 or morning UK time).

DocuSign/eNotary link: https://redwood-e-notary.example.com/session-request (instructions in eNotary_Instructions_Link.txt)

I’ll draft the provisional PO (PO_Draft_20261126.pdf) once Kimberly confirms the PO terms.

—Mateo
Senior Integration Lead, Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Lena Sorensen <lena.s@bluepoint.example.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-26T10:03:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Remote signatory confirmation for tax form and PO kickoff

Lena / team —

Confirmed on our side: Procurement will issue a provisional PO with the following conditions:
- PO will be created as provisional (PO_Draft_20261126.pdf) and routed to Bluepoint with a funds hold.
- Finance will release the PO to active status once Tax confirms TIN mapping to the legal entity and we receive one of the accepted evidence packages (CFO-signed W-8BEN-E OR CFO-signed delegation + e-notary audit trail + bank letter).

Practical items:
- If you choose the delegation path, please upload the delegation PDF to the eNotary link Mateo provided and paste the audit trail URL in reply. The audit trail can be a short text file or a document reference; it just needs to show signer, timestamp, and verification method.
- Please confirm billing address and legal name exactly as you want them to appear on the PO (we’ll mirror the legal name field to the tax mapping).

Once we see the e-notary audit trail and the bank letter, we can move the PO from provisional to active within one business day (Tax review permitting).

Thanks — I’ll attach the draft PO to this thread for review.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: PO_Draft_20261126.pdf (application/pdf, 54KB), eNotary_Instructions_Link.txt (text/plain, 3KB)
