source.subject: Renewal proposal: bridging credit runway + tier harmonization for NovaScale
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.k@novascale.ai>, Procurement Team <procure@novascale.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Renewal scoping: bridging runway + staged per-token ladder

Hi Aisha / NovaScale procurement,

Thanks again for the time earlier this week. Per our conversation I pulled together a pragmatic approach to the renewal that aims to: (1) rightsize the committed volume to NovaScale's trailing 6-month run-rate, (2) protect you from cliff effects by introducing a short-term bridging credit, and (3) phase a per-token discount ladder tied to usage bands across the renewal year.

High-level proposal (details attached):
- Yearly commit: 120M tokens (reflects recent cadence + 10% buffer)
- Bridging credit: $60,000 applied against the first quarter to smooth historical over-commit exposure
- Staged per-token ladder: 0-10M tokens at $0.0009, 10-50M at $0.00075, 50M+ at $0.00060 (banded pricing to reduce step risk)
- Renewal uplift cap: 10% year-over-year seat/commit increase without re-negotiation; larger re-tiers handled via staged true-up cadence

Attached: NOVA_MA_plan_v1.pdf (proposal) and usage_history_novascale.csv (our ingestion of last 12 months).

Ask: can you confirm whether procurement needs the bridging credit to be amortized monthly on your PO, or booked as an up-front credit line? Also, what internal SLA / data residency language will your legal team need included for Private deployment questions?

If this looks directionally correct I can circulate a short mutual action plan and a redlineable contract link.

Thanks,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez | Account Executive | Redwood Inference
M: +1 415-555-0192
[Attachment: NOVA_MA_plan_v1.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: usage_history_novascale.csv (CSV)]

From: Aisha Khan <aisha.k@novascale.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@novascale.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal scoping: bridging runway + staged per-token ladder

Hi Mateo — thanks for the clear write-up. A few quick clarifying questions: 
1) That bridging credit — is it a one-time credit we can apply to the first invoice, or do you prefer amortizing across Q1? Procurement prefers amortized credits for auditability but we can be flexible.
2) The ladder bands look reasonable; can you confirm the per-token price if we commit to a 24-month term instead of 12?
3) Our legal asked whether the renewal would carry forward existing SLA language or if Redwood intends to revise the uptime targets for higher commits.

I'll loop Joe (legal) on the next message; procurement would prefer a clean DocuSign flow once we land on numbers.

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Khan | Head of Platform | NovaScale
A: aisha.k@novascale.ai
[Procurement tracker reference: NS-PROC-1120]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-19T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: FW: Renewal scoping: bridging runway + staged per-token ladder — internal align

Lauren / Aditya — forwarding Aisha's questions. Quick internal alignment note so we respond with a clean offer: 

- Bridging credit: propose $60k amortized monthly across Q1 on their PO to meet auditability ask. This protects their cash flow and avoids an awkward one-off credit entry.
- 24-month term uplift: offer an additional 5% discount on per-token bands if they sign 24 months (i.e., multiply the band prices by 0.95) — this keeps us competitive but caps long-term exposure.
- SLA: keep existing SLA language for Hosted API, but provide a one-page addendum that documents availability targets for their dedicated capacity should they opt to migrate. That addendum is non-economic and should placate legal.
- Uplift cap: keep 10% YOY auto-raise guardrail; above that triggers a mid-term true-up and potential re-tier.

I've attached a quick calc showing how the amortized bridging credit moves the effective first-quarter net price (bridging_credit_calc.xlsx). If this is approved I'll draft the DocuSign and route to Lauren for sign-off.

Cheers,
Mateo
[Attachment: bridging_credit_calc.xlsx]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.k@novascale.ai>, Joe Lin <joe.lin@novascale.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procure@novascale.ai>
Date: 2026-11-19T13:45:00-08:00
Subject: Updated: NovaScale renewal — amortized bridging credit + 24mo option

Hi Aisha / Joe — following internal alignment, here's an updated, actionable offer so procurement/legal can review: 

1) Commit & term options:
   - 12-mo: 120M tokens commit; banded per-token pricing as previously shared (0.0009 / 0.00075 / 0.0006)
   - 24-mo: 120M tokens commit; 5% reduction on each band (applied to per-token rates)

2) Bridging credit:
   - $60,000 credit amortized across Q1 invoices (applies against usage invoices until exhausted)

3) Price protections and governance:
   - 10% annual uplift cap for automatic roll-forward. Any requested committed increase beyond 10% triggers an agreed true-up process and may require model variant mapping for capacity.
   - Quarterly usage review between Redwood & NovaScale (mutual action plan to avoid surprises)

4) Next steps:
   - If procurement accepts amortized credit, we'll generate a DocuSign with the selected term and the amortization line item called out.
   - Legal: we've drafted a 1-page SLA addendum for Dedicated/Private options; confirm if you want it attached to the current renewal or on an as-needed basis.

Attached updated calc and proposed MPA language in the PDF. Please advise on preferred term (12 vs 24) and whether procurement wants the credit listed as an invoice line or a separate credit memo.

Thanks,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez | Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: bridging_credit_calc.xlsx]
[Attachment: NOVA_MA_plan_v1.pdf]

From: Joe Lin <joe.lin@novascale.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Aisha Khan <aisha.k@novascale.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@novascale.ai>
Date: 2026-11-22T09:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Updated: NovaScale renewal — amortized bridging credit + 24mo option

Matt/Team — thanks for the updated offer. A couple of legal/contractual points before we can sign: 

1) Please include a clause clarifying that the bridging credit is non-refundable and applies only to invoices for the contracted account id (we need to avoid accidental application to other entities).
2) SLA addendum: we'll accept the Hosted API default SLA; for the dedicated addendum please include a data-residency clause and incident escalation matrix.
3) Limitation of liability: we need to keep current mutual limits; if Redwood proposes higher exposure for higher commits (e.g., dedicated), call that out and we'll scope separately.

If you can incorporate (1) into the DocuSign envelope and attach the SLA addendum for our review, procurement can begin routing. Also confirm the effective date of the amortization (start of first invoice period vs sign date).

I'll await the DocuSign.

Regards,
Joe Lin | Counsel | NovaScale
[Procurement ticket: NS-PO-2026-87]

1) That bridging credit — is it a one-time credit we can apply to the first invoice, or do you prefer amortizing across Q1? Procurement prefers amortized credits for auditability but we can be flexible.
2) The ladder bands look reasonable; can you confirm the per-token price if we commit to a 24-month term instead of 12?
3) Our legal asked whether the renewal would carry forward existing SLA language or if Redwood intends to revise the uptime targets for higher commits.

I'll loop Joe (legal) on the next message; procurement would prefer a clean DocuSign flow once we land on numbers.

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Khan | Head of Platform | NovaScale
A: aisha.k@novascale.ai
[Procurement tracker reference: NS-PROC-1120]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-19T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: FW: Renewal scoping: bridging runway + staged per-token ladder — internal align

Lauren / Aditya — forwarding Aisha's questions. Quick internal alignment note so we respond with a clean offer: 

- Bridging credit: propose $60k amortized monthly across Q1 on their PO to meet auditability ask. This protects their cash flow and avoids an awkward one-off credit entry.
- 24-month term uplift: offer an additional 5% discount on per-token bands if they sign 24 months (i.e., multiply the band prices by 0.95) — this keeps us competitive but caps long-term exposure.
- SLA: keep existing SLA language for Hosted API, but provide a one-page addendum that documents availability targets for their dedicated capacity should they opt to migrate. That addendum is non-economic and should placate legal.
- Uplift cap: keep 10% YOY auto-raise guardrail; above that triggers a mid-term true-up and potential re-tier.

I've attached a quick calc showing how the amortized bridging credit moves the effective first-quarter net price (bridging_credit_calc.xlsx). If this is approved I'll draft the DocuSign and route to Lauren for sign-off.

Cheers,
Mateo
[Attachment: bridging_credit_calc.xlsx]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.k@novascale.ai>, Joe Lin <joe.lin@novascale.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procure@novascale.ai>
Date: 2026-11-19T13:45:00-08:00
Subject: Updated: NovaScale renewal — amortized bridging credit + 24mo option

Hi Aisha / Joe — following internal alignment, here's an updated, actionable offer so procurement/legal can review: 

1) Commit & term options:
   - 12-mo: 120M tokens commit; banded per-token pricing as previously shared (0.0009 / 0.00075 / 0.0006)
   - 24-mo: 120M tokens commit; 5% reduction on each band (applied to per-token rates)

2) Bridging credit:
   - $60,000 credit amortized across Q1 invoices (applies against usage invoices until exhausted)

3) Price protections and governance:
   - 10% annual uplift cap for automatic roll-forward. Any requested committed increase beyond 10% triggers an agreed true-up process and may require model variant mapping for capacity.
   - Quarterly usage review between Redwood & NovaScale (mutual action plan to avoid surprises)

4) Next steps:
   - If procurement accepts amortized credit, we'll generate a DocuSign with the selected term and the amortization line item called out.
   - Legal: we've drafted a 1-page SLA addendum for Dedicated/Private options; confirm if you want it attached to the current renewal or on an as-needed basis.

Attached updated calc and proposed MPA language in the PDF. Please advise on preferred term (12 vs 24) and whether procurement wants the credit listed as an invoice line or a separate credit memo.

Thanks,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez | Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: bridging_credit_calc.xlsx]
[Attachment: NOVA_MA_plan_v1.pdf]

From: Joe Lin <joe.lin@novascale.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Aisha Khan <aisha.k@novascale.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@novascale.ai>
Date: 2026-11-22T09:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Updated: NovaScale renewal — amortized bridging credit + 24mo option

Matt/Team — thanks for the updated offer. A couple of legal/contractual points before we can sign: 

1) Please include a clause clarifying that the bridging credit is non-refundable and applies only to invoices for the contracted account id (we need to avoid accidental application to other entities).
2) SLA addendum: we'll accept the Hosted API default SLA; for the dedicated addendum please include a data-residency clause and incident escalation matrix.
3) Limitation of liability: we need to keep current mutual limits; if Redwood proposes higher exposure for higher commits (e.g., dedicated), call that out and we'll scope separately.

If you can incorporate (1) into the DocuSign envelope and attach the SLA addendum for our review, procurement can begin routing. Also confirm the effective date of the amortization (start of first invoice period vs sign date).

I'll await the DocuSign.

Regards,
Joe Lin | Counsel | NovaScale
[Procurement ticket: NS-PO-2026-87]

source.subject: Registration funnel wireframe + last-mile reminder plan
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: Registration funnel wireframe + last-mile reminder plan

Hi team —

Putting a pared-down wireframe together for the inbound webinar invite series we’re running with Quantum Analytics. Primary asks: conversion path (invite → register → confirmation → reminder), timezone-aware reminder windows, and a resilient tracking fallback if the UTM click strips or redirects.

Key constraints:
- Target list split (APAC / EMEA / Americas) needs staggered send times.
- We want two reminder touches: 48 hours before and 2 hours before the session for high-priority accounts.
- Fallback tracking: if the tracking domain blocks, use onClick cookie + final landing query param.

I attached the initial segment CSV and the invite copy draft (v3). Please:
- Marissa: review CTA language and subject lines (I tried shorter CTAs for engineers).
- Ben: map the UTM/HubSpot click paths and add fallback options.
- Vivek: confirm capacity for the dedicated partner promo (we may include a 10-seat early access code).

I mocked up a tiny wireframe in the shared sheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1a2b3c-redwood-invite-wireframe

Thanks — Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Field Marketing Manager | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: 20261118-target-list-segments.csv, invite-copy-v3.docx
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 10:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Registration funnel wireframe + last-mile reminder plan

On Wed, Nov 18, 2026 at 9:03 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi team —
> 
> Putting a pared-down wireframe together for the inbound webinar invite series we’re running with Quantum Analytics...

Thanks for the clear kickoff — this looks good. A few quick notes from copy perspective:
- Subject line variants: two options: "See how Quantum Analytics cut inference cost 3x — RSVP" (external-friendly) and "Live: Inference economics for product teams — register" (engineer-friendly).
- CTA text: I recommend "Save my spot" over "Register now" for mobile.
- Confirmation email should include the ics + a short checklist (browser requirements, what to bring).

I updated invite-copy-v3.docx with subject variants and a short confirmation email. Also: we should A/B the 48-hour reminder subject with and without the speaker name.

Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Head of Events | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 12:09:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Registration funnel wireframe + last-mile reminder plan

Mateo / Marissa —

Nice outline. Quick mapping on tracking:
- Primary: utm_source=partner&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=qa-webinar_202611
- Click tracking layer: first-click on redwood-trk.redwood.com -> redirect to final LP with final query params (this keeps HubSpot happy if the partner proxied click strips original UTMs).
- Fallback: if redirect blocked, set a 1-day cookie with campaign metadata and append &campaign_ref=redwood_fallback on LP load so we attribute via landing page script.

I exported a quick utm-tracking-map.csv and attached. For HubSpot: I’ll create a tracking URL in HubSpot and also add an event funnel that marks "registered_from_partner_wire".

Also flagged the highest-priority company domains from the CSV so they get the 2-hour reminder.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Demand Gen | Redwood Inference
From: Sarah Lee <sarah@quantumanalytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 08:32:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Registration funnel wireframe + last-mile reminder plan

Hi Mateo / Marissa —

Thanks for sending the plan. A couple items from our side:
- For APAC sends, our EMEA account team can do the 6pm SGT send if you prefer.
- We’d like the early-access code to be a single-use token per registrant — can Redwood issue and sync codes back to our registration report?
- We prefer the confirmation page to show the speaker bios inline rather than link out; conversion is higher that way.

We’ll review the tracking notes with our engineering lead and confirm whether the redwood-trk redirect is allowed by our security policy (we’ll need the test endpoint to vet).

Sarah
Partner Marketing | Quantum Analytics
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lee <sarah@quantumanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Registration funnel wireframe + last-mile reminder plan

Sarah —

Great — appreciate the quick clarifications. Action items for us:
- Ben: generate single-use promo code flow and share sample CSV by EOD Monday (will include code + registration_id).
- Marissa: update confirmation LP to embed speaker blurbs and send final copy for QA by Monday.
- Vivek: confirm whether we can generate codes from the promo engine (if not, I’ll request engineering assistance).
- Sarah: if you can share the partner security contact, I’ll give them the redwood-trk test endpoint and the minimal traffic pattern we’ll use for verification.

I’ll consolidate into the shared sheet and set a short 30-min sync early next week to finalize send windows.

Thanks all — Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Field Marketing Manager | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Discovery recap + mutual action plan for Luminary Health pilot
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ella Park <ella.park@luminaryhealth.com>; Rajiv Kumar <rajiv.kumar@luminaryhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Discovery recap + next steps (Luminary Health)

Hi Ella / Rajiv —

Thanks again for the time earlier today. Quick recap from our side and a proposed mutual action plan (MPA) to keep momentum toward a pilot decision. I pulled notes from the call and flagged owners / dates below. Please skim and edit where I missed anything.

Top pain points you described:
- High inference cost for vector search and multi-turn chat (esp. on clinical documents).
- Unpredictable latency spikes during morning query bursts impacting clinician UX.
- Compliance constraints around data residency and audit logs for PHI.

Goals you shared:
- Run a 6-week pilot focusing on clinical document summarization and triage workflows.
- Target p95 latency < 300ms for common query lengths and average per-token inference cost reduction of ~40%.
- Demonstrate auditability for data access and retention settings.

Proposed MPA (high level) — owners and tentative dates:
1) Pilot scoping & success metrics (owner: Ella / Luminary) — due Nov 26
2) Security & compliance review: SOC 2 artifact review + KMS integration plan (owner: Rajiv + Redwood sec) — due Dec 3
3) Proposal & pricing for Dedicated capacity with burst (owner: Marissa) — due Dec 7
4) Pilot environment prep (VPC setup, model pinning, test dataset) (owner: Mateo/Redwood infra) — start Dec 8, complete Dec 18
5) Pilot run (6 weeks) — target start Dec 20, wrap Jan 31

Immediate asks / next steps:
- Ella: confirm the stakeholders listed above and the Nov 26 scoping slot.
- Rajiv: I’ll send a short security packet; please point me to the right intake for KMS/HSM details.
- Marissa: can you draft a pricing outline for Dedicated + burst capacity that fits a 6-week pilot profile?

Attachments: I’ve attached a first-pass MPA spreadsheet (Luminary_MPA_v1.xlsx) and a short security evidence summary we typically share for pilots.

If that plan looks reasonable I’ll lock the calendar items and route the security artifacts to Rajiv. Thanks — excited to get this moving.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

-----
From: Ella Park <ella.park@luminaryhealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rajiv Kumar <rajiv.kumar@luminaryhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Discovery recap + next steps (Luminary Health)

Thanks Mateo — this is great and captures the conversation. A few small updates from our side:

- Stakeholders: add Priya Menon (Clinical Ops) and Sameer Gupta (Data Governance). I’ll intro both in a follow-up calendar invite for the Nov 26 scoping session.
- On goals: we also want a simple SLA for availability during the pilot window (we’ll use that internally to justify clinician UX tests).
- Procurement: looping in procurement@luminaryhealth.com for early visibility on contract terms.

Rajiv — for security intake, we have a central vendor portal (procurement@luminaryhealth.com will share the link). For KMS/HSM specifics, I’m listing the tech contact for you in the scoping invite.

I can confirm the Nov 26 scoping window; please add Priya and Sameer.

Best,
Ella Park
Director, Clinical Innovation | Luminary Health
ella.park@luminaryhealth.com
+1 (650) 555-0147

-----
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 11:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Discovery recap + next steps (Luminary Health) — pricing + security asks

Team — forwarding the recap from Mateo. Luminary wants a Dedicated + burst pricing outline and a short security evidence packet. Can we do the following?

Aditya: please draft a pilot pricing scenario (monthly equivalent + estimated token-run cost) that assumes: Dedicated pool sized for ~150 concurrent requests (p95 target) with burst to hosted capacity. Include an optional quantized model SKU to show cost trade-offs. Due: Dec 7 (Marissa will own the external delivery).

Soojin: please pull the SOC 2 artifacts and prepare a one-page summary of audit logs / retention controls we can share. Also include a short note about KMS/HSM integration steps for Luminary’s team. I’ll include both in the procurement track. Due: Dec 3.

Mateo — I can draft a clean MPA doc with participant columns and milestones timeline; will attach to the deck for the scoping session.

Thanks —
Marissa
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

-----

preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ella Park <ella.park@luminaryhealth.com>; Rajiv Kumar <rajiv.kumar@luminaryhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Discovery recap + next steps (Luminary Health)

Hi Ella / Rajiv —

Thanks again for the time earlier today. Quick recap from our side and a proposed mutual action plan (MPA) to keep momentum toward a pilot decision. I pulled notes from the call and flagged owners / dates below. Please skim and edit where I missed anything.

Top pain points you described:
- High inference cost for vector search and multi-turn chat (esp. on clinical documents).
- Unpredictable latency spikes during morning query bursts impacting clinician UX.
- Compliance constraints around data residency and audit logs for PHI.

Goals you shared:
- Run a 6-week pilot focusing on clinical document summarization and triage workflows.
- Target p95 latency < 300ms for common query lengths and average per-token inference cost reduction of ~40%.
- Demonstrate auditability for data access and retention settings.

Proposed MPA (high level) — owners and tentative dates:
1) Pilot scoping & success metrics (owner: Ella / Luminary) — due Nov 26
2) Security & compliance review: SOC 2 artifact review + KMS integration plan (owner: Rajiv + Redwood sec) — due Dec 3
3) Proposal & pricing for Dedicated capacity with burst (owner: Marissa) — due Dec 7
4) Pilot environment prep (VPC setup, model pinning, test dataset) (owner: Mateo/Redwood infra) — start Dec 8, complete Dec 18
5) Pilot run (6 weeks) — target start Dec 20, wrap Jan 31

Immediate asks / next steps:
- Ella: confirm the stakeholders listed above and the Nov 26 scoping slot.
- Rajiv: I’ll send a short security packet; please point me to the right intake for KMS/HSM details.
- Marissa: can you draft a pricing outline for Dedicated + burst capacity that fits a 6-week pilot profile?

Attachments: I’ve attached a first-pass MPA spreadsheet (Luminary_MPA_v1.xlsx) and a short security evidence summary we typically share for pilots.

If that plan looks reasonable I’ll lock the calendar items and route the security artifacts to Rajiv. Thanks — excited to get this moving.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez

source.subject: Evaluation acceptance runway and exit-test catalog
messages:
From: Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot scoping — acceptance criteria request

Hi Mateo / Grace,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team is excited. Before we lock the dates for the pilot kickoff (targeting early Jan), our security and product teams need a short mutual action plan (MAP) that clearly lists: success criteria, exit gates, and the concrete acceptance tests we'll run during the evaluation window.

Can you send a draft MAP we can review with internal stakeholders? High level items that matter to us: production-like latency under 95th percentile, accuracy/concordance on our labeled holdout, and a clear rollback rule if regression is detected. We have some internal constraints on data retention and auditability that may affect instrumentation.

If you can share a one-sheet + acceptance test catalog by EOD Tuesday (our time), I'll circulate to procurement and security.

Elena
Product Lead — AllCare AI

Attachments: none
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 12:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot scoping — acceptance criteria request

Elena — thanks, good ask. Draft attached (MAP_v1.xlsx) and I pushed a working copy to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aMapDraft. Below is a concise acceptance runway and starter test catalog; we can iterate after your review.

Quick summary (proposal):
- Pilot window: 4 weeks active evaluation (13 Jan — 9 Feb, staging → prod mirror traffic).
- Owners: Redwood SRE (Mateo) + AllCare ML Ops (Elena).
- Success KPIs (must meet all to pass):
  1) Latency: P95 generation latency < 600ms for 90% of test traffic (B2C sampling).
  2) Throughput: Sustained 500 requests/minute with <2% error rate on dedicated pool.
  3) Quality: 95% concordance vs AllCare labeled holdout (N=1,000) on primary task; minor tolerance for edge classes with documented follow-ups.
- Exit gates (any one triggers a stop):
  a) Quality drop below 90% on holdout for two consecutive measurement windows (24h).
  b) Sustained error rate >5% for >12 hours.
  c) Observed cost per 1k requests > 1.5x the forecasted unit cost for >48 hours without mitigation plan.
- Acceptance tests (examples, full catalog attached):
  * Smoke: baseline end-to-end inference on 50 canonical prompts (pass/fail).
  * Regression suite: 200 labeled checks covering top 10 intents and edge cases.
  * Load test: ramp to 1.2x expected peak for 30 minutes.
  * Failover test: simulated pod preemption and auto-fallback to variant model.

I'll also include instrumentation expectations: token-level latency traces, token counts per request, and KV cache hit rates. See attached MAP_v1.xlsx for owners, thresholds, and measurement cadence.

If the thresholds above look reasonable, can we lock them and I will create a short sign-off checklist for procurement/security?

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference

Attachments: MAP_v1.xlsx
From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot scoping — acceptance criteria request

Elena — quick notes after scanning Mateo's proposal (thanks Mateo). Two suggested edits to make the MAP operationally tighter:

1) Quality cadence: measure daily for the first two weeks, then every-other-day. If a single-day regression appears, trigger the regression triage playbook (owner: AllCare ML Ops + Redwood AP).
2) Add a concordance sub-metric for high-risk intents (false positive rate) with a fail threshold at 5%. That helps surface clinically-sensitive cases faster.
3) Add a short acceptance test called "Data lineage check" — verify that every inference can be traced to a request id and token counts (this is a requirement from their security lead).

Also attaching a small doc with the pilot acceptance tests in a checklist format so security/procurement can tick off items quickly (pilot-acceptance-tests.docx).

Grace
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference

On Sun, Nov 15, 2026 at 12:30 PM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — thanks, good ask. Draft attached (MAP_v1.xlsx) and I pushed a working copy to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aMapDraft. Below is a concise acceptance runway and starter test catalog; we can iterate after your review.
>
> Quick summary (proposal):
> - Pilot window: 4 weeks active evaluation (13 Jan — 9 Feb, staging → prod mirror traffic).
> - Owners: Redwood SRE (Mateo) + AllCare ML Ops (Elena).
> - Success KPIs (must meet all to pass):
>   1) Latency: P95 generation latency < 600ms for 90% of test traffic (B2C sampling).
>
Attachments: pilot-acceptance-tests.docx
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Finalized MAP + procurement ask (RDW-DEAL-9942)

Karthik / Sean — looping you both in to convert the MAP into procurement and capacity next steps. Elena and Grace have agreed on the thresholds above; Grace's additions (high-risk intent FPR and data-lineage check) are included in the attached artifacts.

Action items I propose now:
- Karthik: prepare a short addendum to the SOW that lists the pilot acceptance criteria and the rollback triggers (to be appended to the standard MSA). We want procurement to approve reserving a dedicated pool with burst headroom for the pilot dates.
- Sean: can you approve a temporary SLA exception for monitoring retention (14 days token-level traces) and confirm pricing uplift for burst capacity? If you need a formal PO, Elena's procurement will provide one once the HubSpot deal is updated.
- Elena: please confirm the holdout set sample (N=1,000) and that security approves the data-lineage check; if there are constraints we'll flag them and update owners.

Final sign-off checklist (for pilot go/no-go):
  [ ] MAP + owners documented in Drive (done)
  [ ] Acceptance-test catalog (checked off by AllCare security)
  [ ] Dedicated pool reserved + pricing agreed
  [ ] Instrumentation (traces, token counts) enabled in staging mirror
  [ ] Rollback & escalation path documented

I'll draft the HubSpot note and attach the MAP artifacts to the deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deals/9942). Grace — once Karthik/Sean confirm procurement and SLA items we can set a hard pilot start date.

Thanks all — Mateo
Senior Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference

On Mon, Nov 16, 2026 at 08:22 AM Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — quick notes after scanning Mateo's proposal (thanks Mateo). Two suggested edits to make the MAP operationally tighter:...

Attachments: MAP_v1.xlsx, pilot-acceptance-tests.docx

  [ ] Dedicated pool reserved + pricing agreed
  [ ] Instrumentation (traces, token counts) enabled in staging mirror
  [ ] Rollback & escalation path documented

I'll draft the HubSpot note and attach the MAP artifacts to the deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deals/9942). Grace — once Karthik/Sean confirm procurement and SLA items we can set a hard pilot start date.

Thanks all — Mateo
Senior Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference

On Mon, Nov 16, 2026 at 08:22 AM Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — quick notes after scanning Mateo's proposal (thanks Mateo). Two suggested edits to make the MAP operationally tighter:...

text:
From: Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Pilot scoping — acceptance criteria request

Hi Mateo / Grace,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team is excited. Before we lock the dates for the pilot kickoff (targeting early Jan), our security and product teams need a short mutual action plan (MAP) that clearly lists: success criteria, exit gates, and the concrete acceptance tests we'll run during the evaluation window.

Can you send a draft MAP we can review with internal stakeholders? High level items that matter to us: production-like latency under 95th percentile, accuracy/concordance on our labeled holdout, and a clear rollback rule if regression is detected. We have some internal constraints on data retention and auditability that may affect instrumentation.

If you can share a one-sheet + acceptance test catalog by EOD Tuesday (our time), I'll circulate to procurement and security.

Elena
Product Lead — AllCare AI

Attachments: none


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 12:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot scoping — acceptance criteria request

Elena — thanks, good ask. Draft attached (MAP_v1.xlsx) and I pushed a working copy to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aMapDraft. Below is a concise acceptance runway and starter test catalog; we can iterate after your review.

Quick summary (proposal):
- Pilot window: 4 weeks active evaluation (13 Jan — 9 Feb, staging → prod mirror traffic).
- Owners: Redwood SRE (Mateo) + AllCare ML Ops (Elena).
- Success KPIs (must meet all to pass):
  1) Latency: P95 generation latency < 600ms for 90% of test traffic (B2C sampling).
  2) Throughput: Sustained 500 requests/minute with <2% error rate on dedicated pool.
  3) Quality: 95% concordance vs AllCare labeled holdout (N=1,000) on primary task; minor tolerance for edge classes with documented follow-ups.
- Exit gates (any one triggers a stop):
  a) Quality drop below 90% on holdout for two consecutive measurement windows (24h).
  b) Sustained error rate >5% for >12 hours.
  c) Observed cost per 1k requests > 1.5x the forecasted unit cost for >48 hours without mitigation plan.
- Acceptance tests (examples, full catalog attached):
  * Smoke: baseline end-to-end inference on 50 canonical prompts (pass/fail).
  * Regression suite: 200 labeled checks covering top 10 intents and edge cases.
  * Load test: ramp to 1.2x expected peak for 30 minutes.
  * Failover test: simulated pod preemption and auto-fallback to variant model.

I'll also include instrumentation expectations: token-level latency traces, token counts per request, and KV cache hit rates. See attached MAP_v1.xlsx for owners, thresholds, and measurement cadence.

If the thresholds above look reasonable, can we lock them and I will create a short sign-off checklist for procurement/security?

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference

Attachments: MAP_v1.xlsx


From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 08:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot scoping — acceptance criteria request

Elena — quick notes after scanning Mateo's proposal (thanks Mateo). Two suggested edits to make the MAP operationally tighter:

1) Quality cadence: measure daily for the first two weeks, then every-other-day. If a single-day regression appears, trigger the regression triage playbook (owner: AllCare ML Ops + Redwood AP).
2) Add a concordance sub-metric for high-risk intents (false positive rate) with a fail threshold at 5%. That helps surface clinically-sensitive cases faster.
3) Add a short acceptance test called "Data lineage check" — verify that every inference can be traced to a request id and token counts (this is a requirement from their security lead).

Also attaching a small doc with the pilot acceptance tests in a checklist format so security/procurement can tick off items quickly (pilot-acceptance-tests.docx).

Grace
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference

On Sun, Nov 15, 2026 at 12:30 PM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — thanks, good ask. Draft attached (MAP_v1.xlsx) and I pushed a working copy to Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aMapDraft. Below is a concise acceptance runway and starter test catalog; we can iterate after your review.
>
> Quick summary (proposal):
> - Pilot window: 4 weeks active evaluation (13 Jan — 9 Feb, staging → prod mirror traffic).
> - Owners: Redwood SRE (Mateo) + AllCare ML Ops (Elena).
> - Success KPIs (must meet all to pass):
>   1) Latency: P95 generation latency < 600ms for 90% of test traffic (B2C sampling).
>
Attachments: pilot-acceptance-tests.docx


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Elena Park <elena@allcare.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Finalized MAP + procurement ask (RDW-DEAL-9942)

Karthik / Sean — looping you both in to convert the MAP into procurement and capacity next steps. Elena and Grace have agreed on the thresholds above; Grace's additions (high-risk intent FPR and data-lineage check) are included in the attached artifacts.

Action items I propose now:
- Karthik: prepare a short addendum to the SOW that lists the pilot acceptance criteria and the rollback triggers (to be appended to the standard MSA). We want procurement to approve reserving a dedicated pool with burst headroom for the pilot dates.
- Sean: can you approve a temporary SLA exception for monitoring retention (14 days token-level traces) and confirm pricing uplift for burst capacity? If you need a formal PO, Elena's procurement will provide one once the HubSpot deal is updated.
- Elena: please confirm the holdout set sample (N=1,000) and that security approves the data-lineage check; if there are constraints we'll flag them and update owners.

Final sign-off checklist (for pilot go/no-go):
  [ ] MAP + owners documented in Drive (done)
  [ ] Acceptance-test catalog (checked off by AllCare security)
  [ ] Dedicated pool reserved + pricing agreed
  [ ] Instrumentation (traces, token counts) enabled in staging mirror
  [ ] Rollback & escalation path documented

I'll draft the HubSpot note and attach the MAP artifacts to the deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deals/9942). Grace — once Karthik/Sean confirm procurement and SLA items we can set a hard pilot start date.

Thanks all — Mateo
Senior Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cloudscale.com>, Tony Li <tony.li@cloudscale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated sizing kickoff — scheduling and pre-call checklist (CloudScale)

Thanks again for the time today, Elena & Tony — good discussion. Quick recap and suggested next steps so we can lock a dedicated sizing kickoff (throughput & latency intake + workload samples):

Summary from discovery (short):
- Primary use case: real-time customer support triage + automatic summarization for post-call workflows.
- Expected peak patterns: short bursts during PST business hours and nightly batch reruns for embeddings.
- Data sensitivity: PII in transcripts; CloudScale prefers private deployment options.

What we need for the sizing kickoff:
1) Representative request profile (CSV with fields: prompt_length, avg_response_tokens, requests_per_minute by interval).
2) Target latency SLO (p50/p95) and acceptable cold-start behavior.
3) Peak concurrency and any soft/hard throughput caps.
4) A list of example prompt templates and typical batch sizes (if you plan batching).

Proposed 45–60m slots for the kickoff (we can do technical deep dive + quick perf simulation plan):
- Wed, Nov 18 — 10:00–11:00 PT
- Wed, Nov 18 — 14:00–15:00 PT
- Thu, Nov 19 — 09:00–10:00 PT

If those don't work, please pick a time via our quick scheduler: https://calendly.com/redwood-inference/dedicated-sizing-30m

I attached a short checklist PDF we use to speed the session (rwd_dedicated_sizing_checklist.pdf) — it lists the artifacts and a suggested agenda. If you can drop the CSV mentioned above and the performance target before the call, we can run a preliminary capacity estimate and bring a sample configuration to the kickoff.

CC'ing Marissa and Aditya so they can align on contractual questions and any security/compliance items we should prep for the call.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this on the calendar.

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0124
[Attachment: rwd_dedicated_sizing_checklist.pdf (pdf, 78KB)]

From: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cloudscale.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Tony Li <tony.li@cloudscale.com>, CloudScale Procurement <procurement@cloudscale.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing kickoff — scheduling and pre-call checklist (CloudScale)

Hi Mateo — thanks for the recap and for the checklist. Wed Nov 18 looks good for us; the 14:00–15:00 PT slot is best. 

I've attached a short requirements workbook with our initial traffic sample and a few prompt examples (CloudScale_requirements.xlsx). High-level targets:
- Sustained steady-state: ~30 requests/sec for inference with average response ~120 tokens.
- Burst target: up to ~120 requests/sec for short windows (~2–3 minutes) during peak events.
- Latency: goal is p95 < 300ms for the normal inference path; p99 can be higher for cold-starts.

Questions for the kickoff so we can be ready:
- Do you need raw transcript files or a summarized CSV? We can provide both.
- What's your recommended warmup strategy to meet the p95 target during bursts?

Adding Procurement to thread for scheduling the contractual/PO conversation after sizing.

Thanks,
Elena Morris
Head of ML Platforms | CloudScale Inc.
elena.morris@cloudscale.com | +1 (206) 555-0199
[Attachment: CloudScale_requirements.xlsx (xlsx, 42KB)]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cloudscale.com>, Tony Li <tony.li@cloudscale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, CloudScale Procurement <procurement@cloudscale.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 08:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing kickoff — scheduling and pre-call checklist (CloudScale)

Elena — perfect, locking Wed Nov 18 14:00 PT. I've sent a calendar invite (Google Meet details included) and attached a quick pre-call checklist we use to run a short simulation before the meeting.

Quick answers inline:
- Raw transcript files are ideal for more accurate tokenization and model-config sims; CSV aggregates are fine for first-pass capacity estimates. If you can provide a small sample (5–10 raw transcripts) that'd be great.
- Warmup strategy: we typically recommend a short steady ramp before anticipated bursts (2–5 minutes at ~30–50% of expected burst RPS) with persistent warm instances for the steady baseline. We'll walk through warmup knobs in the kickoff.

Pre-call asks (please drop these ahead of Wed's call if possible):
- 10–20 representative raw transcripts or a tokenized sample.
- The CSV with requests_per_minute and avg_response_tokens by interval.
- Any existing cost constraints or preferred GPU families (NVIDIA A100/generic vRAM constraints).

Agenda for Wed Nov 18, 14:00 PT (45m):
1) 5m — Introductions / goals
2) 15m — Workload walk-through (you present sample traffic/profile)
3) 15m — Capacity estimation and potential config options (dedicated pools, instance types, batching)
4) 10m — Next steps: procurement touchpoints, pilot timeline, and action items

If Procurement needs a separate slot for licensing/PO, Marissa will coordinate.

See you Wednesday — invite from "Redwood Inference" should be in your inbox.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: rwd_dedicated_sizing_checklist.pdf (pdf, 78KB)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cloudscale.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, CloudScale Procurement <procurement@cloudscale.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing kickoff — scheduling and pre-call checklist (CloudScale)

Thanks everyone — joining from Redwood now. Quick note: we'll keep procurement and legal in the loop but won't block the technical kickoff. After the sizing session, I'll follow up with a proposed commercial path (pilot vs reserved commit) and an outline of typical timelines for Dedicated pools.

Action items I own post-call:
- Draft pilot commercial proposal and share with CloudScale Procurement (target: EOD Fri 11/20).
- Coordinate legal/contracting checklist if CloudScale wants to expedite a pilot.

Looks good to me — Mateo, if you want Aditya to bring the perf tooling outputs to the call, let me know and I'll make sure he's prepared.

Marissa Cole
Senior Sales Lead | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0188

On Tue, Nov 17, 2026 at 08:27 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elena — perfect, locking Wed Nov 18 14:00 PT. I've sent a calendar invite (Google Meet details included) and attached a quick pre-call checklist we use to run a short simulation before the meeting.
> 
> Quick answers inline:
> - Raw transcript files are ideal for more accurate tokenization and model-config sims; CSV aggregates are fine for first-pass capacity estimates. If you can provide a small sample (5–10 raw transcripts) that'd be great.
> - Warmup strategy: we typically recommend a short steady ramp before anticipated bursts (2–5 minutes at ~30–50% of expected burst RPS) with persistent warm instances for the steady baseline. We'll walk through warmup knobs in the kickoff.
> 
> Pre-call asks (please drop these ahead of Wed's call if possible):
> - 10–20 representative raw transcripts or a tokenized sample.
> - The CSV with requests_per_minute and avg_response_tokens by interval.
> - Any existing cost constraints or preferred GPU families (NVIDIA A100/generic vRAM constraints).
> 
> Agenda for Wed Nov 18, 14:00 PT (45m):
> 1) 5m — Introductions / goals
> 2) 15m — Workload walk-through (you present sample traffic/profile)
> 3) 15m — Capacity estimation and potential config options (dedicated pools, instance types, batching)
> 4) 10m — Next steps: procurement touchpoints, pilot timeline, and action items
> 
> If Procurement needs a separate slot for licensing/PO, Marissa will coordinate.
> 
> See you Wednesday — invite from "Redwood Inference" should be in your inbox.

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, CloudScale Procurement <procurement@cloudscale.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 08:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing kickoff — scheduling and pre-call checklist (CloudScale)

Elena — perfect, locking Wed Nov 18 14:00 PT. I've sent a calendar invite (Google Meet details included) and attached a quick pre-call checklist we use to run a short simulation before the meeting.

Quick answers inline:
- Raw transcript files are ideal for more accurate tokenization and model-config sims; CSV aggregates are fine for first-pass capacity estimates. If you can provide a small sample (5–10 raw transcripts) that'd be great.
- Warmup strategy: we typically recommend a short steady ramp before anticipated bursts (2–5 minutes at ~30–50% of expected burst RPS) with persistent warm instances for the steady baseline. We'll walk through warmup knobs in the kickoff.

Pre-call asks (please drop these ahead of Wed's call if possible):
- 10–20 representative raw transcripts or a tokenized sample.
- The CSV with requests_per_minute and avg_response_tokens by interval.
- Any existing cost constraints or preferred GPU families (NVIDIA A100/generic vRAM constraints).

Agenda for Wed Nov 18, 14:00 PT (45m):
1) 5m — Introductions / goals
2) 15m — Workload walk-through (you present sample traffic/profile)
3) 15m — Capacity estimation and potential config options (dedicated pools, instance types, batching)
4) 10m — Next steps: procurement touchpoints, pilot timeline, and action items

If Procurement needs a separate slot for licensing/PO, Marissa will coordinate.

See you Wednesday — invite from "Redwood Inference" should be in your inbox.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: rwd_dedicated_sizing_checklist.pdf (pdf, 78KB)]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena.morris@cloudscale.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, CloudScale Procurement <procurement@cloudscale.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing kickoff — scheduling and pre-call checklist (CloudScale)

Thanks everyone — joining from Redwood now. Quick note: we'll keep procurement and legal in the loop but won't block the technical kickoff. After the sizing session, I'll follow up with a proposed commercial path (pilot vs reserved commit) and an outline of typical timelines for Dedicated pools.

Action items I own post-call:
- Draft pilot commercial proposal and share with CloudScale Procurement (target: EOD Fri 11/20).
- Coordinate legal/contracting checklist if CloudScale wants to expedite a pilot.

Looks good to me — Mateo, if you want Aditya to bring the perf tooling outputs to the call, let me know and I'll make sure he's prepared.

Marissa Cole
Senior Sales Lead | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0188

source.subject: Batched inference: uptime/credit tradeoff proposal
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@aetherdata.com>, procurement@aetherdata.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Nov 2026 09:21:00 -0800
Subject: Batched inference: uptime/credit tradeoff proposal

Hi Emma / Aether procurement team,

Thanks again for the productive call on Friday. I pulled together a concrete proposal that aims to balance your cost target for high-volume batched inference with our standard operational guarantees. High level: batched inference reduces per-token cost but introduces occasional short latency tail events during high concurrency windows. To make this workable for both sides we can do a conditional uptime concession for batched routes in exchange for a lower monthly credit exposure.

Proposal (summary):
- Real-time endpoints (p95 <= 250ms): keep standard uptime 99.95% and unchanged credit schedule.
- Batched inference endpoints (throughput-optimized pools): allow an adjusted uptime target of 99.90% measured monthly, specifically for the batched routing namespace. This exception applies only while requests are routed to the batched pool (we will tag and provide metrics).
- Monthly credit offset: if you accept the batched uptime exception, we reduce the monthly service-credit multiplier by 40% for incidents attributed to batched pool performance (capped by the same monthly credit cap).
- Visibility & controls: Redwood will provide per-route uptime/latency dashboards, weekly consumption reports, and a 30-day rollback clause if SLA impact is materially different than modeled.

I attached a short draft (RDW_SLA_BatchedOffset_v1.pdf) with the suggested redline language and a sample credit-calculation table. If this looks close to what procurement needs, Laura or Marissa can follow up with precise legal-friendly wording.

Next steps:
- Can procurement confirm whether a 40% reduction to the credit multiplier on batched incidents is meaningful for your TCO?
- If yes, we can circulate a redline and aim for a signature on an amendment before the pilot start.

Thanks,
Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
m: +1 415-555-0199
From: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@aetherdata.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@aetherdata.com>, Tom Kwan <tom.kwan@aetherdata.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Batched inference: uptime/credit tradeoff proposal

Mateo — thanks for the clear summary. A few quick clarifying questions from procurement/legal and from our SRE team so we can evaluate: 

1) Attribution & measurement: how will you attribute incidents to the batched pool vs real-time pool? We need guaranteed tagging and a daily feed of incident windows for the first 90 days.
2) Credit math: can you show an example month where batched incidents would hit the cap — we need the worksheet to present to finance.
3) Rollback clause: the 30-day rollback sounds fine but procurement prefers a 14-day early termination right for the pilot if SLA variance > 0.5% vs forecast. Is that acceptable?

We shared your PDF with Tom (legal) and procurement; he asked for a redline that places the batched exception into an explicit appendix and a cross-reference to the monitoring feed. Attached: Aether_Procurement_Checklist.pdf with our internal checkboxes.

Can someone at Redwood produce a toy-month example and confirm the tagging approach? Also, what would the operational playbook look like for escalations (on-call, contact window)?

Best,
Emma

--
Emma Johnson
Head of Partnerships | AetherData
emma.johnson@aetherdata.com
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 14:55:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: proposed redlines / negotiation points for AetherData

Folks — drafting notes based on Emma's questions (for Mateo to send to the customer):

1) Attribution & measurement
- We will tag routed requests with a route_namespace header: "rdw.route_pool=batched". This metadata flows through logs and to the per-route telemetry. We'll provide daily incident exports (CSV) for first 90 days and then weekly thereafter.

2) Credit math: toy-month example (include in reply)
- Monthly billable: $500k (example)
- Incident window attributed to batched pool: 7.2 hours affecting 2% of monthly requests -> triggers credit band equivalent to 1.25% of monthly bill = $6,250.
- With agreed 40% multiplier reduction the payable credit becomes $3,750. Cap remains (as proposed) at 10% of monthly invoice.

3) Rollback / early termination
- We can accept a 14-day early termination for the pilot if observed SLA variance > 0.5% vs forecast; require 14 days' notice and a short remediation period to attempt fixes before termination executes. Add a mutual review call in that 14-day window.

4) Operational playbook / escalations
- On-call: Redwood SRE on primary pager 24/7, contact list in appendix.
- Escalation SLA: initial ack within 30 minutes, incident triage within 2 hours, mitigation plan within 8 hours for batched pool incidents that exceed threshold.

5) Legal redline placement
- Put batched exemption into Appendix B with cross-reference to Section 2.1 (Service definitions) and Section 7 (Remedies). We will propose precise language in RDW_SLA_BatchedOffset_v2.pdf.

Attachments to send to customer: RDW_SLA_BatchedOffset_v2.pdf (redline + toy-month worksheet).

If that aligns, I'll push the redline and Mateo can share the toy-month calculations and the tagging/telemetry extract. Laura — any flags from legal on the early termination phrasing?

Thanks,
Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Director, Commercial Contracts | Redwood Inference
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@aetherdata.com>, Tom Kwan <tom.kwan@aetherdata.com>, procurement@aetherdata.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Batched inference: uptime/credit tradeoff proposal

Emma / Tom / Procurement team — thanks for the quick review and checklist. Short answers and next steps below. I've included Marissa and Laura on follow-up redlines.

Attribution & measurement: we will:
- Tag requests with rdw.route_pool=batched (logs + telemetry).
- Provide a daily incident export (first 90 days) with timestamps, affected route, and sample request ids.

Toy-month example (concrete numbers):
- Monthly invoice: $500,000
- Incident: batched pool experienced intermittent degradation over 7.2 hours affecting ~2% of requests (p95 spikes).
- Standard credit calculation (example language): credit = 1.25% of monthly spend = $6,250.
- With agreed 40% multiplier reduction: payable = $3,750.
- Monthly cap remains 10% ($50,000).

Rollback clause: we can accept Aether's ask for a 14-day early termination right during the pilot if SLA variance > 0.5% vs forecast, provided we have a 14-day remediation window and a joint review call. We'll add explicit language to the appendix.

Escalations: Redwood SRE ack within 30 min, triage within 2 hours, mitigation plan within 8 hours. Primary on-call contact and a dedicated Slack channel will be created for the pilot.

Next steps:
1) Marissa will attach RDW_SLA_BatchedOffset_v2.pdf with redline and toy-month worksheet (Marissa: please attach).
2) If Aether agrees to the 40% multiplier reduction and the 14-day pilot termination carveout, we'll move an amendment to DocuSign and target signature before pilot kick-off (target date: 2026-12-01).

Emma — can you confirm procurement's agreement on the multiplier and termination language so we can circulate the redline? Once confirmed, I'll send the DocuSign link and schedule a 30-minute handoff call with SREs.

Thanks,
Mateo

Attachments: RDW_SLA_BatchedOffset_v2.pdf (proposed redline + example worksheet)

--
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com

From: Suresh Gupta <suresh.gupta@lumenforge.com>

Cc: network-team@lumenforge.com
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: LumenForge private deployment — preflight items and kickoff

Hi Mateo,

Subject: Re: LumenForge private deployment — preflight items and kickoff

Hi Suresh — thanks for the note and the attachment. We're excited to get this moving. Quick summary of what we'll cover in the preflight and the high-level asks from our side: 

- Required network items: preferred VPC peer CIDRs (we recommend non-overlapping /16 or /20 blocks), a list of application and management CIDRs, and your NAT/egress IPs for allowlisting.
- Ports: primary service 443 (TLS) for private endpoints; ephemeral ranges for health checks and KV sync (we usually recommend 32768-61000 for ephemeral, but can restrict on request).
- DNS: private FQDN pattern (we create redwood-internal.<customer>.svc.<region> for endpoint SRV records) — we need zone delegation or conditional forwarders for the private zone.
- On-prem package: we support a signed tarball and an install manifest (attached draft: onprem-manifest-v0.3.xlsx). For air-gapped installs we provide signed artifacts plus an MD5/SHA256 checksum file and an install bootstrap script; transfer via your secure media is fine.

source.subject: BlueHarbor -- peaking contours & inference fit (tokens/sec -> Dedicated sizing)
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-12T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: BlueHarbor: peaking contours & quick ask for Dedicated sizing

Folks — looping you on BlueHarbor (BH-2026-058). They gave us a traffic sketch (attached) and want a firm recommendation for a Dedicated slab they can commit to for Q1 onboarding. Key ask: can we map their committed tokens/sec + bursting behavior to GPU count and demonstrate p95 SLO fit for the suggested model family + a fallback path.

Quick context:
- Primary model suggestion from solutions: MPT-7B quantized (low-cost baseline). Backup is Llama2-13B-quant (higher quality).
- Customer-provided average concurrency: 120 req/s, median generation length ~150 tokens. They expect predictable latency p95 <= 250ms for single-turn generations; committed token baseline they are comfortable with is ~20k tps, but they want headroom for peaks.

Please produce a short run-of-numbers (assumptions + mapping to GPUs, headroom margin, burst slack and an ops recommendation: dedicated slab size, burst/auto-scale ceiling, fallback routing). Attachments: blueharbor_mvp_traffic_estim.xlsx (traffic sheet). Can we turn around by EOD PST?

Thanks,
Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-12T12:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BlueHarbor: peaking contours & quick ask for Dedicated sizing

On Thu, Nov 12, 2026 at 9:15 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Folks — looping you on BlueHarbor (BH-2026-058). They gave us a traffic sketch (attached) and want a firm recommendation for a Dedicated slab they can commit to for Q1 onboarding. Key ask: can we map their committed tokens/sec + bursting behavior to GPU count and demonstrate p95 SLO fit for the suggested model family + a fallback path.
>
> Quick context:
> - Primary model suggestion from solutions: MPT-7B quantized (low-cost baseline). Backup is Llama2-13B-quant (higher quality).
> - Customer-provided average concurrency: 120 req/s, median generation length ~150 tokens. They expect predictable latency p95 <= 250ms for single-turn generations; committed token baseline they are comfortable with is ~20k tps, but they want headroom for peaks.

Matt (ops) — short run below. Assumptions first, then mapping. I'm keeping it conservative: we assume A100-80GB class GPU. If customer runs on other sku, numbers will change.

Assumptions (baseline):
- Average requests/sec (steady): 120 req/s
- Median generation length: 150 tokens
- Baseline committed token target: 20,000 tokens/sec
- Peak observed bursts: up to 2x sustained for short windows -> ~36k tps
- Per-GPU sustained throughput (batching + prefix caching applied):
  - MPT-7B (int8 / 4-bit quantized profile): ~4,000 tokens/sec/GPU at our typical batching distribution
  - Llama2-13B (8-bit/quantized): ~1,600 tokens/sec/GPU
- P95 generation latency budget target: <= 250ms (end-to-end server response for 150 tokens)

Sizing math (high-level):
- Steady tokens/sec = 120 * 150 = 18,000 tps (close to customer baseline)
- With baseline committed 20k tps, steady-state demand ≈ 20k tps
- Peak (2x) = 36k tps

GPU mapping (MPT-7B primary):
- Steady GPUs needed = ceil(20,000 / 4,000) = 5 GPUs
- Peak GPUs needed = ceil(36,000 / 4,000) = 9 GPUs
- Add operational headroom (buffer for model warmup, uneven batching, and maintenance): +20% -> round up
  - Steady w/headroom = 6 GPUs
  - Peak w/headroom = 11 GPUs

Recommendation (Dedicated slab):
- Offer a reserved slab of 12 A100-class GPUs as the committed pool. Rationale: covers peak+20% headroom and gives a 1-GPU spare for maintenance/offline work.
- Autoscale ceiling / burst capacity: allow burst up to 16 GPUs via burst pool (pay-as-you-burst). This covers unexpected 2.5x bursts for a short window without SLO collapse.

Latency and SLO fit notes:
- Estimated p95 for 150-token generation on MPT-7B with 4k tps/GPU: ~120-180ms (depends on batching and KV cache hit rate). That comfortably fits the 250ms p95 target with current headroom.
- If customer upgrades quality to Llama2-13B, per-GPU throughput drops (~1.6k tps). For the same steady 20k tokens/sec we'd need ~13 GPUs (20k/1.6k ≈ 12.5) and peak with 2x burst ≈ 25 GPUs -> operationally expensive. So strategy should be: serve baseline with MPT-7B and route premium requests to Llama2-13B under budgeted fraction or during non-peak windows.

Operational recommendations:
- Canary the Dedicated slab with gradual traffic (10% -> 30% -> 100%) over 48–72 hours.
- Enable prefix caching for known repeated prompts (we saw ~15% repeat patterns in their sample) to reduce tokens served during warm periods.
- Rollout policy: primary MPT-7B dedicated pool; fallback policy to hosted Llama2-13B instances (or dedicated smaller slab) for quality-critical routes.
- Throttling policy: configure a soft throttle at 2x committed baseline and hard throttle at 3x to avoid noisy neighbor effects.

I'll drop a short markdown writeup and an ops playbook (attached: bh_sizing_notes_v1.md). If you want, I can run a more granular simulation (per-minute poisson bursts) tomorrow and provide GPU-hour cost vs committed-price delta for the customer.

Karthik
Senior Infra Eng, Redwood Inference
From: Elena Rossi <elena@blueharbor.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
Cc: procure@blueharbor.ai
Date: 2026-11-13T08:17:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BlueHarbor: peaking contours & inference fit (tokens/sec -> Dedicated sizing)

Thanks Mateo / Karthik — quick clarifying questions from our side: 
1) For the 12-GPU committed slab you recommended, how will short spikes (2.5x for 3–5 minutes) be handled without violating our p95? Will burst capacity automatically spin up, and how long is the warm-up?
2) You mentioned fallback routing to Llama2-13B for higher quality. Isn't 13B slower and therefore riskier for p95? We need best-effort quality on some endpoints but strict latency on others.

We want simple language in the contract: a committed slab size and an optional burst cushion. Can you also add a cost delta for the 12-GPU reserved slab vs a 16-GPU burst-capable configuration (Ballpark hourly $/GPU acceptable).

—Elena
Head of Platform, BlueHarbor AI
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@blueharbor.ai>, procure@blueharbor.ai
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-13T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BlueHarbor: peaking contours & inference fit (tokens/sec -> Dedicated sizing)

Elena — thanks for the questions. Short answers + action items below.

Q1) Handling 2.5x short spikes:
- Burst model: Dedicated slab (12 GPUs) will serve steady traffic. For short spikes that exceed slab capacity we have two levers: 1) automatic burst capacity (6 GPU burst pool in our proposal, adjustable) and 2) routing to hosted spare capacity (we keep a shared pool that responds quickly).
- Warm-up: spinning up a new GPU-backed instance for cold model load typically takes ~45–90s (depends on model and KV/Warm caches). To reduce effective warm-up latency we recommend: pre-warmed standby instances (fraction of burst pool kept warm) and aggressive prefix/KV cache population during predicted peak windows. With 2 pre-warmed GPUs you can shave warm-up cost and keep p95 within target for short bursts.

Q2) Fallback to Llama2-13B for quality-critical requests:
- You're right: 13B has lower per-GPU throughput and can push p95 higher if used as a drop-in replacement. Our intent was not to use 13B as a blanket fallback for latency-sensitive endpoints. Instead:
  - Route quality-critical, non-latency-sensitive endpoints to Llama2-13B (hosted or a separate dedicated pool sized for smaller fractions of traffic).
  - Keep latency-critical endpoints on MPT-7B; for premium quality on those endpoints we can explore prefix expansion or hybrid inference (MPT for draft, Llama2-13B for finalization) but that increases token counts and cost.

Cost-ballpark (illustrative):
- Reserved A100-class GPU (12 GPUs) committed: $X/hr per GPU (commit discounts apply). Estimated committed hourly = 12 * $3.50/hr (example committed rate) = $42/hr. (We will provide firm pricing in contract).
- Burst-capable (16 GPUs available with 4 as burst on-demand): committed 12 + on-demand 4 at $8/hr each = $42/hr + 4*$8 = $74/hr when fully bursting.

Action items: Karthik / Kimberly — can you: 1) produce a 1-page cost table (committed vs committed+burst) with a sensitivity to different peak multipliers (1.5x, 2x, 2.5x) and 2) a minute-level simulation for a 48-hour window showing p95 projections under the recommended slab + 2 pre-warmed GPUs? Mateo will share the materials with Elena and Procure following that.

Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Subject: BlueHarbor: peaking contours & quick ask for Dedicated sizing

Folks — looping you on BlueHarbor (BH-2026-058). They gave us a traffic sketch (attached) and want a firm recommendation for a Dedicated slab they can commit to for Q1 onboarding. Key ask: can we map their committed tokens/sec + bursting behavior to GPU count and demonstrate p95 SLO fit for the suggested model family + a fallback path.

Quick context:
- Primary model suggestion from solutions: MPT-7B quantized (low-cost baseline). Backup is Llama2-13B-quant (higher quality).
- Customer-provided average concurrency: 120 req/s, median generation length ~150 tokens. They expect predictable latency p95 <= 250ms for single-turn generations; committed token baseline they are comfortable with is ~20k tps, but they want headroom for peaks.

Please produce a short run-of-numbers (assumptions + mapping to GPUs, headroom margin, burst slack and an ops recommendation: dedicated slab size, burst/auto-scale ceiling, fallback routing). Attachments: blueharbor_mvp_traffic_estim.xlsx (traffic sheet). Can we turn around by EOD PST?

Thanks,
Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-12T12:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: BlueHarbor: peaking contours & quick ask for Dedicated sizing

On Thu, Nov 12, 2026 at 9:15 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Folks — looping you on BlueHarbor (BH-2026-058). They gave us a traffic sketch (attached) and want a firm recommendation for a Dedicated slab they can commit to for Q1 onboarding. Key ask: can we map their committed tokens/sec + bursting behavior to GPU count and demonstrate p95 SLO fit for the suggested model family + a fallback path.
>
> Quick context:
> - Primary model suggestion from solutions: MPT-7B quantized (low-cost baseline). Backup is Llama2-13B-quant (higher quality).
> - Customer-provided average concurrency: 120 req/s, median generation length ~150 tokens. They expect predictable latency p95 <= 250ms for single-turn generations; committed token baseline they are comfortable with is ~20k tps, but they want headroom for peaks.

Matt (ops) — short run below. Assumptions first, then mapping. I'm keeping it conservative: we assume A100-80GB class GPU. If customer runs on other sku, numbers will change.

Assumptions (baseline):
- Average requests/sec (steady): 120 req/s
- Median generation length: 150 tokens
- Baseline committed token target: 20,000 tokens/sec
- Peak observed bursts: up to 2x sustained for short windows -> ~36k tps
- Per-GPU sustained throughput (batching + prefix caching applied):
  - MPT-7B (int8 / 4-bit quantized profile): ~4,000 tokens/sec/GPU at our typical batching distribution
  - Llama2-13B (8-bit/quantized): ~1,600 tokens/sec/GPU
- P95 generation latency budget target: <= 250ms (end-to-end server response for 150 tokens)

Sizing math (high-level):
- Steady tokens/sec = 120 * 150 = 18,000 tps (close to customer baseline)
- With baseline committed 20k tps, steady-state demand ≈ 20k tps
- Peak (2x) = 36k tps

GPU mapping (MPT-7B primary):
- Steady GPUs needed = ceil(20,000 / 4,000) = 5 GPUs
- Peak GPUs needed = ceil(36,000 / 4,000) = 9 GPUs
- Add operational headroom (buffer for model warmup, uneven batching, and maintenance): +20% -> round up
  - Steady w/headroom = 6 GPUs
  - Peak w/headroom = 11 GPUs

Recommendation (Dedicated slab):
- Offer a reserved slab of 12 A100-class GPUs as the committed pool. Rationale: covers peak+20% headroom and gives a 1-GPU spare for maintenance/offline work.
- Autoscale ceiling / burst capacity: allow burst up to 16 GPUs via burst pool (pay-as-you-burst). This covers unexpected 2.5x bursts for a short window without SLO collapse.

Latency and SLO fit notes:
- Estimated p95 for 150-token generation on MPT-7B with 4k tps/GPU: ~120-180ms (depends on batching and KV cache hit rate). That comfortably fits the 250ms p95 target with current headroom.
- If customer upgrades quality to Llama2-13B, per-GPU throughput drops (~1.6k tps). For the same steady 20k tokens/sec we'd need ~13 GPUs (20k/1.6k ≈ 12.5) and peak with 2x burst ≈ 25 GPUs -> operationally expensive. So strategy should be: serve baseline with MPT-7B and route premium requests to Llama2-13B under budgeted fraction or during non-peak windows.

Operational recommendations:
- Canary the Dedicated slab with gradual traffic (10% -> 30% -> 100%) over 48–72 hours.
- Enable prefix caching for known repeated prompts (we saw ~15% repeat patterns in their sample) to reduce tokens served during warm periods.
- Rollout policy: primary MPT-7B dedicated pool; fallback policy to hosted Llama2-13B instances (or dedicated smaller slab) for quality-critical routes.
- Throttling policy: configure a soft throttle at 2x committed baseline and hard throttle at 3x to avoid noisy neighbor effects.

I'll drop a short markdown writeup and an ops playbook (attached: bh_sizing_notes_v1.md). If you want, I can run a more granular simulation (per-minute poisson bursts) tomorrow and provide GPU-hour cost vs committed-price delta for the customer.

Karthik
Senior Infra Eng, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@curahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire

Hi Sarah —

Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can keep your security team moving: 

1) SOC 2: You asked about our Type II status. We are SOC 2 Type II in scope for our serving runtime and control plane. I attached a one-page summary of the scope and what we can provide under NDA.

2) Retention / deletion SLAs: We discussed retention of logs, snapshots, and customer data. Attached is our retention matrix (redwood-retention-matrix.xlsx) showing default retention windows and where we can support shorter/longer retention per contract. A few quick notes:
   - Application request logs (high-frequency): default 90 days, configurable per-customer up to 365 days for Dedicated.
   - KV/cache snapshots: retained for 7 days by default; longer archival available on request.
   - Backups (cold): retained 30/90/365-day policies depending on plan; snapshots are encrypted at rest.

3) Deletion / Erasure: Our standard SLA for issuing a deletion confirmation and associated audit evidence is 30 days for account-level erasures; expedited 7-day workflow available for enterprise customers with contractual SLA. We can surface deletion audit logs showing successful erasure and related snapshot expirations.

4) Vendor questionnaire: You mentioned a vendor questionnaire. Please send it over — I attached a placeholder we use to route answers internally. If you can share the CuraHealth questionnaire we’ll populate and return it within 3 business days for the security team to review.

Next steps: 
- Sarah: share the CuraHealth_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.docx when you have a moment.
- Mateo/Redwood: Ben and Olga will collate the SOC 2 proof and retention evidence and send back a completed questionnaire + supporting artifacts. Aim: deliverables within 3 business days.

Attaching: redwood-retention-matrix.xlsx, redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (summary only; full report available under NDA)

Cheers,

Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com
[Attachment: redwood-retention-matrix.xlsx (XLSX, 28KB)]
[Attachment: redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (PDF, 175KB)]

From: Sarah Li <sarah.li@curahealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: CuraHealth Security <security@curahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 15:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire

Thanks Mateo — appreciate the clear recap.

Attaching our standard vendor questionnaire (DOCX) and a quick note from our security lead: we need visibility into log retention for access logs and admin activity (what you call "application request logs"), evidence of deletion (both object and snapshot), and confirmation that backups are encrypted and subject to the same deletion policies.

Our security team also asked: can you provide an example deletion-audit export (redacted) so we can see the fields and timestamps we would receive? If not, a CSV sample is fine.

I'll upload the questionnaire to our shared drive as well but attaching here: CuraHealth_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.docx

Thanks — Sarah
Sarah Li
Product & Partnerships 
CuraHealth
[Attachment: CuraHealth_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.docx (DOCX, 62KB)]
