> Hi Sarah —
> 
> Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can keep your security team moving: 
> ...

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 15:45:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire

Ben / Olga — forwarding CuraHealth's questionnaire and Sarah's note. Key asks:

- Provide SOC 2 evidence we can share under NDA (Ben: can you confirm which artifacts we can hand over? E.g., SOC 2 Type II report, bridge letters, scope map).
- Produce a redacted sample deletion-audit export (CSV) that shows timestamps, object id, requester, result, and snapshot references. Sarah specifically called out needing to see the fields.
- Confirm default retention windows in the retention matrix are accurate and call out where we can offer a 7-day expedited erasure SLA (contractual).

Please use the attached questionnaire and return the completed DOCX and evidence to me — I will package and send to Sarah. Target: end of day Monday (2026-12-13). If any item requires more time, flag it here and give an ETA.

Mateo

[Forwarded attachment: CuraHealth_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.docx]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Dec 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire

Thanks for the forward — pulling the artifacts together now. Summary of what we can provide and proposed answers to the CuraHealth questionnaire (high level):

1) SOC 2 evidence: We can provide the SOC 2 Type II report under NDA and a scope map PDF. We'll include a short cover note explaining which services are in-scope (serving runtime, control plane).

2) Deletion audit sample: attached redwood-delta-log-samples.csv (redacted) — includes columns: event_id, object_type, object_id (hashed), requestor_role, action, timestamp_utc, result, snapshot_ref. This is a synthetic but realistic sample to demonstrate fields; we will mark any PII as redacted.

3) Retention & backups: Confirming the retention matrix numbers: request logs default 90 days (configurable), admin logs 365 days for enterprise, backups (cold) retained per plan (30/90/365). Snapshots are retained 7 days by default; we can extend via contractual terms. All backups are encrypted at rest (AES-256) and keys are managed via KMS.

4) Deletion SLA: Standard confirmation delivery = 30 days. For CuraHealth enterprise agreement we can commit to a 7-day deletion confirmation SLA and provide the deletion audit CSV as evidence. Note: snapshot TTL expiration is the mechanism we use to purge historical cache/snapshots; we will include a short diagram in our response about how snapshots are expired and how that links to deletion evidence.

5) Next steps & timeline:
   - Today (Fri 2026-12-11): attach sample CSV and draft answers to questionnaire (attached).
   - Monday EOD (2026-12-13): assemble SOC 2 proof under NDA, final questionnaire, and retention doc.

Attaching: redwood-delta-log-samples.csv, draft_CuraHealth_questionnaire_answers.docx

title: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@curahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire

Hi Sarah —

Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can keep your security team moving: 

1) SOC 2: You asked about our Type II status. We are SOC 2 Type II in scope for our serving runtime and control plane. I attached a one-page summary of the scope and what we can provide under NDA.

2) Retention / deletion SLAs: We discussed retention of logs, snapshots, and customer data. Attached is our retention matrix (redwood-retention-matrix.xlsx) showing default retention windows and where we can support shorter/longer retention per contract. A few quick notes:
   - Application request logs (high-frequency): default 90 days, configurable per-customer up to 365 days for Dedicated.
   - KV/cache snapshots: retained for 7 days by default; longer archival available on request.
   - Backups (cold): retained 30/90/365-day policies depending on plan; snapshots are encrypted at rest.

3) Deletion / Erasure: Our standard SLA for issuing a deletion confirmation and associated audit evidence is 30 days for account-level erasures; expedited 7-day workflow available for enterprise customers with contractual SLA. We can surface deletion audit logs showing successful erasure and related snapshot expirations.

4) Vendor questionnaire: You mentioned a vendor questionnaire. Please send it over — I attached a placeholder we use to route answers internally. If you can share the CuraHealth questionnaire we’ll populate and return it within 3 business days for the security team to review.

Next steps: 
- Sarah: share the CuraHealth_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.docx when you have a moment.
- Mateo/Redwood: Ben and Olga will collate the SOC 2 proof and retention evidence and send back a completed questionnaire + supporting artifacts. Aim: deliverables within 3 business days.

Attaching: redwood-retention-matrix.xlsx, redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (summary only; full report available under NDA)

Cheers,

Mateo Alvarez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com
[Attachment: redwood-retention-matrix.xlsx (XLSX, 28KB)]
[Attachment: redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (PDF, 175KB)]

Our security team also asked: can you provide an example deletion-audit export (redacted) so we can see the fields and timestamps we would receive? If not, a CSV sample is fine.

Ben / Olga — forwarding CuraHealth's questionnaire and Sarah's note. Key asks:

- Provide SOC 2 evidence we can share under NDA (Ben: can you confirm which artifacts we can hand over? E.g., SOC 2 Type II report, bridge letters, scope map).
- Produce a redacted sample deletion-audit export (CSV) that shows timestamps, object id, requester, result, and snapshot references. Sarah specifically called out needing to see the fields.
- Confirm default retention windows in the retention matrix are accurate and call out where we can offer a 7-day expedited erasure SLA (contractual).

Please use the attached questionnaire and return the completed DOCX and evidence to me — I will package and send to Sarah. Target: end of day Monday (2026-12-13). If any item requires more time, flag it here and give an ETA.

Mateo

[Forwarded attachment: CuraHealth_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.docx]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Dec 2026 10:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-call: data retention / deletion SLAs + vendor questionnaire

Thanks for the forward — pulling the artifacts together now. Summary of what we can provide and proposed answers to the CuraHealth questionnaire (high level):

1) SOC 2 evidence: We can provide the SOC 2 Type II report under NDA and a scope map PDF. We'll include a short cover note explaining which services are in-scope (serving runtime, control plane).

2) Deletion audit sample: attached redwood-delta-log-samples.csv (redacted) — includes columns: event_id, object_type, object_id (hashed), requestor_role, action, timestamp_utc, result, snapshot_ref. This is a synthetic but realistic sample to demonstrate fields; we will mark any PII as redacted.

3) Retention & backups: Confirming the retention matrix numbers: request logs default 90 days (configurable), admin logs 365 days for enterprise, backups (cold) retained per plan (30/90/365). Snapshots are retained 7 days by default; we can extend via contractual terms. All backups are encrypted at rest (AES-256) and keys are managed via KMS.

4) Deletion SLA: Standard confirmation delivery = 30 days. For CuraHealth enterprise agreement we can commit to a 7-day deletion confirmation SLA and provide the deletion audit CSV as evidence. Note: snapshot TTL expiration is the mechanism we use to purge historical cache/snapshots; we will include a short diagram in our response about how snapshots are expired and how that links to deletion evidence.

5) Next steps & timeline:
   - Today (Fri 2026-12-11): attach sample CSV and draft answers to questionnaire (attached).
   - Monday EOD (2026-12-13): assemble SOC 2 proof under NDA, final questionnaire, and retention doc.

Attaching: redwood-delta-log-samples.csv, draft_CuraHealth_questionnaire_answers.docx

If you want me to redact anything differently for legal, loop in Olga for a quick pass — Olga, can you review the SOC 2 scope blurb and the deletion SLA language?

Ben Carter

ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

source.subject: Coord: Demo slot swap during conference week — alternative times
messages:
From: Carmen Soto <carmen@finbridge.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.procurement@finbridge.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood demo timing (re:Re:Invent week)

Hi Mateo —

Thanks for the quick response on the RFP. We're excited to see Redwood in action, but heads up: our team is heavily distributed that week and several folks are at re:Invent and RSA on different days.

Current asks: can we move the demo off Wednesday (11/10) to earlier in the week or schedule two shorter blocks so timezone overlap is easier? Our procurement lead (Tom) prefers a morning PST slot, but engineering would only be available late EMEA morning (which is late PST evening).

I attached a short agenda and the list of stakeholders. Ideally we confirm before Friday so Tom can kick off contract paperwork.

Thanks,
Carmen Soto
Director of Product
FinBridge

Attachment: finbridge-demo-agenda.pdf (application/pdf)
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 11:12:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Redwood demo timing (re:Re:Invent week)

Vivek / Priya — forwarding Carmen. FinBridge is a mid-tier payments prospect moving quickly (deal RBW-1098-FB). They flagged re:Invent/RSA overlap and want either an earlier week slot or split sessions to accommodate PST procurement + EMEA engineering.

Quick thoughts:
- Option A: two focused sessions (30m PST morning for procurement + 45m late EMEA morning for engineering deep dive). Keeps sessions targeted and reduces cross-timezone fatigue.
- Option B: single 60m session at 08:30 PST which hits Tom and is a late afternoon for EMEA (not ideal but doable).

Can Priya run the technical double-session if we go with Option A? Vivek, can you confirm if sales leadership wants to be on the procurement call or just loop in for contract discussion?

I'll propose two tentative slots to Carmen after we confirm internal coverage.

— Mateo
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
m: +1 415 555 0123
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 08:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Redwood demo timing (re:Re:Invent week)

Sounds good — I can own the EMEA engineering session.

I ran availability with the solutions team and can do:
- Wed 11/10 07:30–08:00 PST (Procurement-focused 30m) — covers Tom (PST morning)
- Thu 11/11 16:00–16:45 PST (EMEA morning / NA evening) — deep dive and Q&A for engineers

If Carmen prefers a single meeting, I can compress whiteboarding material into a single 60m slot on Thu 11/11 at 08:30 PST, but that will mean some LATE time for our EMEA folks.

Also adding a short pre-demo checklist:
- Confirm data residency question (Carmen asked about audit logs)
- Bring sample SSO flow for procurement
- Send calendar invite + ICS so attendee timezones are clear

I'll draft an agenda and share the ICS once we pick.

— Priya
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Carmen Soto <carmen@finbridge.com>, Tom Alvarez <tom.procurement@finbridge.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 16:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood demo timing (re:Re:Invent week)

Carmen / Tom — thanks for the details and flexibility. Based on internal availability we propose the split-session approach to avoid the re:Invent blackout and reduce cross-timezone friction. Proposed schedule below (attached ICS):

1) Procurement + overview (30m) — Tue 11/16 08:00–08:30 PST (11/16 11:00–11:30 EST)
   - Attendees: Tom, Carmen, Vivek (AE)
   - Focus: pricing, contract milestones, SSO notes

2) Engineering deep dive (45m) — Thu 11/18 16:00–16:45 PST (Fri 11/19 00:00–00:45 CET)
   - Attendees: Carmen, FinBridge engineers, Priya (SE)
   - Focus: latency benchmarks, caching/quantization options, rollout plan

If those look OK I'll send calendar invites (ICS attached) and a short joint agenda. If you prefer sooner, we can try the compressed 60m slot on 11/11 at 08:30 PST, but several of our EMEA folks will be joining outside normal hours.

Next steps: please reply with the preferred option by EOD Wednesday so we can lock rooms and start the mutual action plan.

Thanks — looking forward to demoing the latency improvements for streaming responses.

— Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: 20261109-demo-invite.ics (text/calendar), finbridge-demo-agenda.pdf (application/pdf)

   - Attendees: Tom, Carmen, Vivek (AE)
   - Focus: pricing, contract milestones, SSO notes

2) Engineering deep dive (45m) — Thu 11/18 16:00–16:45 PST (Fri 11/19 00:00–00:45 CET)
   - Attendees: Carmen, FinBridge engineers, Priya (SE)
   - Focus: latency benchmarks, caching/quantization options, rollout plan

If those look OK I'll send calendar invites (ICS attached) and a short joint agenda. If you prefer sooner, we can try the compressed 60m slot on 11/11 at 08:30 PST, but several of our EMEA folks will be joining outside normal hours.

Next steps: please reply with the preferred option by EOD Wednesday so we can lock rooms and start the mutual action plan.

Thanks — looking forward to demoing the latency improvements for streaming responses.

— Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: 20261109-demo-invite.ics (text/calendar), finbridge-demo-agenda.pdf (application/pdf)

source.subject: Clarify invoicing mechanics & liability scope for reseller addendum
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Reseller addendum — invoicing mechanics & liability carveouts (need redlines)

Team,

CloudReseller just sent back the partner paper with a few sticking points we need legal to sign off on before we push the addendum to signature. I attached the current draft (v3) and their tax matrix for reference. High level asks from their procurement:

- They want marketplace fees to be listed as line items on their monthly invoice (they currently receive consolidated cloud bills and need pass-through visibility).
- They requested that Redwood bill the end-customer directly and that CloudReseller be a conduit only — they want no separate invoices from Redwood to Reseller.
- Liability cap: they pushed for a mutual cap at 24 months of fees; they also want carveouts for IP and gross negligence removed.
- Tax remit: they want Redwood to be responsible for withholding taxes in certain APAC countries (see matrix).

Can legal propose redline language that accomplishes the following (priority order):
1) Clear invoicing path we can operationalize (who invoices whom, what appears on the invoice, and how pass-through fees are shown).
2) Reasonable liability cap and carveouts that protect Redwood (especially for third-party claims tied to model outputs).
3) Clarify tax pass-through responsibilities with a fallback for unknown jurisdictions.

Karthik / Marissa — please confirm what we can operationally support for billing (monthly consolidated invoicing vs. per-customer line items) so Kimberly has the right constraints.

Attachments: Redwood-Reseller-Addendum-draft-v3.pdf (redline-ready), CloudReseller_CountryTaxMatrix.xlsx (procurement sheet).

Thanks,

— Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0134

From: Elena Martin <elena.martin@cloudreseller.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@cloudreseller.com
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 10:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Reseller addendum — invoicing mechanics & liability carveouts (need redlines)

Hi Mateo — thanks for the context and the draft. A couple quick clarifications from our side: 

1) Invoicing: we cannot accept Redwood invoicing each end-customer separately. Our finance team requires a single monthly invoice from CloudReseller with discrete line items showing the Redwood marketplace fee per customer so we can trace refunds and chargebacks. If Redwood can provide a CSV export and an invoice line mapping we can ingest, that solves the visibility requirement without changing our AP flows.

2) Billing relationship: we are open to an arrangement where Redwood invoices CloudReseller and CloudReseller invoices its customers, but we need a published SLA for dispute investigation and a two-tier dispute resolution process (30 days for first pass, 90 days for final).

3) Liability cap: 24 months of fees is our standard in the reseller agreements. Moving to 12 months will be a hard sell to our CFO. If you must limit carveouts, we would accept mutual carveouts for gross negligence and will not accept IP carveouts — IP indemnities must be mutual.

4) Taxes: attached matrix is accurate — in a few APAC countries the reseller is not allowed to collect VAT on behalf of a foreign supplier; Redwood will need to accept responsibility there.

We can live with a short negotiation if legal can get us proposed wording this week; procurement needs the final by next Friday to hit our quarter close.

Best,

Elena Martin
Head of Procurement
CloudReseller Inc.
elena.martin@cloudreseller.com

On Thu, Nov 5, 2026 at 9:12 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team,
> 
> CloudReseller just sent back the partner paper with a few sticking points we need legal to sign off on before we push the addendum to signature...

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Martin <elena.martin@cloudreseller.com>, procurement@cloudreseller.com
Date: Mon, 8 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Reseller addendum — invoicing mechanics & liability carveouts (need redlines)

Thanks Mateo — reviewed the thread and attached the proposed redline (summary below). I copied CloudReseller to be transparent about changes we plan to propose.

Proposed approach (high level):
- Invoicing mechanics: Redwood will invoice CloudReseller monthly for aggregate marketplace usage. The invoice will include a machine-readable CSV (delivered via SFTP or secure link) containing per-customer line items (customer id, usage units, marketplace fee). CloudReseller may use that CSV to generate their consolidated invoice to end-customers. We added an "Invoice Data Delivery" section requiring delivery within 5 business days of invoice issuance.

- Billing relationship: We will keep Redwood as the billing party to the Reseller (CloudReseller). We will not directly invoice end-customers. This preserves our accounting and tax obligations and aligns with our revenue recognition.

- Liability cap: Offer a mutual liability cap at the greater of (i) $1,000,000 or (ii) 12 months of Redwood fees paid by CloudReseller under the applicable order form in the prior 12 months. We will maintain carveouts for (a) IP infringement, (b) willful misconduct, and (c) personal data breaches where Redwood is at fault. For third-party claims tied to model outputs we will add explicit notice-and-cure obligations and a limitation on consequential damages.

- Taxes: Add a table-based responsibility matrix (see attached) with a fallback clause: "If a tax authority requires Redwood to withhold or collect, Redwood shall notify CloudReseller within 10 business days and the parties will cooperate to minimize duplication; absent legal compulsion, tax collection and remittance for local indirect taxes shall be the responsibility of the receiving party as indicated in the matrix."

Next steps:
1) Mateo / Karthik: confirm SFTP or secure link is operationally acceptable.
2) Marissa: confirm finance can support CSV mapping and timing.
3) If CloudReseller accepts the above framework, Kimberly will circulate a marked PDF with tracked changes (v4) by EOB tomorrow.

Elena / procurement — let us know if the mutual cap and data delivery approach are a showstopper. If CloudReseller insists on Redwood invoicing end-customers directly, we need to surface that to revenue & tax for an assessment.

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Commercial
Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0178

On Fri, Nov 6, 2026 at 10:15 AM Elena Martin <elena.martin@cloudreseller.com> wrote:
> Hi Mateo — thanks for the context and the draft. A couple quick clarifications from our side: ...

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Martin <elena.martin@cloudreseller.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, procurement@cloudreseller.com
Date: Tue, 9 Nov 2026 15:23:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Reseller addendum — invoicing mechanics & liability carveouts (need redlines)

Elena — thanks for the quick turnaround and procurement timeline. Per Kimberly's note below, the team is aligned on the following operationally feasible compromises: 

- Redwood will invoice CloudReseller (not end-customers). Each monthly invoice will include a downloadable CSV (SFTP/secure link) with per-customer line items that CloudReseller can ingest. Karthik confirms SFTP is available; Marissa will confirm the CSV column mapping by EOD tomorrow.
- Liability cap: Kimberly's proposed mutual cap (greater of $1M or 12 months' fees) is our starting point. If CloudReseller needs 24 months for CFO sign-off, we can offer a stretch to 18 months for strategic deals, but we prefer 12 months for standard addenda.
- Taxes: Redwood will accept responsibility where local law requires supplier withholding; otherwise taxes will follow the matrix and the receiving party is responsible. We will add the notice-and-cooperate clause Kimberly proposed.

Next steps and asks: 
1) Kimberly: please circulate v4 (tracked) so procurement can review with their CFO.
2) Elena / procurement: confirm whether CloudReseller can accept the CSV + SFTP approach and whether 18 months (stretch) vs. 24 months is a hard requirement for signing this quarter.
3) I'll open a HubSpot note (linked) and attach the doc for legal ops to push to DocuSign after approvals.

If procurement wants a call, happy to schedule a 30-min triage with revenue/tax/legal this week.

Cheers,

— Mateo Alvarez
Senior Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0134

Attachments: Redwood-Reseller-Addendum-draft-v4-tracked.pdf (to follow), CloudReseller_CountryTaxMatrix.xlsx

source.subject: Remittance ID mapper & bank proof — kickoff
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@clearlinepay.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 07 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Remittance ID mapper & bank proof — kickoff

Hi Sanjay —

Thanks again for choosing Redwood. To get you into our payment system and make sure invoice remits link cleanly to POs, we need the completed vendor onboarding form + bank proof and a preferred remittance identifier format. Quick checklist below so we can move you to ACTIVE status: 

- Completed vendor onboarding form (signed PDF) — see attached stub.
- W-9 (or equivalent) — attached.
- Bank account details for ACH (routing + account) and wire fallback.
- Example of the remittance identifier you want us to include on payments (we’ll map this to your invoices).

Remit ID guidance: our accounting system accepts a 1–40 character free-form remittance field. Teams typically use one of these conventions:
  • INV-<PO#>-<SHORTVEND> (recommended for smaller partners)
  • VENDID:<vendor_id>|PO:<po_number> (structured, easier to parse)

If you don’t have a preference we’ll default to: RLW-<PO>-<VENDID>. Once we have the form + bank proof we’ll run a micro-deposit and share the amounts (micro-deposits are usually visible within 1–2 business days).

Please attach the completed PDF(s) or reply if you prefer to send via DocuSign — I included a link below for the standard onboarding packet.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Vendor Ops Lead | Redwood Inference
mateo@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0127

Attachments: clearline_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (PDF, filled), clearline_w9.pdf (PDF)
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@clearlinepay.com>

source.subject: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elise Turner <elise.turner@meridianlabs.com>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@meridianlabs.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-27T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs

Hi Elise / Sanjay —

Thanks again for the call this morning. As discussed, I pulled together an initial renewal proposal that aligns to Meridian’s expected product ramp over the next 18 months and includes an optional early upsize path if you accelerate deployment. High-level: 

- Term: 18 months (Nov 1, 2026 — Apr 30, 2028) with option to convert to a 36-month locked rate at the 12-month milestone.
- Capacity: baseline 200 GPU-hours/month, ramping to 1,200 GPU-hours/month by month 12 (milestone schedule in attached).
- Early upsize option: right to add incremental capacity at 60% of list for the first 90 days after signature (pre-provisioned burst pool).
- Co-terming: we can co-term this renewal to your existing data platform contract; if you choose co-term, we’ll pro-rate to align to the platform’s Feb 1 renewal date.
- Auto-renew: default 12-month auto-renew unless canceled with 60 days’ notice; we can change this to opt-in auto-renew per your procurement preference.
- Pricing: blended unit rate with an initial 10% retention discount and a volume ladder that reduces per-GPU cost at 500 / 900 / 1,200 thresholds.

Attached: Meridian-Renewal-Initial-Proposal.pdf (summary + milestone schedule) and usage-runbook.xlsx (projected token/GPU cadence).

Next steps: Can you confirm (1) whether you want co-terming with the platform contract, (2) whether Procurement needs a separate SOW or PO, and (3) your preferred sign date? If helpful, we can build a short amendment that shows both the 18-month path and the 36-month convert option side-by-side.

I’ll loop in Kimberly (Sales Ops) for proration/billing mechanics once you confirm co-term.

Best,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0129

Attachments: Meridian-Renewal-Initial-Proposal.pdf (PDF, 4 pages); usage-runbook.xlsx (Excel, forecast)

From: Elise Turner <elise.turner@meridianlabs.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: Sanjay Patel <sanjay@meridianlabs.com>, Finance Ops <ar@meridianlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-27T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs

Hi Mateo —

Thanks for the clear summary and the attachments. A few quick clarifying Qs from our side: 

1) Co-terming: we prefer co-terming to Feb 1, 2027 if possible. Will that change the upfront credit/proration materially?
2) Conversion to 36-month: can you confirm what the guaranteed rate would be at conversion and whether that requires an immediate one-time payment?
3) Early upsize: the 60% list sounds fair — can you confirm how quickly capacity can be provisioned (hours/days) if we trigger it?
4) Compliance: Procurement will request our SOC 2 and data retention answers again — can you point them to the right evidence bundle?

We’d like to get internal buy-in this week and would aim to sign the amendment next Tuesday (Nov 2) if numbers are OK.

— Elise
Elise Turner
Head of AI Platforms — Meridian Labs
elise.turner@meridianlabs.com

On Tue, Oct 27, 2026 at 09:14 AM Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original proposal attached]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-27T12:20:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs

Kim / Jordan / Priya —

Forwarding Elise’s reply. Needs: co-term proration to Feb 1, confirmation on convert-to-36mo mechanics, provisioning SLA for early upsize, and SOC2 evidence link for Procurement. Can Kimberly help with proration math and draft amendment? Jordan, can you confirm CS readiness to support a speedy upsize (how many nodes/days)? Priya, please tag security for the SOC2 bundle link.

Quoted for quick context:
> From: Elise Turner <elise.turner@meridianlabs.com>
> To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
> Date: 2026-10-27 11:02
> Subject: Re: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs
> 
> Hi Mateo —
> 
> Thanks for the clear summary and the attachments. A few quick clarifying Qs from our side: 
> 
> 1) Co-terming: we prefer co-terming to Feb 1, 2027 if possible. Will that change the upfront credit/proration materially?
> 2) Conversion to 36-month: can you confirm what the guaranteed rate would be at conversion and whether that requires an immediate one-time payment?
> 3) Early upsize: the 60% list sounds fair — can you confirm how quickly capacity can be provisioned (hours/days) if we trigger it?
> 4) Compliance: Procurement will request our SOC 2 and data retention answers again — can you point them to the right evidence bundle?

Thanks,
Elise

Thanks — Mateo

Attachments: Meridian-Renewal-Initial-Proposal.pdf; usage-runbook.xlsx

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-28T09:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs

Mateo —

Quick notes after review: 

- Co-term proration: If Meridian requests Feb 1, 2027 co-term, they will receive a pro-rated credit for Nov 1 — Jan 31 on the 18-month schedule; billing will be aligned to Feb 1 going forward. Net effect: ~2.9% additional prorate credit on Year 1 ARR (we calculated against the milestone ladder in the proposal).
- 36-month conversion: conversion is optional at the 12-month mark and carries a guaranteed blended rate that is 6% below the then-current list rate. No immediate payment required; conversion just re-amortizes remaining committed value over the new term. We should make conversion subject to a written amendment signed at the 12-month milestone.
- Early upsize provisioning: CS can provision burst capacity within 48 hours for standard builds; custom infra touches may take up to 5 business days. Jordan, flag me if we need pre-provisioned reserved nodes vs. on-demand bursting.
- Language: Procurement often asks to avoid automatic auto-renew; propose auto-renew default with a 60-day cancel window, or switch to opt-in auto-renew. Suggest we include both options in the amendment so they can choose.

I drafted an amendment template that shows both co-term and non-co-term flows and the conversion clause. See attachment: amendment-draft.docx.

I recommend we present two commercial paths to Meridian this week: (A) 18-month with early upsize + 10% retention discount (preferred), and (B) 36-month locked rate (lower unit price) but no early upsize entitlement. That gives Procurement a clear tradeoff.

If you want, I can jump on the call with Elise on Friday to walk Procurement through the proration math.

— Kim
Senior Sales Operations Manager — Redwood Inference

Attachments: amendment-draft.docx

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elise Turner <elise.turner@meridianlabs.com>, Sanjay Patel <sanjay@meridianlabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Finance Ops <ar@meridianlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-29T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal proposal: multi-year ramp + early upsize option for Meridian Labs

Hi Elise —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the co-term preference. Per Kim’s note (copied), here are the concrete options for Meridian to review — I’ve attached a one-page comparison too.

Option A — 18-month (default)- Baseline: 200 -> 1,200 GPU-hrs/mo over 12 months- Early upsize: incremental capacity at 60% of list for 90 days after signature; provisioning SLA 48 hours for standard stacks- Conversion: at month 12, option to convert remaining commitment into a 36-month lock with a guaranteed blended rate 6% below then-current list- Billing: co-term proration to Feb 1, 2027; first invoice pro-rated for Nov 1 — Jan 31 credit as noted- Discount: 10% retention discount + volume ladder

Option B — 36-month locked- Lower per-unit rate (additional 8% off A list) but no early upsize entitlement- Billing aligned to Feb 1, 2027 on co-term; standard invoicing cadence

Requested next steps: 
1) Which option (A or B) does Meridian prefer?
2) If A, confirm whether Procurement wants auto-renew default (60-day cancel) or opt-in auto-renew.
3) If A and co-term chosen, Finance Ops — please send PO reference or tell us if you prefer Redwood to issue an amendment + invoice.

I’m happy to jump on a 20-minute call Friday at 9am PT with Procurement to walk the amendment and run through the math.

Best —
Mateo

On Wed, Oct 28, 2026 at 09:10 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> [amendment-draft.docx attached]

Thanks again for the call this morning. As discussed, I pulled together an initial renewal proposal that aligns to Meridian’s expected product ramp over the next 18 months and includes an optional early upsize path if you accelerate deployment. High-level: 

- Term: 18 months (Nov 1, 2026 — Apr 30, 2028) with option to convert to a 36-month locked rate at the 12-month milestone.
- Capacity: baseline 200 GPU-hours/month, ramping to 1,200 GPU-hours/month by month 12 (milestone schedule in attached).
- Early upsize option: right to add incremental capacity at 60% of list for the first 90 days after signature (pre-provisioned burst pool).
- Co-terming: we can co-term this renewal to your existing data platform contract; if you choose co-term, we’ll pro-rate to align to the platform’s Feb 1 renewal date.
- Auto-renew: default 12-month auto-renew unless canceled with 60 days’ notice; we can change this to opt-in auto-renew per your procurement preference.
- Pricing: blended unit rate with an initial 10% retention discount and a volume ladder that reduces per-GPU cost at 500 / 900 / 1,200 thresholds.

Attached: Meridian-Renewal-Initial-Proposal.pdf (summary + milestone schedule) and usage-runbook.xlsx (projected token/GPU cadence).

Next steps: Can you confirm (1) whether you want co-terming with the platform contract, (2) whether Procurement needs a separate SOW or PO, and (3) your preferred sign date? If helpful, we can build a short amendment that shows both the 18-month path and the 36-month convert option side-by-side.

I’ll loop in Kimberly (Sales Ops) for proration/billing mechanics once you confirm co-term.

Best,
Mateo

messages:
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@orbitai.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: sales@orbitai.com
Date: 2026-10-22T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing

Thanks for the demo today — that was super helpful. Our engineering lead wants a short, actionable checklist to get Redwood SDKs running locally so we can validate integration work before we move to a private deployment. High-level asks: 

- Minimal Python + TS sample to call chat + embeddings locally
- Auth setup (token scope, env vars) and advice for dev vs prod tokens
- How to route to a pinned model version and a fallback model
- Example for structured output / tool calling and how it looks in the SDK
- Any gotchas for local CPU-only testing (we may not have GPUs yet)

Would appreciate a short checklist and sample commands we can run this week. Happy to do a 30-min technical call if needed.

Thanks,
Jordan
Director of Product, OrbitAI

---
Attachment: none

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@orbitai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-22T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing

Jordan — thanks for the clear list. Attaching a short checklist and a tiny snippet file (redwood_quickconnect_checklist.pdf, local_env_snippets.txt) that walks through a minimal Python + TS flow and the most common env vars. Core points up front (TL;DR):

1) Quickstart approach (local smoke):
   - Clone: git clone https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-examples && cd orbitai-samples/minimal
   - Python: python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate; pip install -r requirements.txt
   - TS: cd ts && npm install && npm run dev
   - Start: run the included sample server (./run_local.sh for Linux/mac) and run the sample client to verify chat + embeddings.

2) Auth/Env vars:
   - REDWOOD_API_KEY (dev scoped key; rotate for CI)
   - REDWOOD_API_URL (defaults to hosted endpoint; private customers will replace with private control plane URL)
   - REDWOOD_REGION (optional: us, eu, apac)
   - For local dev, a long-lived dev token scoped only to test models is fine; avoid committing to repo. We recommend a short-lived CI token for pipelines.

3) Routing & pinning:
   - SDK call accepts model: "gpt-redwood-1.3" and model_fallback: "gpt-small-quant" in the request options. See sample in checklist.
   - For deterministic routing: set route_policy: {"preference": "latency", "pin": "version:20261015"} in the client config.

4) Structured output & tool calling:
   - Use the SDK's structured_response schema param. Example snippet included for a function call to a DB lookup (returns JSON with keys: 'id','name','score').
   - For local testing, simulate tool endpoints by running the included mock_tools server (mock_tools/).

5) Embeddings & rerankers:
   - Use the embeddings client (embed.create) with batch size 64 for local CPU; switch to 256-1024 when on GPU.
   - For reranking, we include a small reranker example that uses embeddings distances + a lightweight LM score.

Next steps: we can hop on a 30-min call to walk your engineers through the repo and run the smoke tests. Proposed times: Thu 10/23 9–11am PT or Fri 10/24 1–3pm PT. Let me know which works.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: redwood_quickconnect_checklist.pdf, local_env_snippets.txt

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@orbitai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-23T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing (technical notes)

Quick technical notes / gotchas for the OrbitAI team — follow these to avoid false negatives during local validation:

Environment and auth:
- REDWOOD_API_KEY: create a dev key in the Redwood Console -> API Keys. Use the "dev" scope: model:read, model:infer. Do not use an admin key in CI.
- If you're behind a corporate proxy, set HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY before running npm/pip.

Local CPU testing:
- Models used in samples are the "small" family. CPU inference will be slow but deterministic. Set MAX_TOKENS to 256 for smoke tests.
- For embedding generation on CPU, reduce batch_size to 8–16. The snippets in local_env_snippets.txt include tuned defaults.
- If you want to validate behavior only (not performance), run the SDK with REDWOOD_FORCE_QUANT=true to emulate quantized outputs; this reduces memory and speeds up runs.

Routing and fallbacks:
- To pin version: in the SDK client config set pinned_version: "2026-10-15". If pinned version not available, model_fallback will be used automatically — we recommend including a fallback that is from the same family to avoid output drift.
- For canary: set rollout: {"percent": 10} to split traffic for early testing.

Structured output & tool calling specifics:
- Use structured_schema to validate outputs. Example: structured_schema = {"type":"object","properties":{"action":{"type":"string"},"payload":{"type":"object"}}}
- Tool calling: annotate tools in the request with name, description, input_schema. The SDK will surface a tool_call object on partial responses; consume it by calling your mock tool endpoint and pass the tool response back via tool_response. See mock_tools/README.md for an end-to-end example.

Embeddings / reranker tips:
- Use cosine similarity on normalized vectors for stable results. The sample reranker uses embeddings + a small LM score to re-sort candidate responses.
- For local experiments, persist embeddings to ./tmp/embeddings.db (sqlite) so you can iterate without recomputing.

Docker/dev workflow:
- We include a docker-compose.yaml that spins up a tiny web client, mock_tools, and a local runner. Ports: 3000 (TS client), 5000 (python runner), 7000 (mock tools).
- Run: docker-compose up --build; then exec into the python container to run smoke tests: python ./tests/smoke_chat.py

If helpful I can jump on the call and pair with your engineers while they run the checklist.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: none (code snippets in repo)

From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@orbitai.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-25T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing

Appreciate this level of detail — this should get our engineers unblocked. Quick clarifications:

- We're running on AWS (us-east-1) with restricted outbound egress — how much do we need to open up to Redwood hosted endpoints for the smoke tests?
- We may want to swap to a private deployment later; are the env vars in your checklist stable for that change or will we need to rewire a lot?

We can do the Thu 10/23 slot but shifted to 10:30am PT if Karthik can make it — otherwise Fri afternoon works.

Thanks,
Jordan
Director of Product, OrbitAI

- Tool calling: annotate tools in the request with name, description, input_schema. The SDK will surface a tool_call object on partial responses; consume it by calling your mock tool endpoint and pass the tool response back via tool_response. See mock_tools/README.md for an end-to-end example.

Embeddings / reranker tips:
- Use cosine similarity on normalized vectors for stable results. The sample reranker uses embeddings + a small LM score to re-sort candidate responses.
- For local experiments, persist embeddings to ./tmp/embeddings.db (sqlite) so you can iterate without recomputing.

Docker/dev workflow:
- We include a docker-compose.yaml that spins up a tiny web client, mock_tools, and a local runner. Ports: 3000 (TS client), 5000 (python runner), 7000 (mock tools).
- Run: docker-compose up --build; then exec into the python container to run smoke tests: python ./tests/smoke_chat.py

If helpful I can jump on the call and pair with your engineers while they run the checklist.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
Cc: sales@orbitai.com
Date: 2026-10-22T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing

Thanks for the demo today — that was super helpful. Our engineering lead wants a short, actionable checklist to get Redwood SDKs running locally so we can validate integration work before we move to a private deployment. High-level asks: 

- Minimal Python + TS sample to call chat + embeddings locally
- Auth setup (token scope, env vars) and advice for dev vs prod tokens
- How to route to a pinned model version and a fallback model
- Example for structured output / tool calling and how it looks in the SDK
- Any gotchas for local CPU-only testing (we may not have GPUs yet)

Would appreciate a short checklist and sample commands we can run this week. Happy to do a 30-min technical call if needed.

Thanks,
Jordan
Director of Product, OrbitAI

Attachment: none


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@orbitai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-22T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing

Jordan — thanks for the clear list. Attaching a short checklist and a tiny snippet file (redwood_quickconnect_checklist.pdf, local_env_snippets.txt) that walks through a minimal Python + TS flow and the most common env vars. Core points up front (TL;DR):

1) Quickstart approach (local smoke):
   - Clone: git clone https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-examples && cd orbitai-samples/minimal
   - Python: python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate; pip install -r requirements.txt
   - TS: cd ts && npm install && npm run dev
   - Start: run the included sample server (./run_local.sh for Linux/mac) and run the sample client to verify chat + embeddings.

2) Auth/Env vars:
   - REDWOOD_API_KEY (dev scoped key; rotate for CI)
   - REDWOOD_API_URL (defaults to hosted endpoint; private customers will replace with private control plane URL)
   - REDWOOD_REGION (optional: us, eu, apac)
   - For local dev, a long-lived dev token scoped only to test models is fine; avoid committing to repo. We recommend a short-lived CI token for pipelines.

3) Routing & pinning:
   - SDK call accepts model: \"gpt-redwood-1.3\" and model_fallback: \"gpt-small-quant\" in the request options. See sample in checklist.
   - For deterministic routing: set route_policy: {\"preference\": \"latency\", \"pin\": \"version:20261015\"} in the client config.

4) Structured output & tool calling:
   - Use the SDK's structured_response schema param. Example snippet included for a function call to a DB lookup (returns JSON with keys: 'id','name','score').
   - For local testing, simulate tool endpoints by running the included mock_tools server (mock_tools/).

5) Embeddings & rerankers:
   - Use the embeddings client (embed.create) with batch size 64 for local CPU; switch to 256-1024 when on GPU.
   - For reranking, we include a small reranker example that uses embeddings distances + a lightweight LM score.

Next steps: we can hop on a 30-min call to walk your engineers through the repo and run the smoke tests. Proposed times: Thu 10/23 9–11am PT or Fri 10/24 1–3pm PT. Let me know which works.

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: redwood_quickconnect_checklist.pdf, local_env_snippets.txt


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>

Date: 2026-10-23T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's demo — quickstart + local testing (technical notes)

Quick technical notes / gotchas for the OrbitAI team — follow these to avoid false negatives during local validation:

Environment and auth:
- REDWOOD_API_KEY: create a dev key in the Redwood Console -> API Keys. Use the \"dev\" scope: model:read, model:infer. Do not use an admin key in CI.
- If you're behind a corporate proxy, set HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY before running npm/pip.

Local CPU testing:
- Models used in samples are the \"small\" family. CPU inference will be slow but deterministic. Set MAX_TOKENS to 256 for smoke tests.
- For embedding generation on CPU, reduce batch_size to 8–16. The snippets in local_env_snippets.txt include tuned defaults.
- If you want to validate behavior only (not performance), run the SDK with REDWOOD_FORCE_QUANT=true to emulate quantized outputs; this reduces memory and speeds up runs.

Routing and fallbacks:
- To pin version: in the SDK client config set pinned_version: \"2026-10-15\". If pinned version not available, model_fallback will be used automatically — we recommend including a fallback that is from the same family to avoid output drift.
- For canary: set rollout: {\"percent\": 10} to split traffic for early testing.

Structured output & tool calling specifics:
- Use structured_schema to validate outputs. Example: structured_schema = {\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"action\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"payload\":{\"type\":\"object\"}}}
- Tool calling: annotate tools in the request with name, description, input_schema. The SDK will surface a tool_call object on partial responses; consume it by calling your mock tool endpoint and pass the tool response back via tool_response. See mock_tools/README.md for an end-to-end example.

Embeddings / reranker tips:
- Use cosine similarity on normalized vectors for stable results. The sample reranker uses embeddings + a small LM score to re-sort candidate responses.
- For local experiments, persist embeddings to ./tmp/embeddings.db (sqlite) so you can iterate without recomputing.

Docker/dev workflow:
- We include a docker-compose.yaml that spins up a tiny web client, mock_tools, and a local runner. Ports: 3000 (TS client), 5000 (python runner), 7000 (mock tools).
- Run: docker-compose up --build; then exec into the python container to run smoke tests: python ./tests/smoke_chat.py

If helpful I can jump on the call and pair with your engineers while they run the checklist.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

source.subject: MAP: performance fingerprinting & autoscale behavior across hosting modes
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>, BrightServe SRE <sre@brightserve.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00Z
Subject: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan

Hi Elena / BrightServe team,

Thanks for agreeing to run the MAP performance evaluation. Attached is a lightweight MAP worksheet (MAP-v1.xlsx) and the test harness we plan to use. Goal is to baseline latency (TTFT, p50/p95), throughput (tokens/sec, req/sec), and autoscaler behavior across: Hosted (Redwood API), Dedicated (reserved GPU pool), and Private (VPC deployment). Key asks:

- Confirm target model variant to test (we propose rwd-13b-quant-v2).
- Confirm allowed windows for traffic injections (you mentioned maintenance windows Fri-Sun).
- Provide a primary SRE contact for running a coordinated test day.

Proposed approach (high level):
1) Warm-cache steady-state run: ramp to expected peak concurrency (we propose 80 concurrent sessions) and hold for 30 minutes. Measure p50/p95 latency and tokens/sec.
2) Cold-start + burst run: start from idle; run short bursts to evaluate TTFT and autoscale reaction time.
3) Isolation test: run tenant-mixed traffic (control) vs single-tenant-only to observe variance on hosted.
4) Autoscale stress: sustained increasing load to trigger scale events; record scale times and request queuing.

Timing: aim for test week 2026-10-22 to 2026-10-24. MAP worksheet includes a draft close timeline and mutual responsibilities.

Attachments: MAP-v1.xlsx, test-scripts.tar.gz (contains harness and k6 scripts).

Next steps: Elena — can you confirm model choice and available windows? Connor/Marissa/Rishi — I'll need a short runbook for Dedicated and Private contacts.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
m: +1 (415) 555-0132
From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightServe SRE <sre@brightserve.com>, Procurement <procurement@brightserve.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T13:05:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan

Hi Mateo — thanks for the plan and the worksheet.

We can support rwd-13b-quant-v2 for the first pass. Maintenance window preference: 2026-10-23 00:00–04:00 PT for heavier runs, and 2026-10-22 23:00–01:00 PT for lighter checks. SRE will be present for both.

Constraints from our side:
- Procurement requires a final statement of work before we authorize Dedicated capacity or Private deployment access.
- Any test that touches live production keys must be done with a separate eval account; we will provide one.

Assigning SRE contact: Diego Ramos (diego.ramos@brightserve.com) — he will coordinate the runbook with your team.

Elena
Director, Cloud Platform — BrightServe
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:22:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan

Thanks — a few technical clarifications and additions to the MAP test plan:

- Model & harness: we'll pin the run to rwd-13b-quant-v2 w/ sequence length 1024 for consistency. Test harness will exercise both short prompts (<=32 tokens) and long prompts (~600 tokens) to capture token-generation variance.
- Metrics to capture (per request): TTFT (time to first token), total time-to-last-token, token generation rate, GPU utilization, and queue time prior to scheduling. Please also capture instance-level telemetry for Dedicated/Private (nvidia-smi, top).
- Autoscale triggers: we will instrument both request-rate and queue-depth triggers. For Hosted, we expect a managed autoscaler to add capacity in ~45–90s depending on current load. For Dedicated with reserved pools, autoscale is controlled by policy (we'll test a 2x burst policy). For Private, autoscaler behavior depends on your K8s cluster config — please provide HPA settings or we will use a simple CPU-based HPA for the test.
- Isolation scenario: run a noisy neighbor VM on the same partition at 30% baseline utilization and compare p95 delta.

I'll draft a one-page runbook and drop it into the MAP folder; Marissa, can you coordinate the DOCS and acceptance criteria?

Connor
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: BrightServe SRE <sre@brightserve.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-24T07:30:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan — results (initial)

On Fri, Oct 23, 2026 at 23:59 PT BrightServe SRE ran the coordinated test window. Attached: bs_map_benchmark_results.csv (raw), observability-screenshots.zip (dashboards). Summary of key findings (initial, aggregated):

1) Hosted (Redwood API, multi-tenant):
- Warm steady-state: p50 latency = 85ms TTFT included, p95 = 320ms at 60 concurrent sessions (short prompts).
- Throughput: ~3,800 tokens/sec aggregated across region during peak steady-state.
- TTFT cold-start median = 180ms for first token; cold-start p95 = 420ms.
- Autoscale: observed scale events with median reaction time ~70s; some jitter where p95 degraded for ~45–90s during scale-up.
- Isolation: noisy neighbor tests showed up to +18% p95 delta in hosted during mixed-tenancy spikes.

2) Dedicated (reserved pool w/2x burst policy):
- Warm steady-state: p50 = 68ms, p95 = 180ms at equivalent load (60 concurrent).
- Throughput: ~5,400 tokens/sec (higher sustained throughput due to pinned capacity).
- TTFT cold-start median = 140ms, p95 = 280ms.
- Autoscale: policy triggers for burst worked; scale-up within 40–60s when using reserved burst pool with pre-warmed instances. Queueing remained minimal.
- Isolation: p95 delta negligible (<=4%) when noisy neighbor present on same cluster due to resource isolation in reserved pool.

3) Private (VPC deployment on customer infra):
- Warm steady-state: p50 = 72ms, p95 = 210ms (similar to Dedicated once properly tuned).
- Throughput: highly dependent on instance shape — in our test (8 x A10g equiv), we saw ~4,900 tokens/sec.
- TTFT: median 150ms, p95 300ms.
- Autoscale: behavior followed customer HPA; scale-up latency varied widely (30s–3min) depending on cold node provisioning; recommending pre-warmed spare nodes for strict SLOs.
- Isolation: effectively zero cross-tenant variance given dedicated infra.

Observations / Recommendations:
- For predictability and lowest p95 under burst, Dedicated > Private (if Private does not pre-warm) > Hosted.
- Hosted is lowest friction but shows the most variance and needs MAP-level SLAs to guarantee p95 tails.
- If BrightServe requires p95 < 250ms at target concurrency with minimal variance, a Dedicated pool with 2x burst policy or Private with pre-warmed nodes is recommended.

Attachments include the full CSV with per-run traces and screenshots showing autoscale events.

Diego Ramos
SRE — BrightServe
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>, Diego Ramos <diego.ramos@brightserve.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Procurement <procurement@brightserve.com>
Date: 2026-10-25T15:40:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP results & recommended MAP close path — next steps

Thanks Diego and team — excellent data and quick turnaround. Summary / proposed close path for the MAP and procurement items: 

1) Recommendation: Dedicated reserved pool with 2x burst enabled. Rationale: best balance of p95 stability and predictable throughput for the ARR band you shared during kickoff. Private is viable but will require procurement/time to enable pre-warmed nodes. Hosted remains an option for fast time-to-value if BrightServe accepts a MAP-level p95 variance allowance.

2) Proposed commercial path:
- 3-month pilot on Dedicated (baseline capacity sized to observed steady-state plus 25%), with a 30% pilot credit in case of early variance.
- Parallel workstream: procurement statement of work to scope Private if you choose that route.

3) Operational asks:
- Elena/Diego: confirm pilot start date (we propose 2026-11-08) and who will sign the pilot SOW.
- Procurement: share the required template/PO path so we can align billing and access.
- Connor/Marissa: finalize the MAP worksheet with acceptance criteria (I've added the observed p50/p95 numbers into MAP-v1.xlsx).

If everyone agrees, I'll send an updated MAP with the pilot SOW and a short rollout checklist (DNS, KMS access, egress controls for Private).

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments referenced: MAP-v1.xlsx, bs_map_benchmark_results.csv

If everyone agrees, I'll send an updated MAP with the pilot SOW and a short rollout checklist (DNS, KMS access, egress controls for Private).

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments referenced: MAP-v1.xlsx, bs_map_benchmark_results.csv
text:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>, BrightServe SRE <sre@brightserve.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00Z
Subject: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan

Hi Elena / BrightServe team,

Thanks for agreeing to run the MAP performance evaluation. Attached is a lightweight MAP worksheet (MAP-v1.xlsx) and the test harness we plan to use. Goal is to baseline latency (TTFT, p50/p95), throughput (tokens/sec, req/sec), and autoscaler behavior across: Hosted (Redwood API), Dedicated (reserved GPU pool), and Private (VPC deployment). Key asks:

- Confirm target model variant to test (we propose rwd-13b-quant-v2).
- Confirm allowed windows for traffic injections (you mentioned maintenance windows Fri-Sun).
- Provide a primary SRE contact for running a coordinated test day.

Proposed approach (high level):
1) Warm-cache steady-state run: ramp to expected peak concurrency (we propose 80 concurrent sessions) and hold for 30 minutes. Measure p50/p95 latency and tokens/sec.
2) Cold-start + burst run: start from idle; run short bursts to evaluate TTFT and autoscale reaction time.
3) Isolation test: run tenant-mixed traffic (control) vs single-tenant-only to observe variance on hosted.
4) Autoscale stress: sustained increasing load to trigger scale events; record scale times and request queuing.

Timing: aim for test week 2026-10-22 to 2026-10-24. MAP worksheet includes a draft close timeline and mutual responsibilities.

Attachments: MAP-v1.xlsx, test-scripts.tar.gz (contains harness and k6 scripts).

Next steps: Elena — can you confirm model choice and available windows? Connor/Marissa/Rishi — I'll need a short runbook for Dedicated and Private contacts.

Thanks,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
m: +1 (415) 555-0132


From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightServe SRE <sre@brightserve.com>, Procurement <procurement@brightserve.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T13:05:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan

Hi Mateo — thanks for the plan and the worksheet.

We can support rwd-13b-quant-v2 for the first pass. Maintenance window preference: 2026-10-23 00:00–04:00 PT for heavier runs, and 2026-10-22 23:00–01:00 PT for lighter checks. SRE will be present for both.

Constraints from our side:
- Procurement requires a final statement of work before we authorize Dedicated capacity or Private deployment access.
- Any test that touches live production keys must be done with a separate eval account; we will provide one.

Assigning SRE contact: Diego Ramos (diego.ramos@brightserve.com) — he will coordinate the runbook with your team.

Elena
Director, Cloud Platform — BrightServe


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:22:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan

Thanks — a few technical clarifications and additions to the MAP test plan:

- Model & harness: we'll pin the run to rwd-13b-quant-v2 w/ sequence length 1024 for consistency. Test harness will exercise both short prompts (<=32 tokens) and long prompts (~600 tokens) to capture token-generation variance.
- Metrics to capture (per request): TTFT (time to first token), total time-to-last-token, token generation rate, GPU utilization, and queue time prior to scheduling. Please also capture instance-level telemetry for Dedicated/Private (nvidia-smi, top).
- Autoscale triggers: we will instrument both request-rate and queue-depth triggers. For Hosted, we expect a managed autoscaler to add capacity in ~45–90s depending on current load. For Dedicated with reserved pools, autoscale is controlled by policy (we'll test a 2x burst policy). For Private, autoscaler behavior depends on your K8s cluster config — please provide HPA settings or we will use a simple CPU-based HPA for the test.
- Isolation scenario: run a noisy neighbor VM on the same partition at 30% baseline utilization and compare p95 delta.

I'll draft a one-page runbook and drop it into the MAP folder; Marissa, can you coordinate the DOCS and acceptance criteria?

Connor
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: BrightServe SRE <sre@brightserve.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-24T07:30:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — performance fingerprinting and autoscale test plan — results (initial)

On Fri, Oct 23, 2026 at 23:59 PT BrightServe SRE ran the coordinated test window. Attached: bs_map_benchmark_results.csv (raw), observability-screenshots.zip (dashboards). Summary of key findings (initial, aggregated):

1) Hosted (Redwood API, multi-tenant):
- Warm steady-state: p50 latency = 85ms TTFT included, p95 = 320ms at 60 concurrent sessions (short prompts).
- Throughput: ~3,800 tokens/sec aggregated across region during peak steady-state.
- TTFT cold-start median = 180ms for first token; cold-start p95 = 420ms.
- Autoscale: observed scale events with median reaction time ~70s; some jitter where p95 degraded for ~45–90s during scale-up.
- Isolation: noisy neighbor tests showed up to +18% p95 delta in hosted during mixed-tenancy spikes.

2) Dedicated (reserved pool w/2x burst policy):
- Warm steady-state: p50 = 68ms, p95 = 180ms at equivalent load (60 concurrent).
- Throughput: ~5,400 tokens/sec (higher sustained throughput due to pinned capacity).
- TTFT cold-start median = 140ms, p95 = 280ms.
- Autoscale: policy triggers for burst worked; scale-up within 40–60s when using reserved burst pool with pre-warmed instances. Queueing remained minimal.
- Isolation: p95 delta negligible (<=4%) when noisy neighbor present on same cluster due to resource isolation in reserved pool.

3) Private (VPC deployment on customer infra):
- Warm steady-state: p50 = 72ms, p95 = 210ms (similar to Dedicated once properly tuned).
- Throughput: highly dependent on instance shape — in our test (8 x A10g equiv), we saw ~4,900 tokens/sec.
- TTFT: median 150ms, p95 300ms.
- Autoscale: behavior followed customer HPA; scale-up latency varied widely (30s–3min) depending on cold node provisioning; recommending pre-warmed spare nodes for strict SLOs.
- Isolation: effectively zero cross-tenant variance given dedicated infra.

Observations / Recommendations:
- For predictability and lowest p95 under burst, Dedicated > Private (if Private does not pre-warm) > Hosted.
- Hosted is lowest friction but shows the most variance and needs MAP-level SLAs to guarantee p95 tails.
- If BrightServe requires p95 < 250ms at target concurrency with minimal variance, a Dedicated pool with 2x burst policy or Private with pre-warmed nodes is recommended.

Attachments include the full CSV with per-run traces and screenshots showing autoscale events.

Diego Ramos
SRE — BrightServe


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightserve.com>, Diego Ramos <diego.ramos@brightserve.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Procurement <procurement@brightserve.com>
Date: 2026-10-25T15:40:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP results & recommended MAP close path — next steps

Thanks Diego and team — excellent data and quick turnaround. Summary / proposed close path for the MAP and procurement items: 

1) Recommendation: Dedicated reserved pool with 2x burst enabled. Rationale: best balance of p95 stability and predictable throughput for the ARR band you shared during kickoff. Private is viable but will require procurement/time to enable pre-warmed nodes. Hosted remains an option for fast time-to-value if BrightServe accepts a MAP-level p95 variance allowance.

2) Proposed commercial path:
- 3-month pilot on Dedicated (baseline capacity sized to observed steady-state plus 25%), with a 30% pilot credit in case of early variance.
- Parallel workstream: procurement statement of work to scope Private if you choose that route.

3) Operational asks:
- Elena/Diego: confirm pilot start date (we propose 2026-11-08) and who will sign the pilot SOW.
- Procurement: share the required template/PO path so we can align billing and access.
- Connor/Marissa: finalize the MAP worksheet with acceptance criteria (I've added the observed p50/p95 numbers into MAP-v1.xlsx).

If everyone agrees, I'll send an updated MAP with the pilot SOW and a short rollout checklist (DNS, KMS access, egress controls for Private).

Cheers,
Mateo Alvarez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments referenced: MAP-v1.xlsx, bs_map_benchmark_results.csv

messages:
From: Oliver Shaw <oliver.shaw@fjordbank.com>
To: mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
Cc: security@fjordbank.com
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Unusual service-account activity seen; request for IRP + evidence

Hi Mateo,

We observed an unusual spike in calls originating from a Redwood service account tied to our integration (svc-fjord-connector) between 03:12–03:48 UTC on Oct 20. Calls included a heightened rate of token refresh attempts and a handful of 401/403 errors. Our security team flagged this as a potential credential misuse and asked us to request an Incident Response Plan (IRP) snapshot and supporting artifacts so we can complete a scheduled SIG/CUSTOMER-SOC review.

Specifically we need:
- Your current IRP (PDF) and the runbook steps you would follow for an event like this.
- Authentication logs for the redwood service account for Oct 19–21 (UTC).
- Any telemetry or tracing that shows request paths and IPs for the service account calls.
- Confirmation of KMS/HSM access logs around key usage for the same window.

We understand some items may be sensitive; please provide a secure transfer link (S3 presigned, SecureShare, or similar) and mark any material that should be treated highly confidential. We are working against a compliance review timeline and would appreciate an initial evidence index within 48 hours, with the full artifact bundle within 5 business days if possible.

Also, please let us know whether this incident requires a formal joint tabletop or if your SOC prefers to handle root-cause analysis before we schedule a cross-team session.

Thanks in advance for the quick turnaround.

Oliver
Oliver Shaw
Head of Security Operations
FjordBank
oliver.shaw@fjordbank.com
+1 415-555-0198
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Shaw <oliver.shaw@fjordbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Unusual service-account activity seen; request for IRP + evidence

Oliver — thanks for the heads up and the clear ask. Marking received and escalating internally.

Quick answers to your bullets:
- We will provide a redacted version of our IRP (operational runbook) and a separate checklist for SIG/SOC reviews. Expect a PDF and a short index within 24–36 hours.
- Auth logs and request traces for the svc-fjord-connector: we can produce an encrypted zip of the relevant logs (auth-logs-20261019-21.zip). Delivery method: presigned S3 link or your secure portal — which do you prefer?
- KMS audit records: we can export usage events around key IDs used by the connector; we will redact internal account IDs but include timestamps and principal IDs relevant to the window.

Action items for internal team (cc'd):
- Marissa: please pull the IRP export and redact anything customer-specific; produce a short change-log describing the last IRP update.
- Soojin: can you collect the auth traces + KMS export and prepare the artifact index? Add estimated sizes.

Proposed timeline: initial evidence index by EOD Thu Oct 22 (within 48 hours), full encrypted artifact bundle delivered by EOD Tue Oct 27 (5 business days). If you need acceleration, we can prioritize specific artifacts.

Do you want a live call this week for an initial walk-through, or would you prefer to review the index first and then book a follow-up?

Best,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Security Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

On Tue, Oct 20, 2026 at 09:15 AM Oliver Shaw <oliver.shaw@fjordbank.com> wrote:
> We observed an unusual spike in calls originating from a Redwood service account tied to our integration (svc-fjord-connector) between 03:12–03:48 UTC on Oct 20.
> ...
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Shaw <oliver.shaw@fjordbank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, security@fjordbank.com
Date: Fri, 23 Oct 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Evidence index and artifact delivery proposal — FjordBank svc-fjord-connector event (Oct 20)

Oliver / FjordBank security team,

Following up with the initial index and delivery plan. Thanks for the patience while we compiled the artifacts. Below is the index and the proposed secure transfer path.

Summary of what we observed (short):
- Spike in service-account calls from IP block 34.120.0.0/16 tied to our autoscaling fleet during 03:12–03:48 UTC.
- Requests returned a mix of 200 and 401 responses; root cause appears to be a rotated credential that had not deployed to 2 of 4 replica pools (race during rotation). No customer data exfiltration detected.

Artifact index (attached stubs):
1) redwood_irp_v2.pdf (IRP, redacted) — runbook steps, escalation contacts, SIG review checklist.
2) auth-logs-20261020.zip (CSV logs, 12MB) — authentication events for principal svc-fjord-connector 2026-10-19–2026-10-21.
3) kms-usage-20261020.csv (CSV, 1.2MB) — KMS/HSM operation timestamps and requester principal IDs (redacted).
4) postmortem-draft-20261023.md (Markdown, 3 pages) — preliminary root cause, timeline, remediation steps, and follow-ups.

Delivery plan:
- I will create a presigned S3 bucket link (48-hour expiry) encrypted with our KMS. Will share the URL through your secure ticketing portal; if you prefer a managed transfer (SecureShare / sFTP) notify me.
- We will also open a shared workspace (read-only) with the redacted IRP and the postmortem draft for your reviewers.

Next steps we propose:
- Confirm receipt of the presigned link once I send it (target: within 2 hours of this message).
- Joint 60-minute walk-through the week of Oct 26 to review timeline and remediation. Suggested attendees: you, your SOC lead, our on-call infra engineer, and our security compliance manager.

Notes and caveats:
- We treat these artifacts as highly confidential; please do not circulate outside your SOC/Compliance reviewers.
- If your audit requires additional detail (full trace spans, raw kernel logs), we will need a signed data transfer agreement; we can expedite that if required.

Attachments (stubs):
- redwood_irp_v2.pdf (IRP, redacted)
- auth-logs-20261020.zip (encrypted logs)
- postmortem-draft-20261023.md

Please confirm preferred transfer mechanism and whether the proposed joint walk-through works for your team.

Thanks,
Mateo
Senior Security Engineer, Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Unusual service-account activity seen; request for IRP + evidence

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>

Delivery plan:
- I will create a presigned S3 bucket link (48-hour expiry) encrypted with our KMS. Will share the URL through your secure ticketing portal; if you prefer a managed transfer (SecureShare / sFTP) notify me.
- We will also open a shared workspace (read-only) with the redacted IRP and the postmortem draft for your reviewers.

Next steps we propose:
- Confirm receipt of the presigned link once I send it (target: within 2 hours of this message).
- Joint 60-minute walk-through the week of Oct 26 to review timeline and remediation. Suggested attendees: you, your SOC lead, our on-call infra engineer, and our security compliance manager.

Notes and caveats:
- We treat these artifacts as highly confidential; please do not circulate outside your SOC/Compliance reviewers.
- If your audit requires additional detail (full trace spans, raw kernel logs), we will need a signed data transfer agreement; we can expedite that if required.

Attachments (stubs):
- redwood_irp_v2.pdf (IRP, redacted)
- auth-logs-20261020.zip (encrypted logs)
- postmortem-draft-20261023.md

Please confirm preferred transfer mechanism and whether the proposed joint walk-through works for your team.

Our goal for the session is to: walk through the roadmap items tied to your expansion priorities, review performance/latency improvements from the last quarter, and land on a mutually agreed rollout sequence for the dedicated capacity commit.

To make scheduling fast, here are three prioritized 90‑minute windows (all PT):
