source.subject: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration / collaboration outline
messages:
From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Hi Jasmine & Mateo,

Thanks again for the intro earlier this quarter — really excited about the opportunity to work with Redwood. PromptLink has built an orchestration layer for prompt templates, evaluation hooks, and a lightweight tracing agent used by ISVs. We’d like to propose an integration with LlamaChain and Redwood’s SDK to enable: 

- Native prompt templates in LlamaChain that map to PromptLink prompt variants
- A tracing shim that surfaces prompt-version and token attribution into PromptLink observability (sampled traces)
- One-click adapter to route LlamaChain runtime calls to Redwood endpoints with transparent fallback to a local LlamaChain runtime for offline dev flows

Attached is a short outline (promptlink-integration-outline.pdf) with a proposed scope and rough timeline. High level asks:
- Technical contact for an architecture sync (we suggest Mateo)
- Legal contact for a light collaboration agreement / mutual NDA
- Preferred POC window in Q4 (2–4 week POC + 4–6 week pilot)

Would you be open to a 45-minute technical review next week? We’re flexible on demos — can show a quick LlamaChain extension that emits trace metadata.

Thanks,
Alyssa Chen
Partnerships Lead | PromptLink Solutions
Attachment: promptlink-integration-outline.pdf

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Hi Alyssa — great to see this. Thanks for the outline, we reviewed it briefly and the integration pattern looks promising.

Quick responses to your asks:
- Architecture sync: Mateo is the right contact, I’ve looped him in.
- Legal: looping in Marissa (cc) to coordinate a light MOU/NDA.
- POC timing: Q4 works; propose week of Nov 2 for the technical POC kickoff to align with our engineer bandwidth.

For the sync, we want to validate three non-functional constraints up-front so the POC has clear success criteria:
1) Latency/SLO impact when adding the tracing shim (target: <25ms added p50 token latency for streaming flows)
2) Token attribution fidelity (must preserve model variant + prompt template mapping)
3) Compatibility with our runtime fallbacks (quantized variants & prefix/KV cache semantics)

Please confirm if the tracing shim is sampled at call-level or token-level (your outline suggests call-level sampling). Also please share any dependencies the LlamaChain adapter needs (new microservice, sidecar, or pure-Python pip package).

I’ll schedule a 45m sync for next Wednesday; Mateo — can you add a short checklist of what you want Alyssa to demo?

Best,
Jasmine Liu
Head of Ecosystem Partnerships | Redwood Inference

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Thanks, Jasmine — Alyssa, quick tech note ahead of the sync.

We skimmed your outline and the three NFRs are exactly what we’ll validate in the POC. For the demo/checklist, please include the following items: 
- End-to-end demo wiring LlamaChain -> PromptLink adapter -> Redwood hosted endpoint (streaming enabled)
- Trace payload example (sampled) showing prompt template id, template hash, model_id, and token counts
- A simple fallback scenario where the adapter routes to a local LlamaChain process when hosted capacity returns 503
- Instrumented latency report for 1000 short streaming calls (we’ll compare against our baseline)

Implementation notes / concerns: 
- If the adapter requires a synchronous blocking call for every token to PromptLink, that will blow up latency; please confirm shim is batched or emits metadata asynchronously.
- We need the adapter to surface KV-cache hits so we can avoid double-counting tokens for cost attribution.

I attached a quick latency estimate spreadsheet I’ll update after the POC run (latency-estimates.xlsx). On the call we can spot any kernel-level incompatibilities (FP16/INT8 modes) and decide whether we need a small C-extension to preserve performance.

See you Wednesday.

— Mateo
Senior Integrations Engineer | Redwood Inference
Attachment: latency-estimates.xlsx

From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T15:33:00-07:00
Subject: Re: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Thanks for the quick responses — excited to move forward.

Answers inline: 
- Tracing sampling: currently call-level with optional token-level enrichment. For the POC we’ll enable call-level sampling plus token-level metadata (counts & template hashes) in a light mode that is emitted asynchronously to avoid per-token latency.
- Adapter form factor: we can provide a pure-Python pip package (llamachain-promptlink-adapter) and a lightweight sidecar option for customers who want process isolation. Neither requires a blocking per-token network hop by default.

Next steps we propose: 
1) MOU/NDA — Marissa, can you send a short form NDA? We can sign within 48 hours. DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/en/link/PL-NDA-2026
2) Tech kickoff — Nov 2, 10:00am PT (45m): agenda will be architecture review, demo, and POC success metrics. Drive folder with artifacts: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1PromptLink-Integration
3) POC acceptance criteria — Alyssa attached a brief mutual action plan to the Drive (we’ll drop a PDF and a small test harness).

Attached: promptlink-datasheet.png (logo + short product spec). Looking forward to the sync.

Best,
Alyssa
Partnerships Lead | PromptLink Solutions
Attachment: promptlink-datasheet.png

- One-click adapter to route LlamaChain runtime calls to Redwood endpoints with transparent fallback to a local LlamaChain runtime for offline dev flows

Attached is a short outline (promptlink-integration-outline.pdf) with a proposed scope and rough timeline. High level asks:
- Technical contact for an architecture sync (we suggest Mateo)
- Legal contact for a light collaboration agreement / mutual NDA
- Preferred POC window in Q4 (2–4 week POC + 4–6 week pilot)

Would you be open to a 45-minute technical review next week? We’re flexible on demos — can show a quick LlamaChain extension that emits trace metadata.

Thanks,
Alyssa Chen
Partnerships Lead | PromptLink Solutions
Attachment: promptlink-integration-outline.pdf

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Hi Alyssa — great to see this. Thanks for the outline, we reviewed it briefly and the integration pattern looks promising.

Quick responses to your asks:
- Architecture sync: Mateo is the right contact, I’ve looped him in.
- Legal: looping in Marissa (cc) to coordinate a light MOU/NDA.
- POC timing: Q4 works; propose week of Nov 2 for the technical POC kickoff to align with our engineer bandwidth.

For the sync, we want to validate three non-functional constraints up-front so the POC has clear success criteria:
1) Latency/SLO impact when adding the tracing shim (target: <25ms added p50 token latency for streaming flows)
2) Token attribution fidelity (must preserve model variant + prompt template mapping)
3) Compatibility with our runtime fallbacks (quantized variants & prefix/KV cache semantics)

Please confirm if the tracing shim is sampled at call-level or token-level (your outline suggests call-level sampling). Also please share any dependencies the LlamaChain adapter needs (new microservice, sidecar, or pure-Python pip package).

I’ll schedule a 45m sync for next Wednesday; Mateo — can you add a short checklist of what you want Alyssa to demo?

Best,
Jasmine Liu
Head of Ecosystem Partnerships | Redwood Inference

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Thanks, Jasmine — Alyssa, quick tech note ahead of the sync.

We skimmed your outline and the three NFRs are exactly what we’ll validate in the POC. For the demo/checklist, please include the following items: 
- End-to-end demo wiring LlamaChain -> PromptLink adapter -> Redwood hosted endpoint (streaming enabled)
- Trace payload example (sampled) showing prompt template id, template hash, model_id, and token counts
- A simple fallback scenario where the adapter routes to a local LlamaChain process when hosted capacity returns 503
- Instrumented latency report for 1000 short streaming calls (we’ll compare against our baseline)

Implementation notes / concerns: 
- If the adapter requires a synchronous blocking call for every token to PromptLink, that will blow up latency; please confirm shim is batched or emits metadata asynchronously.
- We need the adapter to surface KV-cache hits so we can avoid double-counting tokens for cost attribution.

I attached a quick latency estimate spreadsheet I’ll update after the POC run (latency-estimates.xlsx). On the call we can spot any kernel-level incompatibilities (FP16/INT8 modes) and decide whether we need a small C-extension to preserve performance.

See you Wednesday.

— Mateo
Senior Integrations Engineer | Redwood Inference
Attachment: latency-estimates.xlsx

From: Alyssa Chen <alyssa@promptlink.io>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T15:33:00-07:00
Subject: Re: PromptLink x LlamaChain — Redwood SDK integration proposition

Thanks for the quick responses — excited to move forward.

Answers inline: 
- Tracing sampling: currently call-level with optional token-level enrichment. For the POC we’ll enable call-level sampling plus token-level metadata (counts & template hashes) in a light mode that is emitted asynchronously to avoid per-token latency.
- Adapter form factor: we can provide a pure-Python pip package (llamachain-promptlink-adapter) and a lightweight sidecar option for customers who want process isolation. Neither requires a blocking per-token network hop by default.

Next steps we propose: 
1) MOU/NDA — Marissa, can you send a short form NDA? We can sign within 48 hours. DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/en/link/PL-NDA-2026
2) Tech kickoff — Nov 2, 10:00am PT (45m): agenda will be architecture review, demo, and POC success metrics. Drive folder with artifacts: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1PromptLink-Integration
3) POC acceptance criteria — Alyssa attached a brief mutual action plan to the Drive (we’ll drop a PDF and a small test harness).

Attached: promptlink-datasheet.png (logo + short product spec). Looking forward to the sync.

Best,
Alyssa
Partnerships Lead | PromptLink Solutions
Attachment: promptlink-datasheet.png

source.subject: Post-call recap & lab cadence: Credence latency/throughput A/B
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@credence.ai>, Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence

Thanks again for making time today — good signal on the evaluation priorities. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can align on a lab run that maps back to your SLA and cost targets.

Key goals you called out:
- p95 latency target: <= 140 ms on your 128-token prompts (mix of chat + short completions)
- p99 must be predictable (you called out expensive tail spikes)
- Sustained tokens/sec target for peak 50 concurrent users: ~18k tokens/sec
- SLO tradeoffs: you prefer slightly higher median latency vs >30% cost increase

Where Redwood differentiates (high level):
- Continuous prefix/KV caching to reduce re-computation for repeated contexts
- Dynamic batching that respects latency SLOs (adaptive micro-batches for bursty traffic)
- Quantization profiles we can enable for throughput without large quality regressions (we'll A/B QA)
- Smart routing: fall back to a cheaper model variant under capacity pressure with transparent metrics

Proposed lab cadence (initial scope):
1) Baseline A: Credence baseline workload -> measure p50/p95/p99, tokens/sec, and tail CPU/GPU utilization
2) Redwood B (default config) -> same workload, enable adaptive batching + KV cache
3) Redwood C (throughput-optimized) -> enable int8 quant + larger batch windows; measure quality on your prompt set
4) Failover probe -> simulate 20% capacity pressure and observe fallback behavior and latency spike magnitude

Deliverables: bench spec, raw metrics (CSV), short writeup with diffs and a recommended config for production.

Ask for you: please confirm the test window (we suggested 10/20-10/22) and attach the sample prompt set and a small traffic replay (30–60 minutes) so we can reproduce token distribution.

I'll loop Marissa & Dev to lock the lab schedule and bench spec.

Best,
Mateo
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: credence_postcall_notes.pdf (summary), bench_spec_v1.csv (placeholder)

From: Samira Khan <samira@credence.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence

Thanks — this is exactly the sort of structure we wanted. Quick clarifications: 
- We can make the traffic replay available (staging capture) by EOD 10/19. It includes conversational threads (~70%) and short completions (~30%).
- For p95 target: the 140 ms is for the US-west region only (our primary). p99 target is more of a business ask: "no more than 250 ms except during maintenance windows."
- Quality check: we’ll provide a 40-prompt QA set with scoring criteria (intent preservation + hallucination checks).

Confirming 10/20–10/22 window looks fine. Can you commit to a dry run on the 10/20 morning (US-west) and the full sweep on 10/21? Also including procurement on the thread to start a short NDA addendum discussion for the replay data (procurement@credence.ai).

Samira
Head of Product, Credence Analytics

On Sun, Oct 18, 2026 at 09:12 Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> (see above)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Cc: Samira Khan <samira@credence.ai>, Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence — scheduling & ops

Thanks Samira — good to hear the replay will be ready. Dev / team, here's the operational plan I propose so we don't lose the narrow window: 
- 10/20 09:00–10:30 PT: dry run (single node, replay subset, verify metrics pipeline, confirm p95 computation)
- 10/21 08:00–16:00 PT: full sweep (A, B, C, failover probe). We'll schedule short breaks between runs to reset caches.
- Measurement buckets: p50/p90/p95/p99, tokens/sec, GPU util, mem pressure, per-request queuing time

Dev — please shepherd the bench_spec_v1.csv into bench_spec_v1.json for our harness and add fields: request_time_ms, model_variant, batch_size, kv_hit_rate, quant_mode. Also include the QA knobs for automated quality scoring.

Action items:
- Dev: produce bench harness + estimate of runtime per full sweep (hours) by 10/19 EOD
- Mateo: confirm we can offer a canary config (routing + cheaper variant) during failover probe
- Samira: share the replay and QA prompts (thanks!)

Marissa
Principal Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference

From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Samira Khan <samira@credence.ai>, Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>
Date: 2026-10-20T15:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence — bench harness + schedule

All — put together the initial harness and estimator. Summary below and attached bench_spec_v1.csv (CSV includes mapping to our harness fields).

Quick summary of assumptions and expected runtimes:
- Dry run (10/20): 30 minutes of replay subset (300k tokens), estimate runtime 25–40 minutes to include warmup and metric ingestion.
- Full sweep (10/21): four configs x 45 min runs = ~3.5 hours including cache resets and short QA checks. Expect ~2.8M tokens across the sweep.

Preliminary perf expectations (these are estimates based on our dev cluster and the provided token profile):
- Credence baseline (A): p50 ~ 85 ms, p95 ~ 190 ms, p99 ~ 320 ms, tokens/sec ~ 9.2k
- Redwood default (B): p50 ~ 78 ms, p95 ~ 140–155 ms, p99 ~ 220–260 ms, tokens/sec ~ 14–17k depending on kv_hit_rate
- Redwood throughput (C, int8): p50 ~ 90 ms, p95 ~ 160–175 ms, p99 ~ 240–290 ms, tokens/sec ~ 20–24k (quality validation required)

Notes and caveats:
- KV cache effectiveness will be a major lever; if the provided replay shows >40% repeated prefixes, B vs A will look significantly better on p95. Please include kv_hit_rate in post-run CSV.
- We will capture per-request queuing and micro-batch histograms so we can explain tail behavior to your SREs.
- Failover probe will ramp synthetic load to emulate 20% reduced GPU capacity. We will report the duration and magnitude of latency spikes and whether our routing produced graceful fallbacks.

Attachments: bench_spec_v1.csv (fields: run_id, timestamp, model_variant, batch_size, kv_hit_rate, quant_mode, request_time_ms, gpu_util_pct, tokens_generated)

I'll push the harness to the shared Drive folder and send a short runbook for the dry run.

- Dev
Senior Inference Engineer — Redwood Inference

Cc: Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence

Thanks — this is exactly the sort of structure we wanted. Quick clarifications: 
- We can make the traffic replay available (staging capture) by EOD 10/19. It includes conversational threads (~70%) and short completions (~30%).
- For p95 target: the 140 ms is for the US-west region only (our primary). p99 target is more of a business ask: \"no more than 250 ms except during maintenance windows.\"
- Quality check: we’ll provide a 40-prompt QA set with scoring criteria (intent preservation + hallucination checks).

Confirming 10/20–10/22 window looks fine. Can you commit to a dry run on the 10/20 morning (US-west) and the full sweep on 10/21? Also including procurement on the thread to start a short NDA addendum discussion for the replay data (procurement@credence.ai).

Samira
Head of Product, Credence Analytics

On Sun, Oct 18, 2026 at 09:12 Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com> wrote:
> (see above)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Cc: Samira Khan <samira@credence.ai>, Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence — scheduling & ops

Thanks Samira — good to hear the replay will be ready. Dev / team, here's the operational plan I propose so we don't lose the narrow window: 
- 10/20 09:00–10:30 PT: dry run (single node, replay subset, verify metrics pipeline, confirm p95 computation)
- 10/21 08:00–16:00 PT: full sweep (A, B, C, failover probe). We'll schedule short breaks between runs to reset caches.
- Measurement buckets: p50/p90/p95/p99, tokens/sec, GPU util, mem pressure, per-request queuing time

Dev — please shepherd the bench_spec_v1.csv into bench_spec_v1.json for our harness and add fields: request_time_ms, model_variant, batch_size, kv_hit_rate, quant_mode. Also include the QA knobs for automated quality scoring.

Action items:
- Dev: produce bench harness + estimate of runtime per full sweep (hours) by 10/19 EOD
- Mateo: confirm we can offer a canary config (routing + cheaper variant) during failover probe
- Samira: share the replay and QA prompts (thanks!)

Marissa
Principal Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference


From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwood.com>
Cc: Samira Khan <samira@credence.ai>, Andrew Lee <andrew.lee@credence.ai>
Date: 2026-10-20T15:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & proposed A/B lab cadence — bench harness + schedule

All — put together the initial harness and estimator. Summary below and attached bench_spec_v1.csv (CSV includes mapping to our harness fields).

Quick summary of assumptions and expected runtimes:
- Dry run (10/20): 30 minutes of replay subset (300k tokens), estimate runtime 25–40 minutes to include warmup and metric ingestion.
- Full sweep (10/21): four configs x 45 min runs = ~3.5 hours including cache resets and short QA checks. Expect ~2.8M tokens across the sweep.

Preliminary perf expectations (these are estimates based on our dev cluster and the provided token profile):
- Credence baseline (A): p50 ~ 85 ms, p95 ~ 190 ms, p99 ~ 320 ms, tokens/sec ~ 9.2k
- Redwood default (B): p50 ~ 78 ms, p95 ~ 140–155 ms, p99 ~ 220–260 ms, tokens/sec ~ 14–17k depending on kv_hit_rate
- Redwood throughput (C, int8): p50 ~ 90 ms, p95 ~ 160–175 ms, p99 ~ 240–290 ms, tokens/sec ~ 20–24k (quality validation required)

Notes and caveats:
- KV cache effectiveness will be a major lever; if the provided replay shows >40% repeated prefixes, B vs A will look significantly better on p95. Please include kv_hit_rate in post-run CSV.
- We will capture per-request queuing and micro-batch histograms so we can explain tail behavior to your SREs.
- Failover probe will ramp synthetic load to emulate 20% reduced GPU capacity. We will report the duration and magnitude of latency spikes and whether our routing produced graceful fallbacks.

Attachments: bench_spec_v1.csv (fields: run_id, timestamp, model_variant, batch_size, kv_hit_rate, quant_mode, request_time_ms, gpu_util_pct, tokens_generated)

I'll push the harness to the shared Drive folder and send a short runbook for the dry run.

- Dev
Senior Inference Engineer — Redwood Inference

From: Helena Costa <helena.costa@novaline.io>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, procurement@redwood.com
Date: 2026-10-17T09:13:00-07:00
Subject: Onboarding docs — tax form + initial payment details

Hi Mateo / Redwood procurement,

Thanks for the quick intro. Attached is our completed tax form for the parent entity — Novaline Holdings S.A. (W-8BEN-E). We are a non-US group but will be routing invoices through our US subsidiary for USD billing.

Attachments: Novaline_W-8BEN-E_signed.pdf (signed W-8BEN-E)

A couple of details you might need now:
- Billing entity that will invoice Redwood: Novaline US LLC
- Expected invoice cadence: monthly, NET30
- Remit-to: Novaline US LLC (bank details will be provided after vendor setup)

If you need a copy of the US EIN letter or a signatory authorization for the person signing the W-8, let me know — I can pull those and send them.

Thanks,
Helena Costa
Head of Finance Operations, NovaLine
Helena.Costa@novaline.io | +1 415 555 0182

[Attachment: Novaline_W-8BEN-E_signed.pdf]

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@redwood.com
Date: 2026-10-18T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Onboarding docs — tax form + initial payment details

Looping in Finance and Legal for tax / PO guidance. Vendor says parent is non-US but invoices will come from a US subsidiary (Novaline US LLC). They attached a signed W-8BEN-E for the parent.

Questions:
- Do we need separate vendor records (parent + US subsidiary) in the system, or can we keep a single vendor and attach the US EIN letter to the parent?
- If invoices will be issued by Novaline US LLC, PO should be issued to that legal entity — is there any procurement gating we should apply before creating a PO?

FYI forwarded message below.

On Sat, Oct 17, 2026 at 09:13, Helena Costa <helena.costa@novaline.io> wrote:
> Hi Mateo / Redwood procurement,
> 
> Thanks for the quick intro. Attached is our completed tax form for the parent entity — Novaline Holdings S.A. (W-8BEN-E). We are a non-US group but will be routing invoices through our US subsidiary for USD billing.
> 
> Attachments: Novaline_W-8BEN-E_signed.pdf (signed W-8BEN-E)
> 
> A couple of details you might need now:
> - Billing entity that will invoice Redwood: Novaline US LLC
> - Expected invoice cadence: monthly, NET30
> - Remit-to: Novaline US LLC (bank details will be provided after vendor setup)
> 
> If you need a copy of the US EIN letter or a signatory authorization for the person signing the W-8, let me know — I can pull those and send them.
> 
> Thanks,
> Helena

--
Mateo Alvarez
Product Partnerships | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: NovaLine Payments Ops <vendor-payments@novaline.io>
Date: 2026-10-19T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Onboarding docs — tax form + initial payment details

Thanks for flagging, Mateo. Quick read-through and next steps from Finance: 

1) W-8BEN-E (parent) — good to have on file, but because the invoices will be issued by Novaline US LLC (a US taxpayer), we need documented evidence of the US entity's EIN and confirmation that the invoicing/legal remit-to name matches the EIN. Acceptable evidence: IRS CP575/CP575A, IRS 147C, or a stamped IRS letter.

2) Signatory authority — because the W-8 was signed by an authorized signatory at the parent, we also need a corporate resolution or power-of-attorney that shows the delegated signer for the US subsidiary (if the signer acts on behalf of the US entity).

3) Vendor records & PO routing — finance prefers the PO be issued to the legal entity that will invoice (Novaline US LLC). That means we should create a vendor record for Novaline US LLC and link its parent (Novaline Holdings S.A.) in our vendor profile for consolidation/1099 reporting.

4) Banking / currency — please confirm whether payments will be USD to a US bank account (preferred) or cross-border. If cross-border, we need additional AML/bank confirmation which will slow PO activation.

Can you ask them to provide: Novaline_US_EIN_Letter_147C.pdf and Corporate_Resolution_Signer.pdf? Once we have those, Finance can unblock PO creation and policy checks.

Best,
Monica Patel
Manager, Accounts Payable | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com

--
On Oct 18, 2026, at 11:02, Mateo Alvarez wrote:
> Looping in Finance and Legal for tax / PO guidance. Vendor says parent is non-US but invoices will come from a US subsidiary (Novaline US LLC). They attached a signed W-8BEN-E for the parent.
> 
> Questions:
> - Do we need separate vendor records (parent + US subsidiary) in the system, or can we keep a single vendor and attach the US EIN letter to the parent?
> - If invoices will be issued by Novaline US LLC, PO should be issued to that legal entity — is there any procurement gating we should apply before creating a PO?

From: Helena Costa <helena.costa@novaline.io>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding docs — tax form + initial payment details

Hi Mateo / Monica,

Thanks for the clarity and the checklist — we've attached the two additional items you requested.

Attachments: Novaline_US_EIN_Letter_147C.pdf, Corporate_Resolution_Signer.pdf

Clarifications:
- Novaline US LLC (EIN: XX-XXXXXXX) will be the invoicing entity and should be the payee on the PO.
- The person who signed the W-8 at the parent level is also listed on the corporate resolution as an authorized delegate to sign on behalf of Novaline US LLC.
- Payments will be made in USD to a US bank account (details to follow once vendor record is created).

Next steps requested from Redwood:
1) Create a new vendor record for Novaline US LLC and link parent company in the vendor profile.
2) Issue a PO to Novaline US LLC referencing the Redwood SOW number RDW-5781.
3) Confirm any additional AML steps if banking is outside of our automated bank verification flow.

We're attaching the evidence here to expedite. If you prefer, I can also send certified copies via our legal contact or upload to DocuSign (link below).

DocuSign: https://app.docusign.com/prepare/abc123

Thanks,
Helena Costa
Head of Finance Operations, NovaLine
[Attachment: Novaline_US_EIN_Letter_147C.pdf]
[Attachment: Corporate_Resolution_Signer.pdf]

source.subject: Streaming chat: sudden p99 tail spikes under peak load — customer escalation
messages:
From: Jonah Keane <jonah@kaleidohealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, support@redwood.com
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 21:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Live chat - severe lag / reconnects during evening peak

Hi Mateo / Redwood support,

We started seeing large numbers of reconnects and 5xx responses on our live chat between 19:15-19:45 PDT tonight. Our product team measured p95 at ~1.2s (ok) but p99 shooting up to 8-12s on the streaming endpoint (SSE). That correlates with users reporting long stalls and partial messages. Throughput (messages/min) was normal but tail latency and a small spike in error-rate (0.9%) impacted session continuity.

We rely on the streaming endpoint for our 1:1 clinician chat; customers were frustrated. Can you triage? We need visibility and a mitigation ASAP — this is impacting our retention metrics.

Attaching a short server-side trace and player logs.

— Jonah
Kaleido Health
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jonah Keane <jonah@kaleidohealth.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 22:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Live chat - severe lag / reconnects during evening peak

Team — forwarding an active report from Kaleido (high-value account). Quick context: p99 tail for the SSE/WebSocket streaming chat spiked during their 19:15-19:45 PDT peak. They saw spikes to ~10s, small uptick in 5xxs, and some session reconnects.

Immediate asks:
- Can we confirm whether this is a routing/backpressure issue or a runtime scheduling regression?
- Prioritize any mitigations we can flip without a full deploy (routing to Dedicated pools, temporary rate-limits, or enable per-client circuit-breakers).

I will brief Evelyn/Marissa and put this on the exec watch if we need credits/SLAs. PD incident created (PD-78921) — Dev, please attach any traces to the incident.

Mateo
Account Exec — Redwood Inference
From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jonah Keane <jonah@kaleidohealth.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 22:28:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Live chat - severe lag / reconnects during evening peak

On Sun, Oct 18, 2026 at 10:03 PM Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — forwarding an active report from Kaleido (high-value account). Quick context: p99 tail for the SSE/WebSocket streaming chat spiked during their 19:15-19:45 PDT peak.

Thanks — I've pulled the runtime metrics and traces. Preliminary notes:
- There was a brief backpressure cascade on the shared streaming pool at 19:22. A small % of long-running streams held KV caches and caused scheduling to pile up; this increased the head-of-line blocking for short bursts.
- Autoscale reacted but latency for new allocations is ~45s; during that window p99 spikes.
- No obvious code regression in the last two weeks, but we did push a kernel schedule tweak three days ago that changes affinity heuristics. Worth rolling that back if we see correlation.

Immediate mitigations I can enact (non-disruptive):
1) Route Kaleido traffic to a spare Dedicated pool we keep for emergencies (will reduce contention).
2) Enable per-tenant stream eviction: evict idle KV caches after 30s instead of 300s.
3) Apply temporary conservative batching (smaller batch sizes) for streaming chat to reduce head-of-line variance.

I'll attach traces to PD-78921 and push short-lived routing rule now. ETA 12 minutes for rule to propagate.

— Dev
Senior Software Engineer, Serving Runtime

To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Fwd: Live chat - severe lag / reconnects during evening peak

> Immediate mitigations I can enact (non-disruptive): Route Kaleido traffic to a spare Dedicated pool ...

Update: routed Kaleido to dedicated pool (pool-id ded-rescue-02) at 23:03 PDT. Observed immediate fall in p99 from ~10s to ~600ms for their traffic; global shared pool still shows elevated p99 for a short period while backlog drains.

We also enabled eviction and conservative batching. Error-rate returned to baseline. Attaching a short before/after trace and system snapshot.

Next steps / ask:
- Ops to run a controlled exercise tomorrow to validate autoscale warmup times and ensure we have hot spares for accounts in the 95th percentile of concurrency.
- Product to consider a tenant isolation option that is self-service for customers wanting guaranteed latency at scale (billing implications noted).
- Mateo / Marissa: please confirm whether Kaleido wants a post-incident write-up and whether they need SLA credit or a retro call.

— Soojin

title: Streaming chat: sudden p99 tail spikes under peak load — customer escalation
preview:
From: Jonah Keane <jonah@kaleidohealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>, support@redwood.com
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 21:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Live chat - severe lag / reconnects during evening peak

Hi Mateo / Redwood support,

We started seeing large numbers of reconnects and 5xx responses on our live chat between 19:15-19:45 PDT tonight. Our product team measured p95 at ~1.2s (ok) but p99 shooting up to 8-12s on the streaming endpoint (SSE). That correlates with users reporting long stalls and partial messages. Throughput (messages/min) was normal but tail latency and a small spike in error-rate (0.9%) impacted session continuity.

We rely on the streaming endpoint for our 1:1 clinician chat; customers were frustrated. Can you triage? We need visibility and a mitigation ASAP — this is impacting our retention metrics.

Attaching a short server-side trace and player logs.

— Jonah
Kaleido Health


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo_alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.com>

Mateo
Account Exec — Redwood Inference

source.subject: Getting there & gatekeeping — presenter onboarding and pre-read curation
messages:
From: Irene Choi <irene.ch@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Kumar <sanjay.kumar@veridianhealth.com>, Emily Zhao <ezhao@veridianhealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Getting there & gatekeeping — presenter onboarding and pre-read curation

Hi Sanjay and Emily,

Thanks again for locking the week of Nov 16 for the Veridian leadership workshop. I wanted to kick off logistics and make sure we align on two things today: (1) travel windows for your execs + any arrival constraints (early check-ins / security escorts), and (2) who will require backstage access for the prep day (we need a definitive gate list for the venue).

Quick asks: can you share ETA windows for the five Veridian attendees and confirm whether any attendees will need an on-site escort to the greenroom? If someone has special dietary needs, include that too so we can feed the presenters properly during rehearsals.

Attached is a light pre-read digest (pre-read-digest-v1.pdf) that Kimberly and I drafted — audience: SVP+ and their direct reports. We plan to iterate it once we have final presenter names and any company-facing slides from your side.

Irene
Irene Choi
Events & Customer Ops | Redwood Inference
cell: 415-555-0134

Attachment: pre-read-digest-v1.pdf (summary, 6 pages)
From: Emily Zhao <ezhao@veridianhealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.ch@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sanjay Kumar <sanjay.kumar@veridianhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Oct 2026 11:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Getting there & gatekeeping — presenter onboarding and pre-read curation

Thanks, Irene — quick answers below.

- Travel windows: All five execs arrive midday on Nov 15 (ETD SFO 08:00 PST), depart Nov 17 evening. One exec (Meera Patel) will arrive a day earlier for prep (Nov 14 evening).
- Escort: Meera and our CIO (Rajesh Menon) will need an escort from the hotel to the venue on Nov 15 for the run-through.
- Dietary: Meera — vegetarian (no eggs); Rajesh — pescatarian; rest are standard.

We’ll attach copies of the slide decks by COB Oct 18. Sanjay and I prefer the pre-read be no longer than 8 pages for execs — your digest looks good at a glance.

Emily

On Mon, Oct 12, 2026 at 9:14 AM Irene Choi <irene.ch@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Sanjay and Emily,
> 
> Thanks again for locking the week of Nov 16 for the Veridian leadership workshop...
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Zhao <ezhao@veridianhealth.com>, Sanjay Kumar <sanjay.kumar@veridianhealth.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.ch@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 16 Oct 2026 08:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Getting there & gatekeeping — presenter onboarding and pre-read curation

Hi Emily / Sanjay — looping in Mateo and Lauren for presenter ops + travel lanes. A couple of internal notes and a proposed presenter slate:

Proposed presenter owners (subject to your sign-off):
- Strategy overview & roadmap: Rajesh Menon (CIO, Veridian) — 25 mins (Irene/Beth will own stage time)
- Platform economics & latency story: Meera Patel — 20 mins (tech demo to follow)
- Customer stories / outcomes: Sanjay to lead a 15-min panel with two internal PMs — we will prep Qs

On the gatekeeping/escort piece: Mateo will be point for hotel pickup and greenroom escorts on Nov 15. Lauren will own the pre-read packaging and final deck drop (we will create a single PDF assembly for execs).

Pre-read curation plan:
- Lauren will produce an 8-page executive version (pre-read-digest-v2.pdf) by COB Oct 18.
- We will include one slide per presenter (3–4 bullets + 1 metric) and a one-page agenda.
- Action: Emily — please send the final speaker slide(s) by end of day Oct 18 (pptx or PDF). If you need a template, use the 'Exec Slide' in the shared Drive.

Attachment: travel-lanes.xlsx (draft pickup windows)

Kimberly Park
Senior Program Manager, Events | Redwood Inference

On Wed, Oct 14, 2026 at 11:22 AM Emily Zhao <ezhao@veridianhealth.com> wrote:
> Thanks, Irene — quick answers below.
From: Irene Choi <irene.ch@redwood.ai>
To: Emily Zhao <ezhao@veridianhealth.com>
Cc: Sanjay Kumar <sanjay.kumar@veridianhealth.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 16:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Getting there & gatekeeping — presenter onboarding and pre-read curation

Thanks all — final sync and what’s next.

Summary / confirmations:
- Travel: confirmed arrivals Nov 14 (Meera), Nov 15 midday (others). Mateo has the vendor pickup schedule and will share exact car ETAs (see travel-lanes.xlsx).
- Gatekeeping: greenroom escort confirmed for Meera and Rajesh on Nov 15 (Mateo to meet at hotel lobby).
- Presenters: Rajesh (strategy), Meera (platform), Sanjay (customer panel) — Lauren to consolidate slides and produce pre-read v2.
- Pre-read: Lauren to publish pre-read-digest-v2.pdf and drop into the Drive folder by Oct 18 6pm PT. Irene will send an executive-looking one-page agenda to Veridian the same evening.

Links and attachments:
- Veridian workshop folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2cDEfGHijK - Veridian Workshop Folder
- Draft travel lanes: travel-lanes.xlsx (attached)
- Pre-read (final version coming Oct 18): pre-read-digest-v2.pdf (placeholder)

Next steps (owners):
- Lauren: finalize pre-read and confirm slide-by-slide owners by Oct 18 EOD.
- Mateo: confirm carriage ETAs and venue contact (David H.) by Oct 25.
- Emily / Sanjay: deliver any last-minute speaker notes or sensitive context to Irene privately (irene.ch@redwood.ai).

Irene Choi
Events & Customer Ops | Redwood Inference
phone: 415-555-0134

Attachment: travel-lanes.xlsx

source.subject: Private upgrade: safety windows, pinning guidance, and comms lane
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@finterra-payments.com>
Cc: platform-ops@finterra-payments.com
Date: 2026-10-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Private upgrade: safety windows, pinning guidance, and comms lane

Team / Olivia —

We need to lock in a short, low-risk maintenance window for the 2026-10 private runtime upgrade for FinTerra. This thread is to capture the safety plan (pin behavior, network drain, rollback criteria) and the communication lane for the day-of ops bridge. High-level asks: 

1) Confirm a 2-hour window where FinTerra can accept a brief throughput drop (target: 10–15% window).
2) Agree pinning policy for customer-managed pinned versions and an escape hatch timeline.
3) Validate network-drain procedure and whether we need an additional VIP swap.
4) Identify who will own the rollback decision and the timebox for rollback (recommended 30 minutes after a critical regression).

Attached: upgrade-checklist.xlsx (runbook), network-drain-playbook.pdf, a small config diff for the new KV cache change (config-diff.patch).

I suggest the following preliminary window options (US Pacific times):
- Option A: 2026-10-24 02:00–04:00 PT (low traffic, pre-billing spike)
- Option B: 2026-10-24 22:00–00:00 PT (end-of-day for FinTerra)

Soojin — can you confirm whether the KV cache change requires a pre-warm step or extra draining?
Naomi — can you coordinate comms with FinTerra SRE and confirm which window they prefer?
Karthik — once we have a window, can you prepare the canary + instant-unpin playbook and the decision matrix for rollback?

Thanks — Mateo

--
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Product Manager, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@finterra-payments.com>
Date: 2026-10-17T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Private upgrade: safety windows, pinning guidance, and comms lane

Thanks Mateo — quick technical summary: 

- KV cache change: no mandatory pre-warm is required, but we strongly recommend preloading KV for the two heaviest models used by FinTerra (seq len ~1024). Pre-warm can be a short synthetic run (10 concurrent sessions) 30 minutes prior to cutover.
- Drain behavior: we must drain active token-generating sessions gracefully. The attached network-drain-playbook covers TCP drain + L7 weight shift. We do NOT need a VIP swap unless FinTerra wants zero-IP-change; a weight-based drain is sufficient and safer for their multi-AZ setup.
- Pinning: our policy for pinned customers remains: pinned_version is honored for 72 hours post-upgrade by default. We can override with an immediate unpin (admin action) if the rollback decision is taken.

Rollback triggers I recommend adding to the decision matrix: 
- Severity A: producer-side errors >1% of requests AND latency 2x baseline for >10 minutes -> immediate rollback.
- Severity B: increased 5xx rate but acceptable latency -> continue canary, escalate at 30 minutes.
- Config mismatch (KV / quantization) -> immediate shelve and route to fallback model variant.

I'll add a small preflight script to the checklist that verifies KV sizes, quant tables, and worker kernel versions.

Soojin
Lead Infrastructure Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@finterra-payments.com>
Date: 2026-10-17T12:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Private upgrade: safety windows, pinning guidance, and comms lane

Olivia / FinTerra ops —

We're aiming for Option A (2026-10-24 02:00–04:00 PT) but if you prefer Option B we can accommodate. Please confirm which window you want by EOD 2026-10-19 so we can lock resources.

Day-of comms plan (brief):
- 01:30 PT: Preflight checks complete and shared (runbook pass/fail).
- 01:45 PT: Bridge open (Redwood + FinTerra SREs).
- 02:00 PT: Canary slice (5% traffic) starts; 02:15 PT: expand to 25% if green; 02:45 PT: full ramp to 100% if green.
- Rollback timebox: if Severity A is hit at any point, rollback start within 30 minutes and restore pinned version.

Olivia — can you provide the FinTerra SRE on-call contact and confirm measurement dashboards (we'll need Grafana and token-level latency panels ready)?

I'll prepare the short customer-facing comms and a one-page cutover decision tree for the bridge.

Cheers,
Naomi
Technical Program Manager, Private Ops
Redwood Inference
From: Olivia Grant <olivia.grant@finterra-payments.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: platform-ops@finterra-payments.com
Date: 2026-10-17T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Private upgrade: safety windows, pinning guidance, and comms lane

Thanks all — FinTerra prefers Option A (02:00–04:00 PT on 10/24). Confirmed on-call: 
- SRE primary: Ethan Ruiz <ethan.ruiz@finterra-payments.com> (phone: +1-415-555-0172)
- SRE backup: Priya Shah <priya.shah@finterra-payments.com>

Two additional notes: 
1) We request a 15-minute readout within 1 hour post-cutover highlighting token-cost variance and 95th pct latency for the two heavy models.
2) If a rollback is initiated, FinTerra wants metrics exported (CSV) for the 30-minute window leading up to the rollback for postmortem. Is that feasible?

We confirm we can accept a short 10–15% throughput dip during canary. Please send final runbook and link to bridge by 10/19 EOD.

Olivia
Head of Platform Engineering
FinTerra Payments

source.subject: AWS co-sell: ACE registration + PDM/PSM intros — Nebula Health opportunity
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@aws-partners.com>, Avery Cloud <avery@nebulahealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 11 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: AWS co-sell: ACE registration + PDM/PSM intros — Nebula Health opportunity

Hi Jordan / Avery,

Thanks for taking the intro earlier. Nebula Health is moving quickly on a pilot for outpatient summarization (HIPAA scope) and asked us to start the ACE registration + co-sell path so we can get PDM/PSM pairing and the Marketplace listing alignment on the calendar. Quick context: Nebula expects a pilot procurement decision by end of Nov and wants AWS involvement for procurement facilitation and potential joint funding.

Can you confirm the PDM/PSM owners and whether we should submit the ACE intake form or a full listing application as the first step? I've attached a short needs assessment and a draft mutual action plan to accelerate gating items.

Requested next steps:
- AWS: PDM/PSM names and availability for a 30m intro this week
- Nebula (Avery): internal procurement milestone date + list of security artifacts they need for evaluation
- Redwood: we will complete ACE intake and surface listing artifacts (SOC, architecture, unit cost estimates)

Appreciate the quick response — aiming to lock the kickoff before EOW.

Thanks,
Mateo
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123
[Attachment: 20261011_Nebula_Needs_Assessment.pdf (PDF, 72KB)]
[Attachment: 20261011_Mutual_Action_Plan.xlsx (spreadsheet, 185KB)]

From: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@aws-partners.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.ai>, Avery Cloud <avery@nebulahealth.com>

source.subject: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs
messages:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for taking time yesterday. Attached is an initial proposal outlining three phased reservation options and the warm-start/kickoff schedule we discussed. High level summary below — I focused on ensuring predictable minimum capacity for your interactive inference workloads while giving you burst and warm-start paths for model fine-tuning & batch jobs.

Quick summary (details in attachment):
- Pilot (4 weeks): 8 GPUs (A100-equivalent), isolated lane, priority during PST business hours for validation and perf tuning.
- Phase 1 (month 1–3): 24 GPUs steady-state minimum; allows 2x concurrent development + inference lanes with headroom for bursts.
- Phase 2 (month 4–6): 64 GPUs steady state with configurable burst to 96 for peak events; includes autoscaling policy and dedicated KV-cache warm pool.
- Phase 3 (month 7+): 128 GPUs steady-state (or customer-triggered scale) for production traffic with SLA-backed latency SLAs and optional hot-standby across region.

Other notes:
- Warm-start credits: we recommend a 72-hour warmpool window prior to go-live events (included in the proposal) to pre-populate KV caches and reduce tail latency.
- Decommit clause: 30-day rolling decommit for staged phases, with a 60-day notice for Phase 3 decommits.
- Costs: the attachment includes an ARR estimate and a burn cadence per phase; we can shift to a blended commitment if you prefer smoother monthly burn.

Next steps I propose:
1) You and procurement review the attached workbook and mark preferred ramp.
2) We run a 2-week pilot (8 GPUs) to validate token-cost curves and inference p95.
3) Once pilot metrics look good we sign a Phase 1 commitment and schedule a warm-start window.

Attachment: lumenix-reserve-scenarios-v1.xlsx (reserve sizing workbook)

Best,
Mateo
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
http://redwoodinference.com

From: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lumenix Procurement <procure@lumenixlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T13:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Thanks Mateo — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifying Qs before we loop procurement in for numbers: 

1) For Phase 1 steady state: is the 24 GPU minimum per region or global? Our critical latency traffic is US-East and APAC during overlapping business hours.
2) For warm-start credits, can warm pools be targeted to model families (e.g., embedding models only) or is it per-cluster? We want to keep embedding lanes hot but not necessarily the full generative models warm.
3) If we hit unexpected usage spikes, what’s the typical lead time for burst capacity beyond the quoted burst max?

Can you also add a simple table showing expected p95 token latency at each phase (pilot, phase1, phase2) for the two model families (embedding vs generation)? Procurement wants numbers to plug into their TCO spreadsheet.

—Avery
Head of ML Ops | Lumenix Labs
avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Lumenix Procurement <procure@lumenixlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Avery — great questions, answered inline below and I attached a short MAP draft as well (MAP-draft-lumenix.docx).

1) Phase 1 minimum: that 24 GPU baseline I listed is regional — so we’d recommend 24 GPUs in US-East and a mirrored 24 GPU lane in APAC if you need symmetric latency. If you prefer asymmetric allocation we can do 32/16 split (US/APAC) depending on your traffic profile.

2) Warm pools: warm pools are configurable by model family. We can target embedding-only warm lanes (smaller memory footprint) and a separate warm pool for generation models. The warmpool size counts against reserved capacity but can be configured to be fractional (e.g., 2 GPUs pinned to embedding warmpool).

3) Burst lead time: for burst within committed lanes we provide instant autoscaling up to the burst cap (minutes). For excess capacity beyond burst cap we rely on our geo-burst routing, typical lead time to activate additional shared capacity is 4–8 hours depending on availability; we can provide a formal SLA for managed bursts as an add-on.

Quick p95 guidance (estimated; will validate in pilot):
- Pilot (8 GPUs): embedding p95 ~ 12ms per vector request, generation p95 (short prompts) ~ 180–220ms.
- Phase 1 (24 GPUs regional): embedding p95 ~ 8–10ms, generation p95 ~ 120–160ms.
- Phase 2 (64 GPUs + burst): embedding p95 ~ 6–8ms, generation p95 ~ 90–120ms.

Notes on sizing rationale: embedding workloads are more memory-bound and benefit from pinned warm lanes; generative workloads are compute-bound and benefit from KV cache warmups and larger batch windows for throughput. The attached workbook includes a per-hour burn simulation and an alternate blended-commitment pricing that smooths ARR over months 1–6.

Attachments: MAP-draft-lumenix.docx, lumenix-reserve-scenarios-v1.xlsx

If that looks OK I can have Kimberly put together a clean, procurement-ready one-pager and a redlineable term sheet. Sean, can you confirm whether the managed-burst SLA add-on is possible to include for a proof-of-concept?

Best,
Mateo

On 2026-10-11 13:42, Avery Collins wrote:
> Thanks Mateo — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifying Qs before we loop procurement in for numbers: 
> 
> 1) For Phase 1 steady state: is the 24 GPU minimum per region or global? Our critical latency traffic is US-East and APAC during overlapping business hours.
> 2) For warm-start credits, can warm pools be targeted to model families (e.g., embedding models only) or is it per-cluster? We want to keep embedding lanes hot but not necessarily the full generative models warm.
> 3) If we hit unexpected usage spikes, what’s the typical lead time for burst capacity beyond the quoted burst max?
> 
> Can you also add a simple table showing expected p95 token latency at each phase (pilot, phase1, phase2) for the two model families (embedding vs generation)? Procurement wants numbers to plug into their TCO spreadsheet.
> 
> —Avery

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>, Lumenix Procurement <procure@lumenixlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-13T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Mateo / Kimberly — I reviewed the MAP draft and the sizing workbook. We can include a managed-burst SLA add-on for the pilot and Phase 1 with the following constraints: 1) max managed burst to shared pool = 40% of Phase 1 steady state, 2) activation window for guaranteed managed burst = 6 hours, 3) additional fee applies. I asked Ops to prepare a quick runbook for activation and failover (linked below).

Kimberly — please produce the procurement one-pager with two pricing options: (A) upfront blended commitment (smoother burn) and (B) stepped phase commitments (cheaper at scale). Mateo — I will review estimated ARR numbers and sign off for inclusion in the redlineable term sheet.

Runbook link: https://redwood.ops/runbooks/lumenix-managed-burst

—Sean
Director, Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com

On 2026-10-12 10:05, Mateo Alvarez wrote:
> Avery — great questions, answered inline below and I attached a short MAP draft as well (MAP-draft-lumenix.docx).
> 
> 1) Phase 1 minimum: that 24 GPU baseline I listed is regional — so we’d recommend 24 GPUs in US-East and a mirrored 24 GPU lane in APAC if you need symmetric latency. If you prefer asymmetric allocation we can do 32/16 split (US/APAC) depending on your traffic profile.
> 
> 2) Warm pools: warm pools are configurable by model family. We can target embedding-only warm lanes (smaller memory footprint) and a separate warm pool for generation models. The warmpool size counts against reserved capacity but can be configured to be fractional (e.g., 2 GPUs pinned to embedding warmpool).
> 
> 3) Burst lead time: for burst within committed lanes we provide instant autoscaling up to the burst cap (minutes). For excess capacity beyond burst cap we rely on our geo-burst routing, typical lead time to activate additional shared capacity is 4–8 hours depending on availability; we can provide a formal SLA for managed bursts as an add-on.
> 
> Quick p95 guidance (estimated; will validate in pilot):
> - Pilot (8 GPUs): embedding p95 ~ 12ms per vector request, generation p95 (short prompts) ~ 180–220ms.

title: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for taking time yesterday. Attached is an initial proposal outlining three phased reservation options and the warm-start/kickoff schedule we discussed. High level summary below — I focused on ensuring predictable minimum capacity for your interactive inference workloads while giving you burst and warm-start paths for model fine-tuning & batch jobs.

Quick summary (details in attachment):
- Pilot (4 weeks): 8 GPUs (A100-equivalent), isolated lane, priority during PST business hours for validation and perf tuning.
- Phase 1 (month 1–3): 24 GPUs steady-state minimum; allows 2x concurrent development + inference lanes with headroom for bursts.
- Phase 2 (month 4–6): 64 GPUs steady state with configurable burst to 96 for peak events; includes autoscaling policy and dedicated KV-cache warm pool.
- Phase 3 (month 7+): 128 GPUs steady-state (or customer-triggered scale) for production traffic with SLA-backed latency SLAs and optional hot-standby across region.

Other notes:
- Warm-start credits: we recommend a 72-hour warmpool window prior to go-live events (included in the proposal) to pre-populate KV caches and reduce tail latency.
- Decommit clause: 30-day rolling decommit for staged phases, with a 60-day notice for Phase 3 decommits.
- Costs: the attachment includes an ARR estimate and a burn cadence per phase; we can shift to a blended commitment if you prefer smoother monthly burn.

Next steps I propose:
1) You and procurement review the attached workbook and mark preferred ramp.
2) We run a 2-week pilot (8 GPUs) to validate token-cost curves and inference p95.
3) Once pilot metrics look good we sign a Phase 1 commitment and schedule a warm-start window.

Attachment: lumenix-reserve-scenarios-v1.xlsx (reserve sizing workbook)

Best,
Mateo
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
http://redwoodinference.com



From: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lumenix Procurement <procure@lumenixlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T13:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Thanks Mateo — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifying Qs before we loop procurement in for numbers: 

1) For Phase 1 steady state: is the 24 GPU minimum per region or global? Our critical latency traffic is US-East and APAC during overlapping business hours.
2) For warm-start credits, can warm pools be targeted to model families (e.g., embedding models only) or is it per-cluster? We want to keep embedding lanes hot but not necessarily the full generative models warm.
3) If we hit unexpected usage spikes, what’s the typical lead time for burst capacity beyond the quoted burst max?

Can you also add a simple table showing expected p95 token latency at each phase (pilot, phase1, phase2) for the two model families (embedding vs generation)? Procurement wants numbers to plug into their TCO spreadsheet.

—Avery
Head of ML Ops | Lumenix Labs
avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com



From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Avery Collins <avery.collins@lumenixlabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Lumenix Procurement <procure@lumenixlabs.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Phase-gated reservation + warm-start timeline for Lumenix Labs

Avery — great questions, answered inline below and I attached a short MAP draft as well (MAP-draft-lumenix.docx).

1) Phase 1 minimum: that 24 GPU baseline I listed is regional — so we’d recommend 24 GPUs in US-East and a mirrored 24 GPU lane in APAC if you need symmetric latency. If you prefer asymmetric allocation we can do 32/16 split (US/APAC) depending on your traffic profile.

2) Warm pools: warm pools are configurable by model family. We can target embedding-only warm lanes (smaller memory footprint) and a separate warm pool for generation models. The warmpool size counts against reserved capacity but can be configured to be fractional (e.g., 2 GPUs pinned to embedding warmpool).

3) Burst lead time: for burst within committed lanes we provide instant autoscaling up to the burst cap (minutes). For excess capacity beyond burst cap we rely on our geo-burst routing, typical lead time to activate additional shared capacity is 4–8 hours depending on availability; we can provide a formal SLA for managed bursts as an add-on.

Quick p95 guidance (estimated; will validate in pilot):
- Pilot (8 GPUs): embedding p95 ~ 12ms per vector request, generation p95 (short prompts) ~ 180–220ms.
- Phase 1 (24 GPUs regional): embedding p95 ~ 8–10ms, generation p95 ~ 120–160ms.
- Phase 2 (64 GPUs + burst): embedding p95 ~ 6–8ms, generation p95 ~ 90–120ms.

Notes on sizing rationale: embedding workloads are more memory-bound and benefit from pinned warm lanes; generative workloads are compute-bound and benefit from KV cache warmups and larger batch windows for throughput. The attached workbook includes a per-hour burn simulation and an alternate blended-commitment pricing that smooths ARR over months 1–6.

Attachments: MAP-draft-lumenix.docx, lumenix-reserve-scenarios-v1.xlsx

If that looks OK I can have Kimberly put together a clean, procurement-ready one-pager and a redlineable term sheet. Sean, can you confirm whether the managed-burst SLA add-on is possible to include for a proof-of-concept?

Best,
Mateo

source.subject: Request: Campus credits for AI research course - self-serve conversion
messages:
From: Dr. Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@lakeside.edu>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-10T09:14:00-04:00
Subject: Request: Campus credits for AI research course

Hi Mateo,

I hope youre well. I run the undergraduate research lab at Lakeside Community College and were running an AI-focused semester project for 60 students (two sections). Several student teams created accounts via Redwoods PLG signup and are already experimenting.

We have a small departmental fund to cover infrastructure and would like to request an academic credit allocation (self-serve if possible) that can be distributed to student email domains (@lakeside.edu). Ideal: a single pool of credits we can apportion, valid for the semester (6 months).

Attached: course_syllabus.pdf (short) that outlines expected usage (mainly generation + embeddings for evaluation).

Questions:
- Is there a straightforward self-serve path to convert PLG signups into an academic credit pool?
- If not, what information do you need from us to approve and issue credits?

Were hoping to have this resolved before the lab starts on Oct 20. Happy to hop on a 20-min call if easier.

Thanks,
Dr. Sarah Kim
Director, Undergraduate Research Lab
Lakeside Community College

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Dr. Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@lakeside.edu>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-10T11:02:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Request: Campus credits for AI research course

Hi Sarah 
Thanks for the note and for sharing the syllabus  this is exactly the kind of program we love supporting. We do have a self-serve academic credit flow for small courses, but there are a couple of policy/verification steps well need to complete.

Quick ask to help me triage: can you confirm 1) the primary contact email domain to bulk-assign (sounds like @lakeside.edu), 2) an estimated token/month or dollar amount you'd like for the pool, and 3) whether any student data will be uploaded to private training datasets (none preferred).

Ill loop in Monica (CS) to run the approval and Dev (acct exec) for any contractual or billing notes. I can expedite if youre on a timeline. Ill attach a short credit_request_form to capture the requested fields.

Attachments: credit_request_form.docx (form)

Best,
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions - Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Campus credits for AI research course

On Oct 10, 2026, Mateo Alvarez wrote:
> Hi Sarah 
> Thanks for the note and for sharing the syllabus ...

Mateo / Dev,

I reviewed the syllabus and the ask. Policy notes: our standard academic package for semester courses is a time-limited pool (3-6 months) with a host account owner and domain verification. For classes under 100 students we usually approve a starter pool and allow a single top-up during the term if usage spikes.

Suggested approval: 6-month pool, $1,200 equivalent (approx), domain-limited to @lakeside.edu, no training-data opt-in required. That should cover generation and embeddings at moderate usage levels.

Steps I need from Sarah: completed credit_request_form (fields: contact, billing admin, student headcount, expected peak per day) and a short confirmation that student accounts will not opt into private training. Once I have that, I can generate the pool via self-serve and provide the HubSpot record and a receipt.

Im attaching the latest academic policy for reference.

Attachments: academic_policy_v2.pdf (policy snapshot)

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
To: Dr. Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@lakeside.edu>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T16:38:00-07:00
Subject: Credits issued: Lakeside Community College - AI research lab (self-serve pool)

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for the quick turnaround on the credit_request_form and confirmation (received earlier today). Monica has approved a 6-month academic pool and Ive completed the issuance. Details below and attached receipt.

- Pool size: $1,200 credits (redeemable by @lakeside.edu accounts)
- Validity: 2026-10-12 through 2027-04-12
- Restrictions: domain-bound to @lakeside.edu, single top-up allowed during the term; no training-data opt-in enforced

Follow these next steps to onboard students (self-serve):
1) Share the domain whitelist instructions in our docs: https://redwood.app/docs/academic-credits
2) Students create PLG accounts and request pool access via the admin email you provided; Monica will confirm assignments.
3) For any unforeseen spikes, reply to this thread or open a support ticket and we can provision a short-term burst.

HubSpot record: https://hubspot.redwood.example/deal/HS-45821
DocuSign (MOU snapshot): https://na1.docusign.net/sign/ABC123XYZ

Attachments: credits_receipt_20261012.csv (CSV of issuance)

If youd like a 20-min onboarding for TAs before the term, were happy to schedule next week — Monica can take that.

Best regards,
Mateo Alvarez
Solutions - Redwood Inference
mateo.alvarez@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

---
Redwood Inference | Production-grade LLM inference for engineering teams

Team,

The current slate (attached) assigns two heavy-load interviewers at the same times, which pushes others over quota and risks late cancellations.

Ask: can we rebalance the slate so that no interviewer takes more than two 45‑minute blocks for Sofia's loop AND identify a proxy/shadow who can step in at 4 hours notice if someone needs to swap?

Mateo

source.subject: Order Form invoicing & tax allocation — entity mapping and PO / billing cadence
messages:
From: Jordan Miles <j.miles@nymbushealth.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@nymbushealth.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions on invoice entity and tax allocation

Hi Mateo,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement asked me to confirm a few things before we share the PO:

1) Which Redwood entity will issue the invoices (US or Redwood Europe)?
2) Will your invoices include VAT by default for EU-resident entities, or do we need to self-assess?
3) Can you accommodate a two-tranche invoicing schedule tied to activation (50% at go-live / 50% after 90 days)?

We also have a standard request to include net-45 payment terms on the order form. Please let me know if that is possible or what exceptions might be needed.

Attached: nymbus_initial_requirements.xlsx (spreadsheet)

Best,
Jordan
Jordan Miles
Head of Procurement, Nymbus Health
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodai.com>
To: Jordan Miles <j.miles@nymbushealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Sep 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Order form questions on invoice entity and tax allocation

Jordan —

Appreciate the clear list. Quick answers below and then a short proposal for how we can map this in the order form so finance/legal can sign off.

1) Invoice entity: Redwood Inference, Inc. (US) will be the default invoicing entity for Nymbus Health (US HQ). If you require an EU entity invoice, we can route to Redwood Inference EU, Ltd., but that requires additional tax paperwork (W-8/Certificate of Residency equivalence) and a billing entity addendum.

2) VAT: For EU-registered customers we typically invoice net of VAT and rely on customer self-assessment when appropriate. If you provide a valid EU VAT ID and residency certificate, we can exclude VAT and annotate the invoice accordingly.

3) Two-tranche invoicing: We can support 50/50 split by milestone. We'll reflect the milestone schedule in the Order Form's payment table. Note that staging the invoice schedule may trigger a short-term credit hold if the total commitment is below our minimum committed amount — Kimberly / Ben can confirm operational impacts.

Net terms: Our standard hosted API terms are net-30. We can evaluate net-45 with a finance-approved credit review. Ben, can you confirm if Nymbus's profile (SLA/credit) allows net-45?

Proposed next step:
- Kimberly: can you review the two-tranche wording and any tax-evidence requirements we should request in the order form (tax cert, PO references, remittance instructions)?
- Jordan: if you can share the PO number and billing entity for your side, I will draft an invoice mapping table for inclusion in the order form annex.

I'll attach a draft mapping template.

Attachments: redwood_invoice_mapping_template.xlsx

Thanks,
Mateo
Mateo Alvarez
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>
To: Jordan Miles <j.miles@nymbushealth.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 14:58:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Order form questions on invoice entity and tax allocation

Hi Jordan / Mateo,

Looping in Finance for cash terms and raising the legal redlines we need to capture on the order form annex. High-level comments for inclusion in the next draft: 

• Invoice entity designation: explicit field in the annex that allows selection of Redwood Inference, Inc. (US) OR Redwood Inference EU, Ltd. (EU). If EU entity is selected, Customer must provide: local tax registration (VAT) certificate and signed W-8/W-9 equivalents as applicable.

• VAT treatment: default net of VAT. If customer provides valid VAT ID/registration evidence, add VAT exemption clause and specify reverse-charge mechanics where applicable.

• Payment schedule: accept 50/50 milestone split. Add language that later invoices will be issued upon completion of milestone and are subject to acceptance criteria defined in Annex B. Delayed acceptance does not change payment due date (i.e., invoice due XX days from invoice date).

• Net terms: We can evaluate net-45 for enterprise customers subject to credit review. If approved, add a finance clause that entitles Redwood to require a payment guarantee or prepayment buffer (e.g., first invoice in advance) if credit risk is material.

• Withholding taxes: Customer is responsible for any withholding taxes required by local law; Redwood will provide gross-up language or offset instructions where applicable with proper tax certificates submitted by customer.

• PO and remittance: require PO number on invoice metadata; remittance instructions and bank details to be added to Annex C. 

Please also attach your tax certificate (nymbus_tax_certificate.pdf) so Finance can confirm VAT/residency. If Jordan can provide the PO number this week we will produce a redlined Order Form (clean + markups) and circulate for signature.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Head of Contracts & Commercials | Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodai.com>
To: Jordan Miles <j.miles@nymbushealth.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodai.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwoodai.com>
Date: Thu, 07 Oct 2026 15:42:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Order form questions on invoice entity and tax allocation

Jordan —

Thanks for the details. A few operational notes from Finance: 

• Net-45: we can approve on a per-account basis after a credit check. For an initial production deployment we typically require either net-30 OR net-45 with first invoice paid in advance. If you prefer net-45 without prepayment, please expect a 5–7 business day review.

• Two-tranche invoicing: we will create two invoice numbers tied to the same order form. We ask that the PO references both invoice numbers and milestone IDs to prevent payment allocation issues.

• VAT/Withholding evidence: please send nymbus_tax_certificate.pdf and local VAT registration. If withholding is applied by your AP, we will need a completed withholding worksheet so we can gross-up or issue a credit where appropriate.

I've attached a completed invoice routing sheet with example remittance fields (nymbus_invoice_routing.xlsx). Once you send the PO number and tax cert we'll finalize the order form annex and issue the DocuSign envelope.

Attachments: nymbus_invoice_routing.xlsx

Best,
Ben Carter
Director, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
